```

---

## 2. Business Context

### 2.1 The Project

**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.

**Critical Context:**
- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- This demo must **recover client confidence**
- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The previous development approach failed because:
- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
- No research capability for latest technologies
- No persistent context between sessions
- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly

---

## 3. Technology Stack

### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |

**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### 3.2 Knowledge & Memory

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[GraphRAG (Microsoft)](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)** | Knowledge graph + RAG | Understands relationships, not just similarity |
| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |
| **[LlamaIndex](https://www.llamaindex.ai/)** | Document processing | Best-in-class RAG, agentic workflows |

**Architecture:**
```
Documents вҶ’ LlamaIndex вҶ’ GraphRAG вҶ’ Neo4j (relationships)
                                  вҶ’ Qdrant (embeddings)
```

### 3.3 Visual Testing & Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Playwright MCP](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-tools)** | Browser automation | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Claude Vision** | Screenshot analysis | Semantic understanding of UI |
| **[Applitools](https://applitools.com/)** | Visual AI testing | Enterprise-grade visual regression |
| **[Percy](https://percy.io/)** | Visual review | CI/CD integration |
| **[pixelmatch](https://github.com/mapbox/pixelmatch)** | Pixel comparison | Fast baseline comparison |

### 3.4 Performance Monitoring

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Lighthouse CI](https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci)** | Performance audits | Google's standard |
| **[Web Vitals](https://web.dev/vitals/)** | Core metrics | LCP, CLS, INP |
| **[Sentry](https://sentry.io/)** | Error tracking + RUM | Full observability |
| **[Grafana Faro](https://grafana.com/oss/faro/)** | Frontend observability | Real user monitoring |

### 3.5 Code Quality

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **TypeScript Strict** | Type safety | No `any`, full inference |
| **[ESLint](https://eslint.org/)** | Linting | Configurable, extensible |
| **[Vitest](https://vitest.dev/)** | Unit testing | Fast, Vite-native |
| **[Playwright](https://playwright.dev/)** | E2E testing | Best cross-browser support |
| **[madge](https://github.com/pahen/madge)** | Dependency analysis | Detect circular deps |

### 3.6 Copy & Marketing

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **Claude API** | Copywriting | Best reasoning, brand voice |
| **[Vale](https://vale.sh/)** | Prose linting | Style guide enforcement |
| **[LanguageTool](https://languagetool.org/)** | Grammar checking | Multi-language support |

### 3.7 Design Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Storybook](https://storybook.js.org/)** | Component docs | Visual component library |
| **[Chromatic](https://www.chromatic.com/)** | Visual testing | Storybook integration |
| **OKLCH Color Space** | Color system | Perceptually uniform |
| **[Contrast Ratio](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/)** | Accessibility | WCAG compliance |

---

## 4. The PHAROS Agents

### 4.1 Agent Overview

| Agent | Domain | Responsibility | Key Tools |
|-------|--------|----------------|-----------|
| **ORCHESTRATOR** | Coordination | Manages workflow, context, priorities | LangGraph, Tickets |
| **RESEARCHER** | Knowledge | Finds latest tech, reads docs, investigates | WebSearch, LlamaIndex |
| **ARCHITECT** | Structure | System design, dependencies, patterns | madge, ADRs |
| **ENGINEER** | Code | Implementation, tests, quality | TypeScript, Vitest |
| **DESIGNER** | Visual | UX/UI, layout, colors, hierarchy | Figma, Storybook |
| **COPYWRITER** | Text | Wording, clarity, tone, consistency | Vale, Claude |
| **MARKETING** | Positioning | Messaging, value props, demo narrative | Claude, templates |
| **PERFORMANCE** | Speed | Benchmarks, optimization, profiling | Lighthouse, DevTools |
| **VISION** | Verification | Visual testing, screenshot analysis | Playwright MCP, Claude Vision |
| **QA** | Validation | Integration, E2E, acceptance | Playwright, Vitest |

---

### 4.2 ORCHESTRATOR Agent

**Role:** The conductor of the orchestra. Maintains global context, assigns tasks, resolves conflicts.

**Responsibilities:**
- Read project state from `pharos/insights/`
- Assign tasks to appropriate agents
- Track ticket status and priorities
- Maintain session continuity
- Resolve inter-agent conflicts

**Context Maintained:**
```yaml
project:
  name: MMS Fractal Planner
  goal: Recover client confidence with impressive demo
  standards: [Palantir, Google, Pentagram]

current_state:
  branch: main (demo) | framework/quality-system (PHAROS)
  phase: current development phase
  blockers: list of blocking issues

environment:
  date: 2025-12-13
  os: macOS Darwin 25.1.0
```

---

### 4.3 RESEARCHER Agent

**Role:** The knowledge seeker. Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions.

**Responsibilities:**
- Research latest technologies and best practices
- Read and summarize documentation
- Find solutions to technical problems
- Update knowledge base with findings
- Validate that solutions are current (not outdated)

**Tools:**
- `WebSearch` вҖ” Find latest information
- `WebFetch` вҖ” Read documentation pages
- `LlamaIndex` вҖ” Process and index documents
- `GraphRAG` вҖ” Build knowledge relationships

**Output:**
```markdown
# Research: [Topic]

## Summary
[Key findings in 2-3 sentences]

## Recommendations
1. Use X because Y
2. Avoid Z because W

## Sources
- [Link 1](url) - accessed 2025-12-13
- [Link 2](url) - accessed 2025-12-13

## Caveats
- This may change by [date]
- Alternative: [option]
```

---

### 4.4 ARCHITECT Agent

**Role:** The structural engineer. Ensures system integrity and scalability.

**Responsibilities:**
- Design system architecture
- Review code structure and dependencies
- Enforce architectural constraints (no Ghost Divs, SoA, etc.)
- Create and maintain ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
- Detect and prevent architectural drift

**Checks:**
- [ ] No circular dependencies (`madge --circular`)
- [ ] Proper separation of concerns
- [ ] Data structures are optimal (SoA for large data)
- [ ] State management is correct (Zustand transient vs global)
- [ ] No forbidden patterns per PROJECT.md

**Tools:**
- `madge` вҖ” Dependency graph analysis
- AST analysis for pattern detection
- Custom architecture validators

---

### 4.5 ENGINEER Agent

**Role:** The implementer. Writes production-grade code.

**Responsibilities:**
- Implement features per specification
- Write comprehensive tests
- Follow coding standards strictly
- Handle errors properly
- Document complex logic

**Quality Gates:**
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (zero errors)
- [ ] `pnpm test --run` passes (all tests)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] JSDoc on exported interfaces
- [ ] No `console.log` in production code

**Standards:**
```typescript
// GOOD: Strict typing, clear naming
function calculateMileage(trainId: string, days: number): number {
  // ...
}

// BAD: Any type, unclear
function calc(x: any, y: any) {
  // ...
}
```

---

### 4.6 DESIGNER Agent

**Role:** The visual craftsman. Ensures Pentagram-level design quality.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and enforce visual hierarchy
- Ensure consistent color palette (OKLCH)
- Verify typography and spacing
- Apply cognitive psychology principles
- Ensure accessibility compliance

**Checks:**
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear (F-pattern, Z-pattern)
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
- [ ] Consistent spacing (8px grid)
- [ ] Typography scale is harmonious
- [ ] Interactive elements have feedback states
- [ ] Cognitive load is minimized (7Вұ2 rule)

**Principles:**
- **Pre-attentive processing:** Use color/size for instant recognition
- **Gestalt:** Group related elements, separate unrelated
- **Hick's Law:** Minimize choices at each step
- **Fitts's Law:** Important targets should be large and close

---

### 4.7 COPYWRITER Agent

**Role:** The wordsmith. Ensures professional, clear communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Review all user-facing text
- Ensure consistent terminology
- Maintain professional tone
- Check for typos and grammar
- Verify i18n-readiness

**Checks:**
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors
- [ ] Terminology is consistent throughout
- [ ] Tone is professional (not casual, not stuffy)
- [ ] Labels are clear and concise
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] No placeholder text ("Lorem ipsum", "TODO")
- [ ] No jargon without explanation

**Style Guide:**
```
вң“ "Save changes" (clear action)
вң— "Submit" (ambiguous)

вң“ "Unable to load schedule. Please try again."
вң— "Error 500"

вң“ "Maintenance" (consistent term)
вң— "Maintenance" / "Service" / "Repair" (inconsistent)
```

---

### 4.8 MARKETING Agent

**Role:** The storyteller. Ensures compelling positioning and messaging.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define value propositions
- Craft demo narrative
- Ensure messaging aligns with client needs
- Review all client-facing materials
- Position features for maximum impact

**Demo Narrative Structure:**
```markdown
## Opening Hook (30 seconds)
"Imagine seeing your entire 12-year maintenance plan in one view..."

## Problem Statement
"Currently, your team uses Excel and IBM Maximo separately..."

## Solution Demo
"With Fractal Planner, you can [demonstrate key feature]..."

## Proof Points
"Watch as the system automatically [impressive capability]..."

## Call to Action
"This is the 'Single Pane of Glass' you requested..."
```

**Key Messages:**
- "Google Maps of Time" вҖ” Intuitive navigation metaphor
- "Single Pane of Glass" вҖ” Client's own terminology
- "What-If Analysis" вҖ” RFQ requirement, now visual
- "Zero Latency" вҖ” Technical differentiator

---

### 4.9 PERFORMANCE Agent

**Role:** The speed demon. Ensures Google-scale performance.

**Responsibilities:**
- Benchmark all interactions
- Profile render performance
- Detect memory leaks
- Optimize bundle size
- Monitor Web Vitals

**Targets:**
| Metric | Target | Tool |
|--------|--------|------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) | Chrome DevTools |
| Initial load | <3s | Lighthouse |
| LCP | <2.5s | Web Vitals |
| INP | <200ms | Web Vitals |
| CLS | <0.1 | Web Vitals |
| Bundle size | <500KB gzipped | Vite analyzer |
| Memory | No GC pauses | Performance panel |

**Checks:**
- [ ] No frame drops during animation
- [ ] No main thread blocking >50ms
- [ ] Memory stable over time (no leaks)
- [ ] Bundle is code-split appropriately
- [ ] Assets are optimized (WebP, compression)

---

### 4.10 VISION Agent

**Role:** The eyes. Sees the application like a human.

**Responsibilities:**
- Capture screenshots of application state
- Analyze visual output semantically
- Verify expected elements are visible
- Detect visual regressions
- Provide visual evidence for tickets

**Tools:**
- **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser control
- **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis
- **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level comparison
- **Applitools** вҖ” AI visual testing (optional)

**Verification Flow:**
```
1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
2. Navigate to target URL
3. Set viewport/zoom as specified
4. Capture screenshot
5. Analyze with Vision LLM:
   - "Describe what you see"
   - "Are there colored bars visible?"
   - "Is the canvas blank or populated?"
6. Compare against visual criteria
7. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence
```

---

### 4.11 QA Agent

**Role:** The gatekeeper. Final validation before approval.

**Responsibilities:**
- Run full test suite
- Verify acceptance criteria
- Cross-check all agent approvals
- Make final APPROVED/REJECTED decision
- Document evidence

**Final Checklist:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All E2E tests pass
- [ ] ARCHITECT approved
- [ ] ENGINEER approved
- [ ] DESIGNER approved
- [ ] COPYWRITER approved
- [ ] MARKETING approved
- [ ] PERFORMANCE approved
- [ ] VISION approved

## 7. Integration Points

### 7.1 Enhanced init.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "================================================"
echo "  PHAROS Framework - Project Status Check"
echo "================================================"
echo ""
echo "Date: $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
echo "OS: $(uname -s) $(uname -r)"
echo "Node: $(node -v)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo ""

# Check for rejected tickets
REJECTED=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вқҢ CRITICAL: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) require attention"
  ls pharos/tickets/rejected/
  echo ""
fi

# Check for pending tickets
PENDING=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/pending/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вҸі PENDING: $PENDING ticket(s) awaiting review"
fi

# Check for in-review tickets
INREVIEW=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/in-review/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$INREVIEW" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "рҹ”Қ IN REVIEW: $INREVIEW ticket(s) being reviewed"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Quality Gates ==="
echo "Running: pnpm typecheck..."
pnpm typecheck || { echo "вқҢ TypeCheck FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… TypeCheck passed"

echo "Running: pnpm lint..."
pnpm lint || { echo "вқҢ Lint FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Lint passed"

echo "Running: pnpm test --run..."
pnpm test --run || { echo "вқҢ Tests FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Tests passed"

echo ""
echo "=== All Checks Passed ==="
echo "Ready to proceed with development."
```

## 8. Workflow Examples

### 8.1 Implementing a New Feature

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns task to RESEARCHER
2. RESEARCHER investigates best approach, updates knowledge base
3. ORCHESTRATOR assigns implementation to ENGINEER
4. ENGINEER implements feature, writes tests
5. ENGINEER creates ticket in pending/
6. All agents review in parallel:
   - ARCHITECT checks structure
   - DESIGNER checks visuals
   - COPYWRITER checks text (if any)
   - PERFORMANCE benchmarks
   - VISION captures and analyzes screenshots
7. QA makes final decision
8. If APPROVED вҶ’ merge
9. If REJECTED вҶ’ ENGINEER fixes, resubmits
```

### 8.2 Handling a Bug Report

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR creates investigation ticket
2. VISION captures current state (screenshot)
3. RESEARCHER investigates root cause
4. ARCHITECT determines if structural issue
5. ENGINEER implements fix
6. VISION verifies fix visually
7. QA validates all tests pass
8. APPROVED вҶ’ merge
```

---

# PHAROS Framework

> **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**
>
> *The Lighthouse of Excellence* вҖ” A complete multi-agent development and quality assurance framework that operates like a world-class engineering organization (Google + Palantir + Pentagram standards).

---

## 1. Executive Summary

### 1.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:

- **Researchers** investigate technologies and best practices
- **Architects** design systems and ensure structural integrity
- **Engineers** implement features with production-grade code
- **Designers** ensure Pentagram-level visual excellence
- **Copywriters** ensure professional, clear communication
- **Marketing** ensures proper positioning and messaging
- **Performance Engineers** ensure Google-scale speed
- **QA/Vision** verifies everything works as intended
- **Orchestrator** coordinates all agents and maintains context

### 1.2 The Problem We Solve

Current AI development has critical gaps:

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| AI marks features "done" without verification | Tests pass, screen is blue |
| No systematic quality gates | Inconsistent output quality |
| Context lost between sessions | Repeated mistakes |
| No specialized expertise | Jack of all trades, master of none |
| No research capability | Outdated solutions |
| No visual verification | Blind to actual output |

### 1.3 The PHAROS Solution

A multi-agent system where **specialized agents handle their domain**, communicate via **persistent tickets**, and **nothing ships without unanimous approval**.

## 2. Business Context

### 2.1 The Project

**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.

**Critical Context:**
- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- This demo must **recover client confidence**
- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The previous development approach failed because:
- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
- No research capability for latest technologies
- No persistent context between sessions
- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly

---

## 3. Technology Stack

### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |

**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### 3.2 Knowledge & Memory

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[GraphRAG (Microsoft)](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)** | Knowledge graph + RAG | Understands relationships, not just similarity |
| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |
| **[LlamaIndex](https://www.llamaindex.ai/)** | Document processing | Best-in-class RAG, agentic workflows |

**Architecture:**
```
Documents вҶ’ LlamaIndex вҶ’ GraphRAG вҶ’ Neo4j (relationships)
                                  вҶ’ Qdrant (embeddings)
```

### 3.3 Visual Testing & Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Playwright MCP](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-tools)** | Browser automation | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Claude Vision** | Screenshot analysis | Semantic understanding of UI |
| **[Applitools](https://applitools.com/)** | Visual AI testing | Enterprise-grade visual regression |
| **[Percy](https://percy.io/)** | Visual review | CI/CD integration |
| **[pixelmatch](https://github.com/mapbox/pixelmatch)** | Pixel comparison | Fast baseline comparison |

### 3.4 Performance Monitoring

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Lighthouse CI](https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci)** | Performance audits | Google's standard |
| **[Web Vitals](https://web.dev/vitals/)** | Core metrics | LCP, CLS, INP |
| **[Sentry](https://sentry.io/)** | Error tracking + RUM | Full observability |
| **[Grafana Faro](https://grafana.com/oss/faro/)** | Frontend observability | Real user monitoring |

### 3.5 Code Quality

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |

| **[ESLint](https://eslint.org/)** | Linting | Configurable, extensible |
| **[Vitest](https://vitest.dev/)** | Unit testing | Fast, Vite-native |

## 4. The PHAROS Agents

### 4.1 Agent Overview

| Agent | Domain | Responsibility | Key Tools |
|-------|--------|----------------|-----------|
| **ORCHESTRATOR** | Coordination | Manages workflow, context, priorities | LangGraph, Tickets |
| **RESEARCHER** | Knowledge | Finds latest tech, reads docs, investigates | WebSearch, LlamaIndex |
| **ARCHITECT** | Structure | System design, dependencies, patterns | madge, ADRs |
| **ENGINEER** | Code | Implementation, tests, quality | TypeScript, Vitest |
| **DESIGNER** | Visual | UX/UI, layout, colors, hierarchy | Figma, Storybook |
| **COPYWRITER** | Text | Wording, clarity, tone, consistency | Vale, Claude |
| **MARKETING** | Positioning | Messaging, value props, demo narrative | Claude, templates |

### 4.2 ORCHESTRATOR Agent

**Role:** The conductor of the orchestra. Maintains global context, assigns tasks, resolves conflicts.

**Responsibilities:**
- Read project state from `pharos/insights/`
- Assign tasks to appropriate agents
- Track ticket status and priorities
- Maintain session continuity
- Resolve inter-agent conflicts

**Context Maintained:**
```yaml
project:
  name: MMS Fractal Planner
  goal: Recover client confidence with impressive demo
  standards: [Palantir, Google, Pentagram]

current_state:
  branch: main (demo) | framework/quality-system (PHAROS)
  phase: current development phase
  blockers: list of blocking issues

environment:
  date: 2025-12-13
  os: macOS Darwin 25.1.0
```

---

### 4.3 RESEARCHER Agent

**Role:** The knowledge seeker. Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions.

**Responsibilities:**
- Research latest technologies and best practices
- Read and summarize documentation
- Find solutions to technical problems
- Update knowledge base with findings
- Validate that solutions are current (not outdated)

**Tools:**
- `WebSearch` вҖ” Find latest information
- `WebFetch` вҖ” Read documentation pages
- `LlamaIndex` вҖ” Process and index documents
- `GraphRAG` вҖ” Build knowledge relationships

**Output:**
```markdown
# Research: [Topic]

## Summary
[Key findings in 2-3 sentences]

## Recommendations
1. Use X because Y
2. Avoid Z because W

## Sources
- [Link 1](url) - accessed 2025-12-13
- [Link 2](url) - accessed 2025-12-13

## Caveats
- This may change by [date]
- Alternative: [option]
```

---

### 4.4 ARCHITECT Agent

**Role:** The structural engineer. Ensures system integrity and scalability.

**Responsibilities:**
- Design system architecture
- Review code structure and dependencies
- Enforce architectural constraints (no Ghost Divs, SoA, etc.)
- Create and maintain ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
- Detect and prevent architectural drift

**Checks:**
- [ ] No circular dependencies (`madge --circular`)
- [ ] Proper separation of concerns
- [ ] Data structures are optimal (SoA for large data)
- [ ] State management is correct (Zustand transient vs global)
- [ ] No forbidden patterns per PROJECT.md

**Tools:**
- `madge` вҖ” Dependency graph analysis
- AST analysis for pattern detection
- Custom architecture validators

---

### 4.5 ENGINEER Agent

**Role:** The implementer. Writes production-grade code.

**Responsibilities:**
- Implement features per specification
- Write comprehensive tests
- Follow coding standards strictly
- Handle errors properly
- Document complex logic

**Quality Gates:**
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (zero errors)
- [ ] `pnpm test --run` passes (all tests)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] JSDoc on exported interfaces
- [ ] No `console.log` in production code

**Standards:**
```typescript
// GOOD: Strict typing, clear naming
function calculateMileage(trainId: string, days: number): number {
  // ...
}

// BAD: Any type, unclear
function calc(x: any, y: any) {
  // ...
}
```

---

### 4.6 DESIGNER Agent

**Role:** The visual craftsman. Ensures Pentagram-level design quality.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and enforce visual hierarchy
- Ensure consistent color palette (OKLCH)
- Verify typography and spacing
- Apply cognitive psychology principles
- Ensure accessibility compliance

**Checks:**
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear (F-pattern, Z-pattern)
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
- [ ] Consistent spacing (8px grid)
- [ ] Typography scale is harmonious
- [ ] Interactive elements have feedback states
- [ ] Cognitive load is minimized (7Вұ2 rule)

**Principles:**
- **Pre-attentive processing:** Use color/size for instant recognition
- **Gestalt:** Group related elements, separate unrelated
- **Hick's Law:** Minimize choices at each step
- **Fitts's Law:** Important targets should be large and close

---

### 4.7 COPYWRITER Agent

**Role:** The wordsmith. Ensures professional, clear communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Review all user-facing text
- Ensure consistent terminology
- Maintain professional tone
- Check for typos and grammar
- Verify i18n-readiness

**Checks:**
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors
- [ ] Terminology is consistent throughout
- [ ] Tone is professional (not casual, not stuffy)
- [ ] Labels are clear and concise
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] No placeholder text ("Lorem ipsum", "TODO")
- [ ] No jargon without explanation

**Style Guide:**
```
вң“ "Save changes" (clear action)
вң— "Submit" (ambiguous)

вң“ "Unable to load schedule. Please try again."
вң— "Error 500"

вң“ "Maintenance" (consistent term)
вң— "Maintenance" / "Service" / "Repair" (inconsistent)
```

---

### 4.8 MARKETING Agent

**Role:** The storyteller. Ensures compelling positioning and messaging.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define value propositions
- Craft demo narrative
- Ensure messaging aligns with client needs
- Review all client-facing materials
- Position features for maximum impact

**Demo Narrative Structure:**
```markdown
## Opening Hook (30 seconds)
"Imagine seeing your entire 12-year maintenance plan in one view..."

## Problem Statement
"Currently, your team uses Excel and IBM Maximo separately..."

## Solution Demo
"With Fractal Planner, you can [demonstrate key feature]..."

## Proof Points
"Watch as the system automatically [impressive capability]..."

## Call to Action
"This is the 'Single Pane of Glass' you requested..."
```

**Key Messages:**
- "Google Maps of Time" вҖ” Intuitive navigation metaphor
- "Single Pane of Glass" вҖ” Client's own terminology
- "What-If Analysis" вҖ” RFQ requirement, now visual
- "Zero Latency" вҖ” Technical differentiator

---

### 4.9 PERFORMANCE Agent

**Role:** The speed demon. Ensures Google-scale performance.

**Responsibilities:**
- Benchmark all interactions
- Profile render performance
- Detect memory leaks
- Optimize bundle size
- Monitor Web Vitals

**Targets:**
| Metric | Target | Tool |
|--------|--------|------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) | Chrome DevTools |
| Initial load | <3s | Lighthouse |
| LCP | <2.5s | Web Vitals |
| INP | <200ms | Web Vitals |
| CLS | <0.1 | Web Vitals |
| Bundle size | <500KB gzipped | Vite analyzer |
| Memory | No GC pauses | Performance panel |

**Checks:**
- [ ] No frame drops during animation
- [ ] No main thread blocking >50ms
- [ ] Memory stable over time (no leaks)
- [ ] Bundle is code-split appropriately
- [ ] Assets are optimized (WebP, compression)

---

### 4.10 VISION Agent

**Role:** The eyes. Sees the application like a human.

**Responsibilities:**
- Capture screenshots of application state
- Analyze visual output semantically
- Verify expected elements are visible
- Detect visual regressions
- Provide visual evidence for tickets

**Tools:**
- **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser control
- **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis
- **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level comparison
- **Applitools** вҖ” AI visual testing (optional)

**Verification Flow:**
```
1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
2. Navigate to target URL
3. Set viewport/zoom as specified
4. Capture screenshot
5. Analyze with Vision LLM:
   - "Describe what you see"
   - "Are there colored bars visible?"
   - "Is the canvas blank or populated?"
6. Compare against visual criteria
7. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence
```

---

### 4.11 QA Agent

**Role:** The gatekeeper. Final validation before approval.

**Responsibilities:**
- Run full test suite
- Verify acceptance criteria
- Cross-check all agent approvals
- Make final APPROVED/REJECTED decision
- Document evidence

**Final Checklist:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All E2E tests pass
- [ ] ARCHITECT approved
- [ ] ENGINEER approved
- [ ] DESIGNER approved
- [ ] COPYWRITER approved
- [ ] MARKETING approved
- [ ] PERFORMANCE approved
- [ ] VISION approved

echo ""

# Check for rejected tickets
REJECTED=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вқҢ CRITICAL: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) require attention"
  ls pharos/tickets/rejected/
  echo ""
fi

# Check for pending tickets
PENDING=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/pending/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вҸі PENDING: $PENDING ticket(s) awaiting review"
fi

# Check for in-review tickets
INREVIEW=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/in-review/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$INREVIEW" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "рҹ”Қ IN REVIEW: $INREVIEW ticket(s) being reviewed"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Quality Gates ==="
echo "Running: pnpm typecheck..."
pnpm typecheck || { echo "вқҢ TypeCheck FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… TypeCheck passed"

echo "Running: pnpm lint..."
pnpm lint || { echo "вқҢ Lint FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Lint passed"

echo "Running: pnpm test --run..."
pnpm test --run || { echo "вқҢ Tests FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Tests passed"

echo ""
echo "=== All Checks Passed ==="
echo "Ready to proceed with development."
```

### 7.2 features.json Enhancement

```json
{
  "id": "render-003",
  "name": "LOD 1: Instanced Mesh for Bars",
  "phase": "rendering-layers",
  "status": "pending",
  "pharos": {
    "requiredGates": ["architect", "engineer", "designer", "performance", "vision"],
    "visualCriteria": {
      "minVisibleElements": 100,
      "expectedColors": ["#3ddc84", "#ff6b35", "#4ecdc4"],
      "canvasNotUniform": true
    },
    "performanceCriteria": {
      "maxFrameTime": 16,
      "target60fps": true
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 8. Workflow Examples

### 8.1 Implementing a New Feature

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns task to RESEARCHER
2. RESEARCHER investigates best approach, updates knowledge base
3. ORCHESTRATOR assigns implementation to ENGINEER
4. ENGINEER implements feature, writes tests
5. ENGINEER creates ticket in pending/
6. All agents review in parallel:
   - ARCHITECT checks structure
   - DESIGNER checks visuals
   - COPYWRITER checks text (if any)
   - PERFORMANCE benchmarks
   - VISION captures and analyzes screenshots
7. QA makes final decision
8. If APPROVED вҶ’ merge
9. If REJECTED вҶ’ ENGINEER fixes, resubmits
```

### 8.2 Handling a Bug Report

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR creates investigation ticket
2. VISION captures current state (screenshot)
3. RESEARCHER investigates root cause
4. ARCHITECT determines if structural issue
5. ENGINEER implements fix
6. VISION verifies fix visually
7. QA validates all tests pass
8. APPROVED вҶ’ merge
```

---

## 9. Success Metrics

PHAROS is successful when:

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| False positives | 0 | Features marked "done" that don't work |
| Visual bugs | 0 | Verified by VISION agent |
| Context retention | 100% | No repeated mistakes across sessions |
| Quality consistency | 100% | All output meets standards |
| Iteration speed | <2 cycles | Tickets approved within 2 review cycles |

---

## 10. Sources & References

### Multi-Agent Frameworks
- [Top 7 Frameworks for Building AI Agents in 2025](https://www.analyticsvidhya.com/blog/2024/07/ai-agent-frameworks/)
- [LangGraph vs AutoGen vs CrewAI Comparison](https://latenode.com/blog/platform-comparisons-alternatives/automation-platform-comparisons/langgraph-vs-autogen-vs-crewai-complete-ai-agent-framework-comparison-architecture-analysis-2025)
- [Comparing Open-Source AI Agent Frameworks - Langfuse](https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-03-19-ai-agent-comparison)

### Knowledge & RAG
- [Microsoft GraphRAG GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)
- [GraphRAG: Unlocking LLM Discovery](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/graphrag-unlocking-llm-discovery-on-narrative-private-data/)
- [LlamaIndex - Agentic Document Workflows](https://www.llamaindex.ai/blog/introducing-agentic-document-workflows)

### Vector Databases
- [Vector Database Comparison 2025](https://liquidmetal.ai/casesAndBlogs/vector-comparison/)
- [Best Vector Database For RAG 2025](https://digitaloneagency.com.au/best-vector-database-for-rag-in-2025-pinecone-vs-weaviate-vs-qdrant-vs-milvus-vs-chroma/)

### Visual Testing
- [AI-Powered Visual Testing in Playwright](https://testrig.medium.com/ai-powered-visual-testing-in-playwright-from-pixels-to-perception-dd3ee49911d5)
- [Playwright MCP Integration](https://kailash-pathak.medium.com/modern-test-automation-with-ai-llm-and-playwright-mcp-model-context-protocol-0c311292c7fb)
- [Using Vision LLMs For UI Testing](https://courses.cs.washington.edu/courses/cse503/25wi/final-reports/Using%20Vision%20LLMs%20For%20UI%20Testing.pdf)

### Performance Monitoring
- [Best Frontend Performance Monitoring Tools 2025](https://cssauthor.com/best-frontend-performance-monitoring-tools/)
- [Best Real User Monitoring Tools 2025](https://www.debugbear.com/software/best-real-user-monitoring-tools)

### MCP & Claude
- [Model Context Protocol Introduction](https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol)
- [Advanced Tool Use on Claude](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/advanced-tool-use)
- [Claude Code MCP Documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/mcp)

---

## 11. Getting Started

### 11.1 First-Time Setup

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on the framework branch
git checkout framework/quality-system

# 2. Read the context
cat pharos/insights/context.md

# 3. Check project status
./init.sh

# 4. Review any pending tickets
ls pharos/tickets/pending/
```

### 11.2 As an Agent

1. **Identify your role** from Section 4
2. **Read the context** from `pharos/insights/context.md`
3. **Check for tickets** in your domain
4. **Perform your checks** per your agent specification
5. **Update the ticket** with your decision and evidence
6. **Log your session** in `pharos/insights/session-log.md`

### 11.3 Agent Invocation Prompt

```markdown
You are the PHAROS [AGENT_NAME] Agent.

Read the current context from: pharos/insights/context.md
Check for tickets requiring your review in: pharos/tickets/pending/

Your responsibilities:
[List from Section 4]

Your checks:
[Checklist from Section 4]

Update any reviewed tickets with:
- status: APPROVED or REJECTED
- feedback: Specific, actionable feedback
- evidence: Screenshots, logs, or other proof

Log your session in: pharos/insights/session-log.md
```

---

*PHAROS: Guiding development toward excellence, one agent at a time.*

---

2025-12-13T22:30:00Z (by: Human + ORCHESTRATOR)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|

| **Goal** | Recover client confidence with impressive demo |
| **Standards** | Palantir (data) + Google (engineering) + Pentagram (design) |

---

## 2. Current State

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|

| **Main Branch** | `main` (demo development) |
| **Phase** | PHAROS Framework Setup |
| **Blockers** | None |
| **Priority** | Complete PHAROS framework, then apply to demo |

---

## 3. Environment

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Date** | 2025-12-13 |
| **OS** | macOS Darwin 25.1.0 |
| **Node** | v20.x (verify with `node -v`) |
| **Package Manager** | pnpm 8.x |
| **IDE** | Cursor |

---

## 4. Critical Business Context

### The Situation
- Initial client presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- Client confidence needs to be recovered
- This demo must be **impressive** вҖ” not just functional

### The Product
- **128 trains** across 2 fleets (Kinki Sharyo + Alstom)
- **12 years** of maintenance scheduling
- **50,000+ schedule blocks** to render
- Must feel like "Google Maps" but for time/scheduling

### The RFQ Requirements (Summary)
1. Short-term planning (weekly, monthly)
2. Long-term planning (yearly, 1-10 years)
3. "What-If" simulations (Scenario Mode)
4. Mileage underrun/overrun warnings
5. Resource utilization visibility
6. Single interface for maintenance + operations

---

## 5. Technical Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

| Constraint | Allowed | Forbidden |
|------------|---------|-----------|
| Scrolling | Virtual Scroller (math-based) | Native DOM / Ghost Divs |
| Data | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (large data) |
| State | Zustand (transient + global) | Redux, Context (high-freq) |
| Rendering | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM (>100 items), SVG |
| Animation | requestAnimationFrame | setInterval, setTimeout |

---

## 6. Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) |
| Initial load | <3s |
| Drag operations | 60fps constant |
| Memory | No GC pauses |
| Data capacity | 50,000+ blocks |

---

## 7. Active Tickets

| ID | Title | Status | Assigned To |
|----|-------|--------|-------------|
| вҖ” | No active tickets yet | вҖ” | вҖ” |

---

## 8. Recent Decisions

| Date | Decision | Rationale |
|------|----------|-----------|
| 2025-12-13 | Framework name: PHAROS | Palantir + Harness + Anthropic + R + O + S |
| 2025-12-13 | Use LangGraph for orchestration | Complex state, graph workflows |
| 2025-12-13 | Use Qdrant for vectors | Performance + complex filters |
| 2025-12-13 | Use GraphRAG for knowledge | Relationship understanding |

---

## 9. Key Documentation

| Document | Purpose | Location |
|----------|---------|----------|
| PHAROS Framework | Agent system specification | `pharos/PHAROS.md` |
| Project Architecture | Technical SSOT | `PROJECT.md` |
| UX Specification | Demo narrative, visuals | `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` |
| Client Requirements | Original RFQ | `docs/RFQ.md` |
| Agent Instructions | Per-agent guidelines | `pharos/agents/*.md` |

---

## 10. Session Continuity Notes

### What Was Done Last Session
- Created PHAROS framework branch
- Researched multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen)
- Researched knowledge systems (GraphRAG, LlamaIndex, Qdrant)
- Researched visual testing (Playwright MCP, Vision LLM)
- Created PHAROS.md with complete agent specifications

### What Needs To Be Done Next
1. Add coordination agents (Project Manager, Tech Lead, Product Owner)
2. Create agent instruction files
3. Create ticket templates
4. Create initial integration with init.sh
5. Test framework with a sample ticket

---

## 11. Agent Quick Reference

### Development Agents
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| RESEARCHER | Investigates technologies, reads docs |
| ARCHITECT | System design, dependencies, patterns |
| ENGINEER | Implementation, tests, code quality |
| DESIGNER | UX/UI, visual design, accessibility |
| COPYWRITER | Text, wording, terminology |
| MARKETING | Positioning, messaging, demo narrative |
| PERFORMANCE | Speed, memory, optimization |
| VISION | Visual verification, screenshots |
| QA | Final validation, acceptance |

### Coordination Agents
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| PROJECT_MANAGER | Timeline, priorities, dependencies |
| TECH_LEAD | Technical decisions, code review |
| PRODUCT_OWNER | Requirements, client alignment |
| ORCHESTRATOR | Process facilitation, context |

---

*When in doubt, re-read this document. Context is everything.*

# PHAROS Framework

> **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**
>
> *The Lighthouse of Excellence* вҖ” A complete multi-agent development and quality assurance framework that operates like a world-class engineering organization (Google + Palantir + Pentagram standards).

---

## 1. Executive Summary

### 1.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:

- **Researchers** investigate technologies and best practices
- **Architects** design systems and ensure structural integrity
- **Engineers** implement features with production-grade code
- **Designers** ensure Pentagram-level visual excellence
- **Copywriters** ensure professional, clear communication
- **Marketing** ensures proper positioning and messaging
- **Performance Engineers** ensure Google-scale speed
- **QA/Vision** verifies everything works as intended
- **Orchestrator** coordinates all agents and maintains context

### 1.2 The Problem We Solve

Current AI development has critical gaps:

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| AI marks features "done" without verification | Tests pass, screen is blue |
| No systematic quality gates | Inconsistent output quality |
| Context lost between sessions | Repeated mistakes |
| No specialized expertise | Jack of all trades, master of none |
| No research capability | Outdated solutions |
| No visual verification | Blind to actual output |

### 1.3 The PHAROS Solution

A multi-agent system where **specialized agents handle their domain**, communicate via **persistent tickets**, and **nothing ships without unanimous approval**.

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                           PHAROS AGENT NETWORK                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ӮRESEARCHERв”Ӯ в”ӮARCHITECT в”Ӯ в”Ӯ ENGINEER в”Ӯ в”Ӯ DESIGNER в”Ӯ в”ӮCOPYWRITERв”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ӮMARKETINGв”Ӯ в”ӮPERFORMANCв”Ӯ в”Ӯ  VISION  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ    QA    в”Ӯ в”ӮORCHESTRATв”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ    TICKET SYSTEM      в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ  (Persistent State)   в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

---

## 2. Business Context

### 2.1 The Project

**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.

**Critical Context:**
- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- This demo must **recover client confidence**
- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The previous development approach failed because:
- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
- No research capability for latest technologies
- No persistent context between sessions
- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly

---

## 3. Technology Stack

### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |

**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### 3.2 Knowledge & Memory

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[GraphRAG (Microsoft)](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)** | Knowledge graph + RAG | Understands relationships, not just similarity |
| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |
| **[LlamaIndex](https://www.llamaindex.ai/)** | Document processing | Best-in-class RAG, agentic workflows |

**Architecture:**
```
Documents вҶ’ LlamaIndex вҶ’ GraphRAG вҶ’ Neo4j (relationships)
                                  вҶ’ Qdrant (embeddings)
```

### 3.3 Visual Testing & Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Playwright MCP](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-tools)** | Browser automation | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Claude Vision** | Screenshot analysis | Semantic understanding of UI |
| **[Applitools](https://applitools.com/)** | Visual AI testing | Enterprise-grade visual regression |
| **[Percy](https://percy.io/)** | Visual review | CI/CD integration |
| **[pixelmatch](https://github.com/mapbox/pixelmatch)** | Pixel comparison | Fast baseline comparison |

### 3.4 Performance Monitoring

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Lighthouse CI](https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci)** | Performance audits | Google's standard |
| **[Web Vitals](https://web.dev/vitals/)** | Core metrics | LCP, CLS, INP |
| **[Sentry](https://sentry.io/)** | Error tracking + RUM | Full observability |
| **[Grafana Faro](https://grafana.com/oss/faro/)** | Frontend observability | Real user monitoring |

### 3.5 Code Quality

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **TypeScript Strict** | Type safety | No `any`, full inference |
| **[ESLint](https://eslint.org/)** | Linting | Configurable, extensible |
| **[Vitest](https://vitest.dev/)** | Unit testing | Fast, Vite-native |
| **[Playwright](https://playwright.dev/)** | E2E testing | Best cross-browser support |
| **[madge](https://github.com/pahen/madge)** | Dependency analysis | Detect circular deps |

### 3.6 Copy & Marketing

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **Claude API** | Copywriting | Best reasoning, brand voice |
| **[Vale](https://vale.sh/)** | Prose linting | Style guide enforcement |

input.new_string:
### 1.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:

**Leadership & Coordination:**
- **Project Manager** вҖ” Timeline, priorities, dependencies, stakeholder communication
- **Tech Lead** вҖ” Technical decisions, code standards, architectural guidance
- **Product Owner** вҖ” Requirements alignment, client needs, acceptance criteria

**Development Team:**
- **Researcher** вҖ” Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions
- **Architect** вҖ” System design, dependencies, patterns, structural integrity
- **Engineer** вҖ” Implementation, tests, production-grade code
- **Designer** вҖ” UX/UI, visual hierarchy, accessibility, cognitive psychology
- **Copywriter** вҖ” Wording, terminology, tone, clarity
- **Marketing** вҖ” Positioning, messaging, demo narrative, value propositions

**Quality Assurance:**
- **Performance Engineer** вҖ” Speed, memory, optimization, benchmarks
- **Vision Agent** вҖ” Visual verification, screenshot analysis
- **QA Agent** вҖ” Final validation, acceptance testing

**Orchestration:**
- **Orchestrator** вҖ” Process facilitation, context maintenance, agent coordination

# PHAROS Framework

> **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**
>
> *The Lighthouse of Excellence* вҖ” A complete multi-agent development and quality assurance framework that operates like a world-class engineering organization (Google + Palantir + Pentagram standards).

---

## 1. Executive Summary

### 1.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:

- **Researchers** investigate technologies and best practices
- **Architects** design systems and ensure structural integrity
- **Engineers** implement features with production-grade code
- **Designers** ensure Pentagram-level visual excellence
- **Copywriters** ensure professional, clear communication
- **Marketing** ensures proper positioning and messaging
- **Performance Engineers** ensure Google-scale speed
- **QA/Vision** verifies everything works as intended
- **Orchestrator** coordinates all agents and maintains context

### 1.2 The Problem We Solve

Current AI development has critical gaps:

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| AI marks features "done" without verification | Tests pass, screen is blue |
| No systematic quality gates | Inconsistent output quality |
| Context lost between sessions | Repeated mistakes |
| No specialized expertise | Jack of all trades, master of none |
| No research capability | Outdated solutions |
| No visual verification | Blind to actual output |

### 1.3 The PHAROS Solution

A multi-agent system where **specialized agents handle their domain**, communicate via **persistent tickets**, and **nothing ships without unanimous approval**.

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ  в”ӮRESEARCHERв”Ӯ в”ӮARCHITECT в”Ӯ в”Ӯ ENGINEER в”Ӯ в”Ӯ DESIGNER в”Ӯ в”ӮCOPYWRITERв”Ӯ         в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ    TICKET SYSTEM      в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ  (Persistent State)   в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

---

## 2. Business Context

### 2.1 The Project

**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.

**Critical Context:**
- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- This demo must **recover client confidence**
- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The previous development approach failed because:
- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
- No research capability for latest technologies
- No persistent context between sessions
- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly

---

## 3. Technology Stack

### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |

**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### 3.2 Knowledge & Memory

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[GraphRAG (Microsoft)](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)** | Knowledge graph + RAG | Understands relationships, not just similarity |
| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |
| **[LlamaIndex](https://www.llamaindex.ai/)** | Document processing | Best-in-class RAG, agentic workflows |

**Architecture:**
```
Documents вҶ’ LlamaIndex вҶ’ GraphRAG вҶ’ Neo4j (relationships)
                                  вҶ’ Qdrant (embeddings)
```

### 3.3 Visual Testing & Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Playwright MCP](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-tools)** | Browser automation | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Claude Vision** | Screenshot analysis | Semantic understanding of UI |
| **[Applitools](https://applitools.com/)** | Visual AI testing | Enterprise-grade visual regression |
| **[Percy](https://percy.io/)** | Visual review | CI/CD integration |
| **[pixelmatch](https://github.com/mapbox/pixelmatch)** | Pixel comparison | Fast baseline comparison |

### 3.4 Performance Monitoring

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Lighthouse CI](https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci)** | Performance audits | Google's standard |
| **[Web Vitals](https://web.dev/vitals/)** | Core metrics | LCP, CLS, INP |
| **[Sentry](https://sentry.io/)** | Error tracking + RUM | Full observability |
| **[Grafana Faro](https://grafana.com/oss/faro/)** | Frontend observability | Real user monitoring |

### 3.5 Code Quality

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **TypeScript Strict** | Type safety | No `any`, full inference |
| **[ESLint](https://eslint.org/)** | Linting | Configurable, extensible |
| **[Vitest](https://vitest.dev/)** | Unit testing | Fast, Vite-native |
| **[Playwright](https://playwright.dev/)** | E2E testing | Best cross-browser support |
| **[madge](https://github.com/pahen/madge)** | Dependency analysis | Detect circular deps |

### 3.6 Copy & Marketing

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **Claude API** | Copywriting | Best reasoning, brand voice |
| **[Vale](https://vale.sh/)** | Prose linting | Style guide enforcement |
| **[LanguageTool](https://languagetool.org/)** | Grammar checking | Multi-language support |

### 3.7 Design Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Storybook](https://storybook.js.org/)** | Component docs | Visual component library |
| **[Chromatic](https://www.chromatic.com/)** | Visual testing | Storybook integration |
| **OKLCH Color Space** | Color system | Perceptually uniform |
| **[Contrast Ratio](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/)** | Accessibility | WCAG compliance |

---

## 4. The PHAROS Agents

### 4.1 Agent Overview

| Agent | Domain | Responsibility | Key Tools |
|-------|--------|----------------|-----------|
| **ORCHESTRATOR** | Coordination | Manages workflow, context, priorities | LangGraph, Tickets |
| **RESEARCHER** | Knowledge | Finds latest tech, reads docs, investigates | WebSearch, LlamaIndex |
| **ARCHITECT** | Structure | System design, dependencies, patterns | madge, ADRs |
| **ENGINEER** | Code | Implementation, tests, quality | TypeScript, Vitest |
| **DESIGNER** | Visual | UX/UI, layout, colors, hierarchy | Figma, Storybook |
| **COPYWRITER** | Text | Wording, clarity, tone, consistency | Vale, Claude |
| **MARKETING** | Positioning | Messaging, value props, demo narrative | Claude, templates |
| **PERFORMANCE** | Speed | Benchmarks, optimization, profiling | Lighthouse, DevTools |
| **VISION** | Verification | Visual testing, screenshot analysis | Playwright MCP, Claude Vision |
| **QA** | Validation | Integration, E2E, acceptance | Playwright, Vitest |

---

### 4.2 ORCHESTRATOR Agent

**Role:** The conductor of the orchestra. Maintains global context, assigns tasks, resolves conflicts.

**Responsibilities:**
- Read project state from `pharos/insights/`
- Assign tasks to appropriate agents
- Track ticket status and priorities
- Maintain session continuity
- Resolve inter-agent conflicts

**Context Maintained:**
```yaml
project:
  name: MMS Fractal Planner
  goal: Recover client confidence with impressive demo
  standards: [Palantir, Google, Pentagram]

current_state:
  branch: main (demo) | framework/quality-system (PHAROS)
  phase: current development phase
  blockers: list of blocking issues

environment:
  date: 2025-12-13
  os: macOS Darwin 25.1.0
```

---

### 4.3 RESEARCHER Agent

**Role:** The knowledge seeker. Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions.

**Responsibilities:**
- Research latest technologies and best practices
- Read and summarize documentation
- Find solutions to technical problems
- Update knowledge base with findings
- Validate that solutions are current (not outdated)

**Tools:**
- `WebSearch` вҖ” Find latest information
- `WebFetch` вҖ” Read documentation pages
- `LlamaIndex` вҖ” Process and index documents
- `GraphRAG` вҖ” Build knowledge relationships

**Output:**
```markdown
# Research: [Topic]

## Summary
[Key findings in 2-3 sentences]

## Recommendations
1. Use X because Y
2. Avoid Z because W

## Sources
- [Link 1](url) - accessed 2025-12-13
- [Link 2](url) - accessed 2025-12-13

## Caveats
- This may change by [date]
- Alternative: [option]
```

---

### 4.4 ARCHITECT Agent

**Role:** The structural engineer. Ensures system integrity and scalability.

**Responsibilities:**
- Design system architecture
- Review code structure and dependencies
- Enforce architectural constraints (no Ghost Divs, SoA, etc.)
- Create and maintain ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
- Detect and prevent architectural drift

**Checks:**
- [ ] No circular dependencies (`madge --circular`)
- [ ] Proper separation of concerns
- [ ] Data structures are optimal (SoA for large data)
- [ ] State management is correct (Zustand transient vs global)
- [ ] No forbidden patterns per PROJECT.md

**Tools:**
- `madge` вҖ” Dependency graph analysis
- AST analysis for pattern detection
- Custom architecture validators

---

### 4.5 ENGINEER Agent

**Role:** The implementer. Writes production-grade code.

**Responsibilities:**
- Implement features per specification
- Write comprehensive tests
- Follow coding standards strictly
- Handle errors properly
- Document complex logic

**Quality Gates:**
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (zero errors)
- [ ] `pnpm test --run` passes (all tests)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] JSDoc on exported interfaces
- [ ] No `console.log` in production code

**Standards:**
```typescript
// GOOD: Strict typing, clear naming
function calculateMileage(trainId: string, days: number): number {
  // ...
}

// BAD: Any type, unclear
function calc(x: any, y: any) {
  // ...
}
```

---

### 4.6 DESIGNER Agent

**Role:** The visual craftsman. Ensures Pentagram-level design quality.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and enforce visual hierarchy
- Ensure consistent color palette (OKLCH)
- Verify typography and spacing
- Apply cognitive psychology principles
- Ensure accessibility compliance

**Checks:**
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear (F-pattern, Z-pattern)
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
- [ ] Consistent spacing (8px grid)
- [ ] Typography scale is harmonious
- [ ] Interactive elements have feedback states
- [ ] Cognitive load is minimized (7Вұ2 rule)

**Principles:**
- **Pre-attentive processing:** Use color/size for instant recognition
- **Gestalt:** Group related elements, separate unrelated
- **Hick's Law:** Minimize choices at each step
- **Fitts's Law:** Important targets should be large and close

---

### 4.7 COPYWRITER Agent

**Role:** The wordsmith. Ensures professional, clear communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Review all user-facing text
- Ensure consistent terminology
- Maintain professional tone
- Check for typos and grammar
- Verify i18n-readiness

**Checks:**
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors
- [ ] Terminology is consistent throughout
- [ ] Tone is professional (not casual, not stuffy)
- [ ] Labels are clear and concise
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] No placeholder text ("Lorem ipsum", "TODO")
- [ ] No jargon without explanation

**Style Guide:**
```
вң“ "Save changes" (clear action)
вң— "Submit" (ambiguous)

вң“ "Unable to load schedule. Please try again."
вң— "Error 500"

вң“ "Maintenance" (consistent term)
вң— "Maintenance" / "Service" / "Repair" (inconsistent)
```

---

### 4.8 MARKETING Agent

**Role:** The storyteller. Ensures compelling positioning and messaging.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define value propositions
- Craft demo narrative
- Ensure messaging aligns with client needs
- Review all client-facing materials
- Position features for maximum impact

**Demo Narrative Structure:**
```markdown
## Opening Hook (30 seconds)
"Imagine seeing your entire 12-year maintenance plan in one view..."

## Problem Statement
"Currently, your team uses Excel and IBM Maximo separately..."

## Solution Demo
"With Fractal Planner, you can [demonstrate key feature]..."

## Proof Points
"Watch as the system automatically [impressive capability]..."

## Call to Action
"This is the 'Single Pane of Glass' you requested..."
```

**Key Messages:**
- "Google Maps of Time" вҖ” Intuitive navigation metaphor
- "Single Pane of Glass" вҖ” Client's own terminology
- "What-If Analysis" вҖ” RFQ requirement, now visual
- "Zero Latency" вҖ” Technical differentiator

---

### 4.9 PERFORMANCE Agent

**Role:** The speed demon. Ensures Google-scale performance.

**Responsibilities:**
- Benchmark all interactions
- Profile render performance
- Detect memory leaks
- Optimize bundle size
- Monitor Web Vitals

**Targets:**
| Metric | Target | Tool |
|--------|--------|------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) | Chrome DevTools |
| Initial load | <3s | Lighthouse |
| LCP | <2.5s | Web Vitals |
| INP | <200ms | Web Vitals |
| CLS | <0.1 | Web Vitals |
| Bundle size | <500KB gzipped | Vite analyzer |
| Memory | No GC pauses | Performance panel |

**Checks:**
- [ ] No frame drops during animation
- [ ] No main thread blocking >50ms
- [ ] Memory stable over time (no leaks)
- [ ] Bundle is code-split appropriately
- [ ] Assets are optimized (WebP, compression)

---

### 4.10 VISION Agent

**Role:** The eyes. Sees the application like a human.

**Responsibilities:**
- Capture screenshots of application state
- Analyze visual output semantically
- Verify expected elements are visible
- Detect visual regressions
- Provide visual evidence for tickets

**Tools:**
- **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser control
- **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis
- **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level comparison
- **Applitools** вҖ” AI visual testing (optional)

**Verification Flow:**
```
1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
2. Navigate to target URL
3. Set viewport/zoom as specified
4. Capture screenshot
5. Analyze with Vision LLM:
   - "Describe what you see"
   - "Are there colored bars visible?"
   - "Is the canvas blank or populated?"
6. Compare against visual criteria
7. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence
```

---

### 4.11 QA Agent

**Role:** The gatekeeper. Final validation before approval.

**Responsibilities:**
- Run full test suite
- Verify acceptance criteria
- Cross-check all agent approvals
- Make final APPROVED/REJECTED decision
- Document evidence

**Final Checklist:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All E2E tests pass
- [ ] ARCHITECT approved
- [ ] ENGINEER approved
- [ ] DESIGNER approved
- [ ] COPYWRITER approved
- [ ] MARKETING approved
- [ ] PERFORMANCE approved
- [ ] VISION approved

---

## 5. The Ticket System

### 5.1 Directory Structure

```
pharos/tickets/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ pending/          # Awaiting review
в”ңв”Җв”Җ in-review/        # Currently being reviewed
в”ңв”Җв”Җ approved/         # Passed all gates
в”ңв”Җв”Җ rejected/         # Failed with feedback
в””в”Җв”Җ archive/          # Historical records
```

### 5.2 Ticket Schema

```json
{
  "ticketId": "TKT-2025-001",
  "featureId": "render-003",
  "title": "LOD 1: Instanced Mesh for Bars",
  "description": "Implement instanced rendering for maintenance bars",

  "submittedBy": "ENGINEER",
  "submittedAt": "2025-12-13T22:00:00Z",
  "status": "PENDING",

  "gates": {
    "architect": { "status": "PENDING", "agent": null, "feedback": null },
    "engineer": { "status": "APPROVED", "agent": "ENGINEER", "feedback": "Self-review passed" },
    "designer": { "status": "PENDING", "agent": null, "feedback": null },
    "copywriter": { "status": "N/A", "agent": null, "feedback": "No user-facing text" },

Prevent context loss between sessions. All learnings are persisted.

в”ңв”Җв”Җ context.md          # Current project context (always read first)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ session-log.md      # Chronological session history
в”ңв”Җв”Җ known-issues.md     # Documented problems and solutions
в”ңв”Җв”Җ patterns.md         # Good and bad patterns identified
в”ңв”Җв”Җ decisions.md        # Architecture Decision Records
в””в”Җв”Җ research/           # Research findings by topic
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ multi-agent.md
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ visualization.md
    в””в”Җв”Җ ...
```

## 7. Integration Points

### 7.1 Enhanced init.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "================================================"
echo "  PHAROS Framework - Project Status Check"
echo "================================================"
echo ""
echo "Date: $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
echo "OS: $(uname -s) $(uname -r)"
echo "Node: $(node -v)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo ""

# Check for rejected tickets
REJECTED=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вқҢ CRITICAL: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) require attention"
  ls pharos/tickets/rejected/
  echo ""
fi

# Check for pending tickets
PENDING=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/pending/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вҸі PENDING: $PENDING ticket(s) awaiting review"
fi

# Check for in-review tickets
INREVIEW=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/in-review/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$INREVIEW" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "рҹ”Қ IN REVIEW: $INREVIEW ticket(s) being reviewed"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Quality Gates ==="
echo "Running: pnpm typecheck..."
pnpm typecheck || { echo "вқҢ TypeCheck FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… TypeCheck passed"

echo "Running: pnpm lint..."
pnpm lint || { echo "вқҢ Lint FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Lint passed"

echo "Running: pnpm test --run..."
pnpm test --run || { echo "вқҢ Tests FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Tests passed"

echo ""
echo "=== All Checks Passed ==="
echo "Ready to proceed with development."
```

## 8. Workflow Examples

### 8.1 Implementing a New Feature

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns task to RESEARCHER
2. RESEARCHER investigates best approach, updates knowledge base
3. ORCHESTRATOR assigns implementation to ENGINEER
4. ENGINEER implements feature, writes tests
5. ENGINEER creates ticket in pending/
6. All agents review in parallel:
   - ARCHITECT checks structure
   - DESIGNER checks visuals
   - COPYWRITER checks text (if any)
   - PERFORMANCE benchmarks
   - VISION captures and analyzes screenshots
7. QA makes final decision
8. If APPROVED вҶ’ merge
9. If REJECTED вҶ’ ENGINEER fixes, resubmits
```

### 8.2 Handling a Bug Report

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR creates investigation ticket
2. VISION captures current state (screenshot)
3. RESEARCHER investigates root cause
4. ARCHITECT determines if structural issue
5. ENGINEER implements fix
6. VISION verifies fix visually
7. QA validates all tests pass
8. APPROVED вҶ’ merge
```

---

## 9. Success Metrics

PHAROS is successful when:

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| False positives | 0 | Features marked "done" that don't work |
| Visual bugs | 0 | Verified by VISION agent |
| Context retention | 100% | No repeated mistakes across sessions |
| Quality consistency | 100% | All output meets standards |
| Iteration speed | <2 cycles | Tickets approved within 2 review cycles |

## 11. Getting Started

### 11.1 First-Time Setup

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on the framework branch
git checkout framework/quality-system

# 2. Read the context
cat pharos/insights/context.md

# 3. Check project status
./init.sh

# 4. Review any pending tickets
ls pharos/tickets/pending/
```

### 11.2 As an Agent

1. **Identify your role** from Section 4
2. **Read the context** from `pharos/insights/context.md`
3. **Check for tickets** in your domain
4. **Perform your checks** per your agent specification
5. **Update the ticket** with your decision and evidence
6. **Log your session** in `pharos/insights/session-log.md`

### 11.3 Agent Invocation Prompt

```markdown
You are the PHAROS [AGENT_NAME] Agent.

Read the current context from: pharos/insights/context.md
Check for tickets requiring your review in: pharos/tickets/pending/

Your responsibilities:
[List from Section 4]

Your checks:
[Checklist from Section 4]

Update any reviewed tickets with:
- status: APPROVED or REJECTED
- feedback: Specific, actionable feedback
- evidence: Screenshots, logs, or other proof

Log your session in: pharos/insights/session-log.md
```

## 4B. Coordination Agents

### 4B.1 PROJECT_MANAGER Agent

**Role:** The planner. Manages timeline, priorities, and stakeholder communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and track project milestones
- Prioritize tasks and manage backlog
- Identify dependencies between tasks
- Communicate status to stakeholders
- Manage risks and blockers
- Ensure deadlines are realistic and met

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/roadmap.md` вҖ” Project timeline and milestones
- `pharos/insights/backlog.md` вҖ” Prioritized task list
- `pharos/insights/risks.md` вҖ” Risk register

**Key Decisions:**
- What to work on next
- When to cut scope vs extend timeline
- How to communicate delays

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review current state from context.md
2. Check ticket status across all queues
3. Update roadmap and priorities
4. Assign next tasks to ORCHESTRATOR
5. Communicate blockers to TECH_LEAD
```

---

### 4B.2 TECH_LEAD Agent

**Role:** The technical authority. Makes architectural decisions and maintains code quality standards.

**Responsibilities:**
- Make final technical decisions when agents disagree
- Review critical code changes
- Maintain coding standards document
- Guide ARCHITECT and ENGINEER on complex issues
- Balance technical debt vs feature delivery
- Approve major architectural changes

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/decisions.md` вҖ” Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
- `pharos/insights/standards.md` вҖ” Coding standards and conventions
- `pharos/insights/tech-debt.md` вҖ” Technical debt tracker

**Key Decisions:**
- Which technology/library to use
- How to structure complex features
- When to refactor vs ship
- How to resolve technical disagreements

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review ARCHITECT proposals
2. Validate against PROJECT.md constraints
3. Make ADR if significant decision
4. Communicate decision to ENGINEER
5. Update standards if needed
```

---

### 4B.3 PRODUCT_OWNER Agent

**Role:** The client advocate. Ensures product meets client needs and RFQ requirements.

**Responsibilities:**
- Translate RFQ requirements into acceptance criteria
- Validate features against client expectations
- Prioritize features by client value
- Define "done" from client perspective
- Review demo narrative and messaging
- Ensure UX matches client workflow

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/requirements.md` вҖ” Interpreted requirements with acceptance criteria
- `pharos/insights/client-feedback.md` вҖ” Client feedback and concerns
- `pharos/insights/demo-script.md` вҖ” Demo narrative and talking points

**Key Decisions:**
- Does this feature meet client needs?
- What is the priority from client perspective?
- Is the demo compelling?
- Does the UX match railway operations workflow?

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review RFQ.md for original requirements
2. Check UX_FOR_DEMO.md for alignment
3. Validate ticket acceptance criteria
4. Review MARKETING messaging for accuracy
5. Approve/reject from client perspective
```

**RFQ Alignment Checklist:**
- [ ] Short-term planning (weekly, monthly) supported
- [ ] Long-term planning (1-10 years) supported
- [ ] What-If simulation available
- [ ] Mileage warnings visible
- [ ] Resource utilization clear
- [ ] Single interface (no page navigation)

---

### 4B.4 ORCHESTRATOR Agent (Enhanced)

**Role:** The facilitator. Coordinates agent workflow and maintains context.

**Responsibilities:**
- Maintain `pharos/insights/context.md` (always current)
- Route tasks to appropriate agents
- Facilitate agent communication
- Resolve process blockers (not technical)
- Ensure session continuity
- Log all sessions in session-log.md

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/context.md` вҖ” Current project state (READ FIRST)
- `pharos/insights/session-log.md` вҖ” Chronological session history
- `pharos/tickets/` вҖ” Ticket routing and status

**Workflow Coordination:**

```

## 4B. Coordination Agents

### 4B.1 PROJECT_MANAGER Agent

**Role:** The planner. Manages timeline, priorities, and stakeholder communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and track project milestones
- Prioritize tasks and manage backlog
- Identify dependencies between tasks
- Communicate status to stakeholders
- Manage risks and blockers
- Ensure deadlines are realistic and met

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/roadmap.md` вҖ” Project timeline and milestones
- `pharos/insights/backlog.md` вҖ” Prioritized task list
- `pharos/insights/risks.md` вҖ” Risk register

**Key Decisions:**
- What to work on next
- When to cut scope vs extend timeline
- How to communicate delays

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review current state from context.md
2. Check ticket status across all queues
3. Update roadmap and priorities
4. Assign next tasks to ORCHESTRATOR
5. Communicate blockers to TECH_LEAD
```

---

### 4B.2 TECH_LEAD Agent

**Role:** The technical authority. Makes architectural decisions and maintains code quality standards.

**Responsibilities:**
- Make final technical decisions when agents disagree
- Review critical code changes
- Maintain coding standards document
- Guide ARCHITECT and ENGINEER on complex issues
- Balance technical debt vs feature delivery
- Approve major architectural changes

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/decisions.md` вҖ” Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
- `pharos/insights/standards.md` вҖ” Coding standards and conventions
- `pharos/insights/tech-debt.md` вҖ” Technical debt tracker

**Key Decisions:**
- Which technology/library to use
- How to structure complex features
- When to refactor vs ship
- How to resolve technical disagreements

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review ARCHITECT proposals
2. Validate against PROJECT.md constraints
3. Make ADR if significant decision
4. Communicate decision to ENGINEER
5. Update standards if needed
```

---

### 4B.3 PRODUCT_OWNER Agent

**Role:** The client advocate. Ensures product meets client needs and RFQ requirements.

**Responsibilities:**
- Translate RFQ requirements into acceptance criteria
- Validate features against client expectations
- Prioritize features by client value
- Define "done" from client perspective
- Review demo narrative and messaging
- Ensure UX matches client workflow

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/requirements.md` вҖ” Interpreted requirements with acceptance criteria
- `pharos/insights/client-feedback.md` вҖ” Client feedback and concerns
- `pharos/insights/demo-script.md` вҖ” Demo narrative and talking points

**Key Decisions:**
- Does this feature meet client needs?
- What is the priority from client perspective?
- Is the demo compelling?
- Does the UX match railway operations workflow?

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review RFQ.md for original requirements
2. Check UX_FOR_DEMO.md for alignment
3. Validate ticket acceptance criteria
4. Review MARKETING messaging for accuracy
5. Approve/reject from client perspective
```

**RFQ Alignment Checklist:**
- [ ] Short-term planning (weekly, monthly) supported
- [ ] Long-term planning (1-10 years) supported
- [ ] What-If simulation available
- [ ] Mileage warnings visible
- [ ] Resource utilization clear
- [ ] Single interface (no page navigation)

---

### 4B.4 ORCHESTRATOR Agent (Enhanced)

**Role:** The facilitator. Coordinates agent workflow and maintains context.

**Responsibilities:**
- Maintain `pharos/insights/context.md` (always current)
- Route tasks to appropriate agents
- Facilitate agent communication
- Resolve process blockers (not technical)
- Ensure session continuity
- Log all sessions in session-log.md

**Artifacts Managed:**
- `pharos/insights/context.md` вҖ” Current project state (READ FIRST)
- `pharos/insights/session-log.md` вҖ” Chronological session history
- `pharos/tickets/` вҖ” Ticket routing and status

**Workflow Coordination:**

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
