# PHAROS Framework

> **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**
>
> *The Lighthouse of Excellence* вҖ” A complete multi-agent development and quality assurance framework that operates like a world-class engineering organization (Google + Palantir + Pentagram standards).

---

## 1. Executive Summary

### 1.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:

**Leadership & Coordination:**
- **Project Manager** вҖ” Timeline, priorities, dependencies, stakeholder communication
- **Tech Lead** вҖ” Technical decisions, code standards, architectural guidance
- **Product Owner** вҖ” Requirements alignment, client needs, acceptance criteria

**Development Team:**
- **Researcher** вҖ” Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions
- **Architect** вҖ” System design, dependencies, patterns, structural integrity
- **Engineer** вҖ” Implementation, tests, production-grade code
- **Designer** вҖ” UX/UI, visual hierarchy, accessibility, cognitive psychology
- **Copywriter** вҖ” Wording, terminology, tone, clarity
- **Marketing** вҖ” Positioning, messaging, demo narrative, value propositions

**Quality Assurance:**
- **Performance Engineer** вҖ” Speed, memory, optimization, benchmarks
- **Vision Agent** вҖ” Visual verification, screenshot analysis
- **QA Agent** вҖ” Final validation, acceptance testing

**Orchestration:**
- **Orchestrator** вҖ” Process facilitation, context maintenance, agent coordination

### 1.2 The Problem We Solve

Current AI development has critical gaps:

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| AI marks features "done" without verification | Tests pass, screen is blue |
| No systematic quality gates | Inconsistent output quality |
| Context lost between sessions | Repeated mistakes |
| No specialized expertise | Jack of all trades, master of none |
| No research capability | Outdated solutions |
| No visual verification | Blind to actual output |

### 1.3 The PHAROS Solution

A multi-agent system where **specialized agents handle their domain**, communicate via **persistent tickets**, and **nothing ships without unanimous approval**.

```

## 2. Business Context

### 2.1 The Project

**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.

**Critical Context:**
- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- This demo must **recover client confidence**
- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The previous development approach failed because:
- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
- No research capability for latest technologies
- No persistent context between sessions
- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly

---

## 3. Technology Stack

### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |

**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### 3.2 Knowledge & Memory

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[GraphRAG (Microsoft)](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)** | Knowledge graph + RAG | Understands relationships, not just similarity |
| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |
| **[LlamaIndex](https://www.llamaindex.ai/)** | Document processing | Best-in-class RAG, agentic workflows |

**Architecture:**
```
Documents вҶ’ LlamaIndex вҶ’ GraphRAG вҶ’ Neo4j (relationships)
                                  вҶ’ Qdrant (embeddings)
```

### 3.3 Visual Testing & Verification

| **[Applitools](https://applitools.com/)** | Visual AI testing | Enterprise-grade visual regression |
| **[Percy](https://percy.io/)** | Visual review | CI/CD integration |
| **[pixelmatch](https://github.com/mapbox/pixelmatch)** | Pixel comparison | Fast baseline comparison |

### 4.2 ORCHESTRATOR Agent

**Role:** The conductor of the orchestra. Maintains global context, assigns tasks, resolves conflicts.

**Responsibilities:**
- Read project state from `pharos/insights/`
- Assign tasks to appropriate agents
- Track ticket status and priorities
- Maintain session continuity
- Resolve inter-agent conflicts

**Context Maintained:**
```yaml
project:
  name: MMS Fractal Planner
  goal: Recover client confidence with impressive demo
  standards: [Palantir, Google, Pentagram]

current_state:
  branch: main (demo) | framework/quality-system (PHAROS)
  phase: current development phase
  blockers: list of blocking issues

environment:
  date: 2025-12-13
  os: macOS Darwin 25.1.0
```

---

### 4.3 RESEARCHER Agent

**Role:** The knowledge seeker. Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions.

**Responsibilities:**
- Research latest technologies and best practices
- Read and summarize documentation
- Find solutions to technical problems
- Update knowledge base with findings
- Validate that solutions are current (not outdated)

**Tools:**
- `WebSearch` вҖ” Find latest information
- `WebFetch` вҖ” Read documentation pages
- `LlamaIndex` вҖ” Process and index documents
- `GraphRAG` вҖ” Build knowledge relationships

**Output:**
```markdown
# Research: [Topic]

## Summary
[Key findings in 2-3 sentences]

## Recommendations
1. Use X because Y
2. Avoid Z because W

## Sources
- [Link 1](url) - accessed 2025-12-13
- [Link 2](url) - accessed 2025-12-13

## Caveats
- This may change by [date]
- Alternative: [option]
```

---

### 4.4 ARCHITECT Agent

**Role:** The structural engineer. Ensures system integrity and scalability.

**Responsibilities:**
- Design system architecture
- Review code structure and dependencies
- Enforce architectural constraints (no Ghost Divs, SoA, etc.)
- Create and maintain ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
- Detect and prevent architectural drift

**Checks:**
- [ ] No circular dependencies (`madge --circular`)
- [ ] Proper separation of concerns
- [ ] Data structures are optimal (SoA for large data)
- [ ] State management is correct (Zustand transient vs global)
- [ ] No forbidden patterns per PROJECT.md

**Tools:**
- `madge` вҖ” Dependency graph analysis
- AST analysis for pattern detection
- Custom architecture validators

---

### 4.5 ENGINEER Agent

**Role:** The implementer. Writes production-grade code.

**Responsibilities:**
- Implement features per specification
- Write comprehensive tests
- Follow coding standards strictly
- Handle errors properly
- Document complex logic

**Quality Gates:**
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (zero errors)
- [ ] `pnpm test --run` passes (all tests)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] JSDoc on exported interfaces
- [ ] No `console.log` in production code

**Standards:**
```typescript
// GOOD: Strict typing, clear naming
function calculateMileage(trainId: string, days: number): number {
  // ...
}

// BAD: Any type, unclear
function calc(x: any, y: any) {
  // ...
}
```

---

### 4.6 DESIGNER Agent

**Role:** The visual craftsman. Ensures Pentagram-level design quality.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and enforce visual hierarchy
- Ensure consistent color palette (OKLCH)
- Verify typography and spacing
- Apply cognitive psychology principles
- Ensure accessibility compliance

**Checks:**
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear (F-pattern, Z-pattern)
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
- [ ] Consistent spacing (8px grid)
- [ ] Typography scale is harmonious
- [ ] Interactive elements have feedback states
- [ ] Cognitive load is minimized (7Вұ2 rule)

**Principles:**
- **Pre-attentive processing:** Use color/size for instant recognition
- **Gestalt:** Group related elements, separate unrelated
- **Hick's Law:** Minimize choices at each step
- **Fitts's Law:** Important targets should be large and close

---

### 4.7 COPYWRITER Agent

**Role:** The wordsmith. Ensures professional, clear communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Review all user-facing text
- Ensure consistent terminology
- Maintain professional tone
- Check for typos and grammar
- Verify i18n-readiness

**Checks:**
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors
- [ ] Terminology is consistent throughout
- [ ] Tone is professional (not casual, not stuffy)
- [ ] Labels are clear and concise
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] No placeholder text ("Lorem ipsum", "TODO")
- [ ] No jargon without explanation

**Style Guide:**
```
вң“ "Save changes" (clear action)
вң— "Submit" (ambiguous)

вң“ "Unable to load schedule. Please try again."
вң— "Error 500"

вң“ "Maintenance" (consistent term)
вң— "Maintenance" / "Service" / "Repair" (inconsistent)
```

---

### 4.8 MARKETING Agent

**Role:** The storyteller. Ensures compelling positioning and messaging.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define value propositions
- Craft demo narrative
- Ensure messaging aligns with client needs
- Review all client-facing materials
- Position features for maximum impact

**Demo Narrative Structure:**
```markdown
## Opening Hook (30 seconds)
"Imagine seeing your entire 12-year maintenance plan in one view..."

## Problem Statement
"Currently, your team uses Excel and IBM Maximo separately..."

## Solution Demo
"With Fractal Planner, you can [demonstrate key feature]..."

## Proof Points
"Watch as the system automatically [impressive capability]..."

## Call to Action
"This is the 'Single Pane of Glass' you requested..."
```

**Key Messages:**
- "Google Maps of Time" вҖ” Intuitive navigation metaphor
- "Single Pane of Glass" вҖ” Client's own terminology
- "What-If Analysis" вҖ” RFQ requirement, now visual
- "Zero Latency" вҖ” Technical differentiator

---

### 4.9 PERFORMANCE Agent

**Role:** The speed demon. Ensures Google-scale performance.

**Responsibilities:**
- Benchmark all interactions
- Profile render performance
- Detect memory leaks
- Optimize bundle size
- Monitor Web Vitals

**Targets:**
| Metric | Target | Tool |
|--------|--------|------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) | Chrome DevTools |
| Initial load | <3s | Lighthouse |
| LCP | <2.5s | Web Vitals |
| INP | <200ms | Web Vitals |
| CLS | <0.1 | Web Vitals |
| Bundle size | <500KB gzipped | Vite analyzer |
| Memory | No GC pauses | Performance panel |

**Checks:**
- [ ] No frame drops during animation
- [ ] No main thread blocking >50ms
- [ ] Memory stable over time (no leaks)
- [ ] Bundle is code-split appropriately
- [ ] Assets are optimized (WebP, compression)

---

### 4.10 VISION Agent

**Role:** The eyes. Sees the application like a human.

**Responsibilities:**
- Capture screenshots of application state
- Analyze visual output semantically
- Verify expected elements are visible
- Detect visual regressions
- Provide visual evidence for tickets

**Tools:**
- **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser control
- **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis
- **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level comparison
- **Applitools** вҖ” AI visual testing (optional)

**Verification Flow:**
```
1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
2. Navigate to target URL
3. Set viewport/zoom as specified
4. Capture screenshot
5. Analyze with Vision LLM:
   - "Describe what you see"
   - "Are there colored bars visible?"
   - "Is the canvas blank or populated?"
6. Compare against visual criteria
7. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence
```

---

### 4.11 QA Agent

**Role:** The gatekeeper. Final validation before approval.

**Responsibilities:**
- Run full test suite
- Verify acceptance criteria
- Cross-check all agent approvals
- Make final APPROVED/REJECTED decision
- Document evidence

**Final Checklist:**
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All E2E tests pass
- [ ] ARCHITECT approved
- [ ] ENGINEER approved
- [ ] DESIGNER approved
- [ ] COPYWRITER approved
- [ ] MARKETING approved

## 7. Integration Points

### 7.1 Enhanced init.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "================================================"
echo "  PHAROS Framework - Project Status Check"
echo "================================================"
echo ""
echo "Date: $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
echo "OS: $(uname -s) $(uname -r)"
echo "Node: $(node -v)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo ""

# Check for rejected tickets
REJECTED=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вқҢ CRITICAL: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) require attention"
  ls pharos/tickets/rejected/
  echo ""
fi

# Check for pending tickets
PENDING=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/pending/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вҸі PENDING: $PENDING ticket(s) awaiting review"
fi

# Check for in-review tickets
INREVIEW=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/in-review/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$INREVIEW" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "рҹ”Қ IN REVIEW: $INREVIEW ticket(s) being reviewed"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Quality Gates ==="
echo "Running: pnpm typecheck..."
pnpm typecheck || { echo "вқҢ TypeCheck FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… TypeCheck passed"

echo "Running: pnpm lint..."
pnpm lint || { echo "вқҢ Lint FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Lint passed"

echo "Running: pnpm test --run..."
pnpm test --run || { echo "вқҢ Tests FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Tests passed"

echo ""
echo "=== All Checks Passed ==="
echo "Ready to proceed with development."
```

## 8. Workflow Examples

### 8.1 Implementing a New Feature

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns task to RESEARCHER
2. RESEARCHER investigates best approach, updates knowledge base
3. ORCHESTRATOR assigns implementation to ENGINEER
4. ENGINEER implements feature, writes tests
5. ENGINEER creates ticket in pending/
6. All agents review in parallel:
   - ARCHITECT checks structure
   - DESIGNER checks visuals
   - COPYWRITER checks text (if any)
   - PERFORMANCE benchmarks
   - VISION captures and analyzes screenshots
7. QA makes final decision
8. If APPROVED вҶ’ merge
9. If REJECTED вҶ’ ENGINEER fixes, resubmits
```

### 8.2 Handling a Bug Report

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR creates investigation ticket
2. VISION captures current state (screenshot)
3. RESEARCHER investigates root cause
4. ARCHITECT determines if structural issue
5. ENGINEER implements fix
6. VISION verifies fix visually
7. QA validates all tests pass
8. APPROVED вҶ’ merge
```

- [Playwright MCP Integration](https://kailash-pathak.medium.com/modern-test-automation-with-ai-llm-and-playwright-mcp-model-context-protocol-0c311292c7fb)
- [Using Vision LLMs For UI Testing](https://courses.cs.washington.edu/courses/cse503/25wi/final-reports/Using%20Vision%20LLMs%20For%20UI%20Testing.pdf)

### Performance Monitoring
- [Best Frontend Performance Monitoring Tools 2025](https://cssauthor.com/best-frontend-performance-monitoring-tools/)
- [Best Real User Monitoring Tools 2025](https://www.debugbear.com/software/best-real-user-monitoring-tools)

### MCP & Claude
- [Model Context Protocol Introduction](https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol)
- [Advanced Tool Use on Claude](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/advanced-tool-use)
- [Claude Code MCP Documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/mcp)

---

## 11. Getting Started

### 11.1 First-Time Setup

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on the framework branch
git checkout framework/quality-system

# 2. Read the context
cat pharos/insights/context.md

# 3. Check project status
./init.sh

# 4. Review any pending tickets
ls pharos/tickets/pending/
```

### 11.2 As an Agent

1. **Identify your role** from Section 4
2. **Read the context** from `pharos/insights/context.md`
3. **Check for tickets** in your domain
4. **Perform your checks** per your agent specification
5. **Update the ticket** with your decision and evidence
6. **Log your session** in `pharos/insights/session-log.md`

### 11.3 Agent Invocation Prompt

```markdown
You are the PHAROS [AGENT_NAME] Agent.

Read the current context from: pharos/insights/context.md
Check for tickets requiring your review in: pharos/tickets/pending/

Your responsibilities:
[List from Section 4]

Your checks:
[Checklist from Section 4]

Update any reviewed tickets with:
- status: APPROVED or REJECTED
- feedback: Specific, actionable feedback
- evidence: Screenshots, logs, or other proof

Log your session in: pharos/insights/session-log.md
```

---

*PHAROS: Guiding development toward excellence, one agent at a time.*

lines: +**Role:** The planner. Manages timeline, priorities, and stakeholder communication.
+- Ensure deadlines are realistic and met
+- When to cut scope vs extend timeline
+2. Check ticket status across all queues
+**Role:** The technical authority. Makes architectural decisions and maintains code quality standards.
+- Make final technical decisions when agents disagree
+- Guide ARCHITECT and ENGINEER on complex issues
+- Balance technical debt vs feature delivery
+**Role:** The client advocate. Ensures product meets client needs and RFQ requirements.
+- Translate RFQ requirements into acceptance criteria
+- `pharos/insights/requirements.md` вҖ” Interpreted requirements with acceptance criteria
+- `pharos/insights/demo-script.md` вҖ” Demo narrative and talking points
+- Does this feature meet client needs?
+- What is the priority from client perspective?
+- Does the UX match railway operations workflow?
+**Role:** The facilitator. Coordinates agent workflow and maintains context.
+в”Ӯ  2. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns to RESEARCHER              в”Ӯ

# PHAROS Session Log

> Chronological record of all development sessions. Each entry preserves context for future sessions.

---

## Session: 2025-12-13 22:00 вҖ” Framework Creation

### Participants
- Human (Project Owner)
- Claude (ORCHESTRATOR + multiple agent roles)

### Context at Start
- Project had documentation but no quality framework
- Previous AI work resulted in "tests pass, screen blue" syndrome
- Need to recover client confidence with impressive demo

### Actions Taken
1. Discussed need for multi-agent quality framework
2. Researched current technologies:
   - Multi-agent: LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen
   - Knowledge: GraphRAG, LlamaIndex, Qdrant
   - Visual testing: Playwright MCP, Vision LLM
   - Performance: Lighthouse, Web Vitals, Sentry
3. Named framework "PHAROS" (Palantir + Harness + Anthropic + R + O + S)
4. Created framework branch: `framework/quality-system`
5. Created PHAROS.md with complete agent specifications
6. Defined 13 agents across 4 categories:
   - Leadership: PROJECT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD, PRODUCT_OWNER
   - Development: RESEARCHER, ARCHITECT, ENGINEER, DESIGNER, COPYWRITER, MARKETING
   - QA: PERFORMANCE, VISION, QA
   - Orchestration: ORCHESTRATOR

### Key Decisions
| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| LangGraph for orchestration | Complex state, graph workflows, fault-tolerant |
| Qdrant for vectors | Performance + complex filters needed |
| GraphRAG for knowledge | Relationship understanding, not just similarity |
| Ticket-based communication | Persistent, async, traceable across sessions |

### Learnings
- The problem wasn't just "seeing the screen" вҖ” it was lack of systematic quality enforcement
- Need coordination agents (PM, Tech Lead, PO) not just execution agents
- PHAROS = complete engineering organization simulation

### Artifacts Created
- `pharos/PHAROS.md` вҖ” Complete framework specification
- `pharos/insights/context.md` вҖ” Project context (read first)
- `pharos/insights/session-log.md` вҖ” This file
- `pharos/templates/ticket.json` вҖ” Ticket template
- Directory structure for tickets and insights

### Open Questions
1. How to implement agent invocation in practice?
2. Should agents run in parallel or sequence?
3. How to handle agent disagreements?

### Next Session Should
1. Create individual agent instruction files
2. Create sample ticket to test workflow
3. Integrate PHAROS checks into init.sh
4. Begin applying framework to demo development

---

## Template for Future Sessions

```markdown
## Session: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM вҖ” Title

### Participants
- [Who was involved]

### Context at Start
- [State of project when session began]

### Actions Taken
1. [Action 1]
2. [Action 2]

### Key Decisions
| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| [Decision] | [Why] |

### Learnings
- [What was learned]

### Artifacts Created/Modified
- [File paths and what changed]

### Open Questions
1. [Unresolved questions]

### Next Session Should
1. [Priority tasks for next session]
```

---

*Always add new sessions at the TOP of this file (reverse chronological order).*

input.content:
# Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

> Technical decisions and their rationale. Maintained by TECH_LEAD.

---

## ADR-001: Multi-Agent Orchestration Framework

**Date:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Accepted
**Decider:** TECH_LEAD

### Context
Need to orchestrate multiple AI agents with different specializations working on the same project.

### Options Considered
1. **LangGraph** вҖ” Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant
2. **CrewAI** вҖ” Role-based teams, YAML-driven, simpler
3. **AutoGen** вҖ” Conversational collaboration, async-first

### Decision
Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration with **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### Rationale
- LangGraph provides better state management for complex, multi-step workflows
- Fault-tolerance and recovery are critical for long-running agent tasks
- CrewAI patterns help define clear roles without full framework overhead

### Consequences
- Steeper learning curve than CrewAI alone
- Better long-term flexibility for complex scenarios
- Can integrate LlamaIndex tools seamlessly

---

## ADR-002: Vector Database Selection

**Date:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Accepted
**Decider:** TECH_LEAD

### Context
Need vector database for storing embeddings for RAG and semantic search.

### Options Considered
1. **Pinecone** вҖ” Fully managed, expensive, best for zero-ops
2. **Weaviate** вҖ” Hybrid search, graph capabilities
3. **Qdrant** вҖ” Rust-based, fast, complex filters
4. **ChromaDB** вҖ” Simple, great for prototyping

### Decision
Use **Qdrant** for production, **ChromaDB** for local development/prototyping.

### Rationale
- Qdrant offers best performance for complex metadata filtering
- Project requires filtering by train ID, date ranges, maintenance types
- ChromaDB allows fast iteration during development

### Consequences
- Need to manage Qdrant deployment (or use cloud)
- Two database configs (dev vs prod)

---

## ADR-003: Knowledge Graph Architecture

**Date:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Accepted
**Decider:** TECH_LEAD

### Context
Need to understand relationships between project documents, not just semantic similarity.

### Decision
Use **Microsoft GraphRAG** with **Neo4j** for graph storage.

### Rationale
- GraphRAG extracts entities and relationships from documents
- Better for "What are the main themes?" type queries
- Neo4j is industry standard for graph databases

### Consequences
- More complex setup than simple RAG
- LLM costs for graph extraction
- Powerful query capabilities

---

## ADR-004: Visual Testing Strategy

**Date:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Accepted
**Decider:** TECH_LEAD

### Context
Need to verify visual output matches specifications. Previous approach failed ("tests pass, screen blue").

### Decision
Multi-layer visual testing:
1. **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser automation, screenshot capture
2. **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis of screenshots
3. **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level regression detection

### Rationale
- Playwright MCP is officially supported by Anthropic
- Claude Vision can understand "are there colored bars?" semantically
- pixelmatch catches pixel-level regressions cheaply

### Consequences
- Requires running dev server for visual tests
- Vision analysis has API costs
- Need to maintain baseline screenshots

---

## Template for Future ADRs

```markdown
## ADR-NNN: Title

**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Status:** Proposed | Accepted | Deprecated | Superseded
**Decider:** AGENT_NAME

### Context
[Why is this decision needed?]

### Options Considered
1. **Option A** вҖ” Description
2. **Option B** вҖ” Description

### Decision
[What was decided]

### Rationale
[Why this option was chosen]

### Consequences
[What are the implications, both positive and negative]
```

# Known Issues & Solutions

> Documented problems and their solutions to prevent repeated mistakes across sessions.

---

## ISSUE-001: "Tests Pass, Screen Blue" Syndrome

**Discovered:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Root cause identified, fix pending
**Severity:** Critical

### Symptoms
- All unit tests pass
- All E2E tests pass
- Lint passes
- But application shows solid blue screen with no data

### Root Cause
1. `loader.ts` mutates TypedArrays directly
2. Zustand store is not notified of changes
3. `FractalRenderer` subscription never fires
4. Canvas remains empty

### Solution
```typescript
// In src/services/mock/loader.ts, after populating arrays:
simulationStore.setState({ schedule: store.schedule });
```

### Prevention
- VISION agent must verify visual output, not just test results
- Add visual criteria to every rendering ticket
- Never mark rendering feature "done" without screenshot verification

---

## ISSUE-002: Context Loss Between Sessions

**Discovered:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Mitigated by PHAROS
**Severity:** High

### Symptoms
- AI repeats same mistakes across sessions
- Proposes solutions already tried and failed
- Loses track of project decisions

### Root Cause
- No persistent memory across sessions
- No structured logging of decisions
- No shared knowledge base

### Solution
PHAROS framework with:
- `pharos/insights/context.md` вҖ” Always read first
- `pharos/insights/session-log.md` вҖ” Chronological history
- `pharos/insights/decisions.md` вҖ” ADRs
- `pharos/insights/known-issues.md` вҖ” This file

### Prevention
- Every session must start by reading context.md
- Every session must end by updating session-log.md
- Major decisions must be recorded as ADRs

---

## ISSUE-003: AI Cannot "See" Visual Output

**Discovered:** 2025-12-13
**Status:** Mitigated by PHAROS VISION agent
**Severity:** Critical

### Symptoms
- AI claims feature is working
- Visual inspection shows otherwise
- No mechanism for AI to verify visual state

### Root Cause
- AI operates on code and test output only
- No visual feedback loop
- Screenshots not part of verification process

### Solution
PHAROS VISION agent with:
- Playwright MCP for browser automation
- Screenshot capture for every visual feature
- Claude Vision for semantic analysis
- Visual criteria in every rendering ticket

### Prevention
- Every visual feature requires VISION agent approval
- Screenshots must be attached as evidence
- Visual criteria must be explicit and measurable

---

## Template for Future Issues

```markdown
## ISSUE-NNN: Title

**Discovered:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Status:** Open | In Progress | Resolved | Mitigated
**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low

### Symptoms
- [What was observed]

### Root Cause
[Why it happened]

### Solution
[How to fix it]

### Prevention
[How to prevent it from happening again]
```

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(pharos): implement PHAROS multi-agent quality framework

PHAROS (Palantir + Harness + Anthropic + R + O + S) is a complete
multi-agent development framework that simulates a world-class
engineering organization.

Agents defined (13 total):
- Leadership: PROJECT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD, PRODUCT_OWNER
- Development: RESEARCHER, ARCHITECT, ENGINEER, DESIGNER, COPYWRITER, MARKETING
- Quality: PERFORMANCE, VISION, QA
- Orchestration: ORCHESTRATOR

Technology stack researched and selected:

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically:

1. **Initial Request**: User asked which file describes the visual aspects of the app. I found documentation files and identified that `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` was referenced but didn't exist yet.

2. **User restored two files** (`docs/PROJECT.md` and `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md`) and asked me to verify if they were removed for being outdated or not aligned with the project.

3. **Analysis of restored files**: I found that `docs/PROJECT.md` was outdated (used Ghost Div pattern vs current Virtual Scroller), but `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` was valid and should be kept.

4. **User clarified the project objective**: This is an RFQ response to recover client confidence after a bad initial presentation (user had COVID). The project should be like "Google Maps of Time" - seamless zoom from 12-year strategic view to daily operations, with bidirectional propagation of changes.

5. **User introduced Baseline vs Scenario mode**: For "What-If" simulations as required by the RFQ.

6. **User corrected my understanding about zoom levels**: It's not just 3 levels, but multiple levels for data analysis, with different chart types available per level (like Google Maps has Map/Satellite/Terrain).

7. **Documentation update requested**: User asked me to update all documentation in English with the complete vision:
   - Updated `PROJECT.md` with business context, 8 zoom levels, bidirectional propagation, Baseline vs Scenario mode
   - Updated `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` with complete UX spec, demo script, visualization options per level
   - Updated `CLAUDE.md` with new concepts
   - Removed duplicate `docs/PROJECT.md`

8. **User introduced urgent files**: `urgent-plan.txt` and `urgent-problems.txt` containing critical issues about "Blind AI" syndrome - tests pass but screen is blue.

9. **User clarified the REAL problem**: Not just "seeing the screen" but the lack of a systematic architecture that GUARANTEES quality at Palantir + Google + Pentagram standards across ALL aspects (Design, Software, Wording, Performance, etc.).

10. **PHAROS Framework creation**: User requested a complete multi-agent framework on a separate branch (`framework/quality-system`) that simulates a world-class engineering organization.

11. **Framework name**: PHAROS (Palantir + Harness + Anthropic + palantiR + gOogle + harnesS)

12. **User added coordination agents**: PROJECT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD, PRODUCT_OWNER

13. **Technology research**: I researched and documented:
    - Multi-agent: LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen
    - Knowledge: GraphRAG, Neo4j, Qdrant, LlamaIndex
    - Visual testing: Playwright MCP, Claude Vision, Applitools
    - Performance: Lighthouse, Web Vitals, Sentry

14. **Final request**: User asked to backup original harness files and create fresh files focused ONLY on PHAROS, so a new chat without context can continue following the harness.

Summary:
## 1. Primary Request and Intent

The user's core requests evolved through the conversation:

1. **Initial**: Identify documentation for visual aspects of the app
2. **Middle**: Update all project documentation to reflect the complete vision:
   - "Google Maps of Time" metaphor - seamless zoom from 12-year strategic to daily operations
   - 8 zoom levels with different visualizations per level

4. **Final request**: Create **PHAROS Framework** - a complete multi-agent development framework that simulates a world-class engineering organization, on a separate branch, with harness files reset for a fresh context continuation.

**Business Context**: This is an RFQ response for a railway operator (128 trains, 12 years). The initial presentation failed (presenter had COVID), and this demo must recover client confidence.

## 2. Key Technical Concepts

**Multi-Agent Framework (PHAROS)**:
- 13 specialized agents across 4 categories

- Quality gates that ALL must pass before "done"

**Technology Stack Selected**:

| Quality | PERFORMANCE, VISION, QA |

**Core Product Concepts**:
- "Google Maps of Time" - continuous zoom from 12 years to hours

- Baseline vs Scenario mode for What-If simulations
- Bidirectional Fractal propagation

# MMS Fractal Planner - AI Agent Instructions

## 1. The Prime Directive

You are the Senior GeoView Advisor. You do not write "toy code". You write high-performance, memory-safe, engineering-grade TypeScript.

**PROJECT.md is the Single Source of Truth.** If your training data conflicts with PROJECT.md, follow PROJECT.md.

### 1.1 Business Context

This is a **response to an RFQ** from a railway operator. The goal is to build an impressive demo that:
- Recovers client confidence after a suboptimal initial presentation
- Proves technical excellence through fluid, zero-latency interactions
- Addresses all RFQ requirements visually and functionally

### 1.2 The Core Metaphor: "Google Maps of Time"

The app is like Google Maps but for time:
- **Zoom out** вҶ’ See 12 years of strategic planning (heatmaps)
- **Zoom in** вҶ’ See daily operations (Gantt, individual trains)
- **Continuous transition** вҶ’ No page reloads, seamless zoom
- **Bidirectional propagation** вҶ’ Changes at any level ripple up and down

---

## 2. The Harness Protocol

Reference: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Before writing any code, you must execute this loop:

1. **OBSERVE:** Run `./init.sh` to see the current state and progress log.
2. **PLAN:** Read `features.json`. Select the next `pending` task.
3. **THINK:** Write a brief plan in your scratchpad.
   - _Example:_ "Implementing SoA. I need `Float64Array` for dates. I will avoid `new Date()` inside the render loop."
4. **ACT:** Write the code.
5. **VERIFY:** Run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test --run && pnpm lint` - ALL must pass.
6. **DOCUMENT:** Update `claude-progress.txt` and `features.json`.

**CRITICAL RULE:** ALL tests must pass before ANY feature can be marked as 'done'. No exceptions.

---

## 3. Architecture Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

| Feature | Allowed | Forbidden |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| **Scrolling** | Virtual Scroller (Math-based) | Native DOM Scrolling / Ghost Divs |
| **Data Structures** | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (for massive data) |
| **State** | Zustand (Transient & Global) | Redux, Context API (for high-freq updates) |
| **Rendering** | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM Elements (for >100 items), SVG |
| **Loop** | `requestAnimationFrame` | `setInterval`, `setTimeout` (for anim) |

---

## 4. Key Product Concepts

### 4.1 Zoom Levels (8 Semantic Levels)

| Temporal Scale | Default Visualization | Analysis Focus |
|----------------|----------------------|----------------|
| **12 Years** | Heatmap (saturation) | Total capacity, CAPEX/OPEX |
| **3-5 Years** | Stacked Bars + Trends | Fleet lifecycle patterns |
| **1-2 Years** | Combo Chart | Seasonality, peaks |
| **Quadrimester** | Workload Equalizer | Workload vs capacity |
| **1-3 Months** | Weekly Bars | Resource conflicts |
| **2-4 Weeks** | Simplified Gantt | Track occupancy |
| **1 Week** | Detailed Gantt | Per-train status |
| **1-3 Days** | Capsules + Details | Fitness, km remaining |

### 4.2 Visualization Selector

Like Google Maps' `[Map] [Satellite] [Terrain]`, each zoom level offers multiple chart options:
- User can switch between available visualizations for current zoom level
- Each level has a default (recommended) visualization
- See `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` for full chart options per level

### 4.3 Baseline vs Scenario Mode

| Mode | Description | Editing |
|------|-------------|---------|
| **BASELINE** | Production plan | Restricted |
| **SCENARIO** | Fork for "What-If" | Free editing |

Scenarios allow consequence-free experimentation with compare & apply workflow.

### 4.4 Fractal Propagation

Changes propagate bidirectionally:
- Operational change вҶ’ affects monthly вҶ’ affects yearly вҶ’ affects 12-year
- Strategic change вҶ’ affects yearly вҶ’ affects monthly вҶ’ affects daily

---

## 5. Coding Standards

- **Strict Typing:** No `any`. Use `unknown` or Generics if unsure.
- **JSDoc:** Required for all exported interfaces and complex algorithms (explain _why_, not just _what_).
- **No Comments in Body:** Code should be self-documenting. Use comments only for "Why" or weird math.
- **File Structure:**
  - `src/engine/`: Core logic, Physics, Math, SoA
  - `src/ui/`: React Components (Overlay, Sidebar, Constraint Deck)
  - `src/render/`: PixiJS specific code (Shaders, Layers)
  - `src/services/`: Mock API, future BFF integration

---

## 6. Common Commands

```bash
# Environment
./init.sh             # Check status and architecture compliance
pnpm dev              # Vite dev server (port 5173)

# Quality (ALL must pass before marking done)
pnpm typecheck        # TypeScript strict check
pnpm lint             # ESLint
pnpm test --run       # Vitest unit tests

# E2E Testing
pnpm test:e2e         # Playwright E2E tests

# Development
pnpm storybook        # Component documentation
```

---

## 7. Critical Documentation

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `PROJECT.md` | **SSOT** - Architecture, business context, technical spec |
| `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` | UX specification, demo script, visualization options |
| `docs/RFQ.md` | Original client requirements |
| `docs/DEMO_DATA.md` | Fleet data, maintenance types |
| `features.json` | Task tracking with phases |
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session log & next steps |

---

## 8. Performance Targets

| Metric | Target | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| **Zoom/Pan** | <16ms (60fps) | Zero perceived latency |
| **Initial load** | <3s | First meaningful paint |
| **Drag operations** | 60fps constant | No drops during interaction |
| **Memory** | No GC pauses | Smooth animation |
| **Data capacity** | 50,000+ blocks | 12 years Г— 128 trains |

---

## 9. Commit Guidelines

- Commit after each significant progress
- **NO co-authored-by** in commits
- Clear, descriptive messages
- Update `claude-progress.txt` after major milestones

Example:
```
feat(engine): implement SoA simulation store

- Added TypedArrays for schedule blocks
- Implemented Interval Tree for collision detection
```

---

## 10. Definition of Done

A feature is ONLY done when:

1. вң… Code compiles without errors (`pnpm typecheck`)
2. вң… All unit tests pass (`pnpm test --run`)
3. вң… Linting passes (`pnpm lint`)
4. вң… E2E smoke tests pass (`pnpm test:e2e`)
5. вң… Feature works as specified in `PROJECT.md` or `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md`
6. вң… `features.json` updated to `done`
7. вң… `claude-progress.txt` updated with session notes

#!/bin/bash

# MMS Harness - Environment Verifier & Test Gate
# Run this at the start of every session.
# Based on: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

set -e

echo "=================================================="
echo " MMS FRACTAL PLANNER - HARNESS PROTOCOL"
echo "=================================================="

# 1. Check Core Tools
echo "[1/6] Checking Environment..."
node -v >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "вқҢ Node.js missing"; exit 1; }
pnpm -v >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "вқҢ pnpm missing"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Node $(node -v) | pnpm $(pnpm -v)"

# 2. Check Architecture Compliance
echo ""
echo "[2/6] Verifying Architecture..."

WARNINGS=0

if [ -f "astro.config.mjs" ]; then
  echo "вҡ пёҸ  WARNING: Astro config detected. Project should be pure Vite (SPA)."
  WARNINGS=$((WARNINGS + 1))
fi

if grep -q '"@reduxjs/toolkit"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "вҡ пёҸ  WARNING: Redux detected. Architecture requires Zustand."
  WARNINGS=$((WARNINGS + 1))
fi

if [ $WARNINGS -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "вң… Architecture compliance OK"
fi

# 3. MANDATORY TEST GATE
echo ""
echo "[3/6] Running Mandatory Test Gate..."
echo "     (ALL tests must pass before any work can be committed)"

TEST_FAILED=0

echo "  вҶ’ TypeScript Check..."
if pnpm typecheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… TypeScript OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ TypeScript FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

echo "  вҶ’ Unit Tests (Vitest)..."
if pnpm test --run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… Unit Tests OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ Unit Tests FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

echo "  вҶ’ ESLint..."
if pnpm lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… ESLint OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ ESLint FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

echo "  вҶ’ E2E Smoke Tests (Playwright)..."
if pnpm test:e2e >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… E2E Tests OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ E2E Tests FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

if [ $TEST_FAILED -eq 1 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "рҹҡЁ TEST GATE FAILED!"
  echo "   You MUST fix all tests before proceeding with any new features."
  echo "   This is a BLOCKING requirement per the harness protocol."
  echo ""
fi

# 4. Load Context
echo ""
echo "[4/6] Loading Project State..."
if [ -f "claude-progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "--- LAST SESSION LOG (tail) ---"
  tail -n 30 claude-progress.txt
  echo "-------------------------------"
else
  echo "вқҢ No progress log found!"
fi

# 5. Show Git Status
echo ""
echo "[5/6] Git Status..."
git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || echo "Not a git repo"
git status -s 2>/dev/null || true

# 6. Show Next Steps
echo ""
echo "[6/6] Recommended Next Action:"
if [ -f "features.json" ]; then
  NEXT_TASK=$(grep -A1 '"status": "pending"' features.json | head -2 | grep '"name"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"name": "\([^"]*\)".*/\1/')
  if [ -n "$NEXT_TASK" ]; then
    echo "   Next pending task: $NEXT_TASK"
  fi
fi
echo "   Refer to 'features.json' for the full task list."

echo ""
echo "=================================================="
if [ $TEST_FAILED -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "вң… HARNESS READY - All checks passed"
  echo "   Run 'pnpm dev' to start development."
else
  echo "вқҢ HARNESS BLOCKED - Fix tests first"
  echo "   Run 'pnpm typecheck && pnpm test --run && pnpm lint' to diagnose"
fi
echo "=================================================="

exit $TEST_FAILED

# PHAROS Project Context

> **READ THIS FIRST** вҖ” This document contains the current state of the project and must be read by every agent before taking any action.

---

## Last Updated
2025-12-13T22:30:00Z (by: Human + ORCHESTRATOR)

---

## 1. Project Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Project Name** | MMS Fractal Planner |
| **Codename** | "Google Maps of Time" |
| **Type** | RFQ Response / Demo |
| **Client** | Railway operator (128 trains, 2 fleets) |
| **Goal** | Recover client confidence with impressive demo |
| **Standards** | Palantir (data) + Google (engineering) + Pentagram (design) |

---

## 2. Current State

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Active Branch** | `framework/quality-system` (PHAROS development) |
| **Main Branch** | `main` (demo development) |
| **Phase** | PHAROS Framework Setup |
| **Blockers** | None |
| **Priority** | Complete PHAROS framework, then apply to demo |

---

## 3. Environment

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Date** | 2025-12-13 |
| **OS** | macOS Darwin 25.1.0 |
| **Node** | v20.x (verify with `node -v`) |
| **Package Manager** | pnpm 8.x |
| **IDE** | Cursor |

---

## 4. Critical Business Context

### The Situation
- Initial client presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- Client confidence needs to be recovered
- This demo must be **impressive** вҖ” not just functional

### The Product
- **128 trains** across 2 fleets (Kinki Sharyo + Alstom)
- **12 years** of maintenance scheduling
- **50,000+ schedule blocks** to render
- Must feel like "Google Maps" but for time/scheduling

### The RFQ Requirements (Summary)
1. Short-term planning (weekly, monthly)
2. Long-term planning (yearly, 1-10 years)
3. "What-If" simulations (Scenario Mode)
4. Mileage underrun/overrun warnings
5. Resource utilization visibility
6. Single interface for maintenance + operations

---

## 5. Technical Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

| Constraint | Allowed | Forbidden |
|------------|---------|-----------|
| Scrolling | Virtual Scroller (math-based) | Native DOM / Ghost Divs |
| Data | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (large data) |
| State | Zustand (transient + global) | Redux, Context (high-freq) |
| Rendering | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM (>100 items), SVG |
| Animation | requestAnimationFrame | setInterval, setTimeout |

---

## 6. Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) |
| Initial load | <3s |
| Drag operations | 60fps constant |
| Memory | No GC pauses |
| Data capacity | 50,000+ blocks |

---

## 7. Active Tickets

| ID | Title | Status | Assigned To |
|----|-------|--------|-------------|
| вҖ” | No active tickets yet | вҖ” | вҖ” |

---

## 8. Recent Decisions

| Date | Decision | Rationale |
|------|----------|-----------|
| 2025-12-13 | Framework name: PHAROS | Palantir + Harness + Anthropic + R + O + S |
| 2025-12-13 | Use LangGraph for orchestration | Complex state, graph workflows |
| 2025-12-13 | Use Qdrant for vectors | Performance + complex filters |
| 2025-12-13 | Use GraphRAG for knowledge | Relationship understanding |

---

## 9. Key Documentation

| Document | Purpose | Location |
|----------|---------|----------|
| PHAROS Framework | Agent system specification | `pharos/PHAROS.md` |
| Project Architecture | Technical SSOT | `PROJECT.md` |
| UX Specification | Demo narrative, visuals | `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` |
| Client Requirements | Original RFQ | `docs/RFQ.md` |
| Agent Instructions | Per-agent guidelines | `pharos/agents/*.md` |

---

## 10. Session Continuity Notes

### What Was Done Last Session
- Created PHAROS framework branch
- Researched multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen)
- Researched knowledge systems (GraphRAG, LlamaIndex, Qdrant)
- Researched visual testing (Playwright MCP, Vision LLM)
- Created PHAROS.md with complete agent specifications

### What Needs To Be Done Next
1. Add coordination agents (Project Manager, Tech Lead, Product Owner)
2. Create agent instruction files
3. Create ticket templates
4. Create initial integration with init.sh
5. Test framework with a sample ticket

---

## 11. Agent Quick Reference

### Development Agents
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| RESEARCHER | Investigates technologies, reads docs |
| ARCHITECT | System design, dependencies, patterns |
| ENGINEER | Implementation, tests, code quality |
| DESIGNER | UX/UI, visual design, accessibility |
| COPYWRITER | Text, wording, terminology |
| MARKETING | Positioning, messaging, demo narrative |
| PERFORMANCE | Speed, memory, optimization |
| VISION | Visual verification, screenshots |
| QA | Final validation, acceptance |

### Coordination Agents
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| PROJECT_MANAGER | Timeline, priorities, dependencies |
| TECH_LEAD | Technical decisions, code review |
| PRODUCT_OWNER | Requirements, client alignment |
| ORCHESTRATOR | Process facilitation, context |

---

1вҶ’# PHAROS Framework
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’> **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**
     4вҶ’>
     5вҶ’> *The Lighthouse of Excellence* вҖ” A complete multi-agent development and quality assurance framework that operates like a world-class engineering organization (Google + Palantir + Pentagram standards).
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’### 1.1 What PHAROS Is
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’**Leadership & Coordination:**
    16вҶ’- **Project Manager** вҖ” Timeline, priorities, dependencies, stakeholder communication
    17вҶ’- **Tech Lead** вҖ” Technical decisions, code standards, architectural guidance
    18вҶ’- **Product Owner** вҖ” Requirements alignment, client needs, acceptance criteria
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’**Development Team:**
    21вҶ’- **Researcher** вҖ” Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions
    22вҶ’- **Architect** вҖ” System design, dependencies, patterns, structural integrity
    23вҶ’- **Engineer** вҖ” Implementation, tests, production-grade code
    24вҶ’- **Designer** вҖ” UX/UI, visual hierarchy, accessibility, cognitive psychology
    25вҶ’- **Copywriter** вҖ” Wording, terminology, tone, clarity
    26вҶ’- **Marketing** вҖ” Positioning, messaging, demo narrative, value propositions
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’**Quality Assurance:**
    29вҶ’- **Performance Engineer** вҖ” Speed, memory, optimization, benchmarks
    30вҶ’- **Vision Agent** вҖ” Visual verification, screenshot analysis
    31вҶ’- **QA Agent** вҖ” Final validation, acceptance testing
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’**Orchestration:**
    34вҶ’- **Orchestrator** вҖ” Process facilitation, context maintenance, agent coordination
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’### 1.2 The Problem We Solve
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’Current AI development has critical gaps:
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’| Problem | Impact |
    41вҶ’|---------|--------|
    42вҶ’| AI marks features "done" without verification | Tests pass, screen is blue |
    43вҶ’| No systematic quality gates | Inconsistent output quality |
    44вҶ’| Context lost between sessions | Repeated mistakes |
    45вҶ’| No specialized expertise | Jack of all trades, master of none |
    46вҶ’| No research capability | Outdated solutions |
    47вҶ’| No visual verification | Blind to actual output |
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’### 1.3 The PHAROS Solution
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’A multi-agent system where **specialized agents handle their domain**, communicate via **persistent tickets**, and **nothing ships without unanimous approval**.
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’```
    54вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

    56вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                             в”Ӯ

    76вҶ’## 2. Business Context
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’### 2.1 The Project
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’**Critical Context:**
    83вҶ’- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
    84вҶ’- This demo must **recover client confidence**
    85вҶ’- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
    86вҶ’- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
    87вҶ’- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’The previous development approach failed because:
    92вҶ’- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
    93вҶ’- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
    94вҶ’- No research capability for latest technologies
    95вҶ’- No persistent context between sessions
    96вҶ’- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’---
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’## 3. Technology Stack
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
   105вҶ’|------------|---------|------------|
   106вҶ’| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
   107вҶ’| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
   108вҶ’| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

   114вҶ’| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |

   117вҶ’| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
   118вҶ’| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |

   196вҶ’**Role:** The conductor of the orchestra. Maintains global context, assigns tasks, resolves conflicts.

   198вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   199вҶ’- Read project state from `pharos/insights/`
   200вҶ’- Assign tasks to appropriate agents
   201вҶ’- Track ticket status and priorities
   202вҶ’- Maintain session continuity
   203вҶ’- Resolve inter-agent conflicts
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’**Context Maintained:**
   206вҶ’```yaml
   207вҶ’project:
   208вҶ’  name: MMS Fractal Planner
   209вҶ’  goal: Recover client confidence with impressive demo
   210вҶ’  standards: [Palantir, Google, Pentagram]
   211вҶ’
   212вҶ’current_state:
   213вҶ’  branch: main (demo) | framework/quality-system (PHAROS)
   214вҶ’  phase: current development phase
   215вҶ’  blockers: list of blocking issues
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’environment:
   218вҶ’  date: 2025-12-13
   219вҶ’  os: macOS Darwin 25.1.0
   220вҶ’```

   224вҶ’### 4.3 RESEARCHER Agent
   225вҶ’
   226вҶ’**Role:** The knowledge seeker. Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions.

   228вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   229вҶ’- Research latest technologies and best practices
   230вҶ’- Read and summarize documentation
   231вҶ’- Find solutions to technical problems
   232вҶ’- Update knowledge base with findings
   233вҶ’- Validate that solutions are current (not outdated)
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’**Tools:**
   236вҶ’- `WebSearch` вҖ” Find latest information
   237вҶ’- `WebFetch` вҖ” Read documentation pages
   238вҶ’- `LlamaIndex` вҖ” Process and index documents
   239вҶ’- `GraphRAG` вҖ” Build knowledge relationships
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’**Output:**
   242вҶ’```markdown
   243вҶ’# Research: [Topic]
   244вҶ’
   245вҶ’## Summary
   246вҶ’[Key findings in 2-3 sentences]

   250вҶ’2. Avoid Z because W
   251вҶ’

   254вҶ’- [Link 2](url) - accessed 2025-12-13

   263вҶ’### 4.4 ARCHITECT Agent
   264вҶ’
   265вҶ’**Role:** The structural engineer. Ensures system integrity and scalability.
   266вҶ’
   267вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   268вҶ’- Design system architecture
   269вҶ’- Review code structure and dependencies
   270вҶ’- Enforce architectural constraints (no Ghost Divs, SoA, etc.)
   271вҶ’- Create and maintain ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
   272вҶ’- Detect and prevent architectural drift
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’**Checks:**
   275вҶ’- [ ] No circular dependencies (`madge --circular`)
   276вҶ’- [ ] Proper separation of concerns
   277вҶ’- [ ] Data structures are optimal (SoA for large data)
   278вҶ’- [ ] State management is correct (Zustand transient vs global)
   279вҶ’- [ ] No forbidden patterns per PROJECT.md
   280вҶ’
   281вҶ’**Tools:**
   282вҶ’- `madge` вҖ” Dependency graph analysis
   283вҶ’- AST analysis for pattern detection
   284вҶ’- Custom architecture validators
   285вҶ’

   288вҶ’### 4.5 ENGINEER Agent
   289вҶ’
   290вҶ’**Role:** The implementer. Writes production-grade code.
   291вҶ’
   292вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   293вҶ’- Implement features per specification
   294вҶ’- Write comprehensive tests
   295вҶ’- Follow coding standards strictly
   296вҶ’- Handle errors properly
   297вҶ’- Document complex logic
   298вҶ’
   299вҶ’**Quality Gates:**
   300вҶ’- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (zero errors)
   301вҶ’- [ ] `pnpm test --run` passes (all tests)
   302вҶ’- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
   303вҶ’- [ ] No `any` types
   304вҶ’- [ ] JSDoc on exported interfaces
   305вҶ’- [ ] No `console.log` in production code
   306вҶ’
   307вҶ’**Standards:**
   308вҶ’```typescript
   309вҶ’// GOOD: Strict typing, clear naming
   310вҶ’function calculateMileage(trainId: string, days: number): number {
   311вҶ’  // ...
   312вҶ’}
   313вҶ’
   314вҶ’// BAD: Any type, unclear
   315вҶ’function calc(x: any, y: any) {
   316вҶ’  // ...
   317вҶ’}
   318вҶ’```
   319вҶ’
   320вҶ’---
   321вҶ’
   322вҶ’### 4.6 DESIGNER Agent
   323вҶ’
   324вҶ’**Role:** The visual craftsman. Ensures Pentagram-level design quality.
   325вҶ’
   326вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   327вҶ’- Define and enforce visual hierarchy
   328вҶ’- Ensure consistent color palette (OKLCH)
   329вҶ’- Verify typography and spacing
   330вҶ’- Apply cognitive psychology principles
   331вҶ’- Ensure accessibility compliance
   332вҶ’
   333вҶ’**Checks:**
   334вҶ’- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear (F-pattern, Z-pattern)
   335вҶ’- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
   336вҶ’- [ ] Consistent spacing (8px grid)
   337вҶ’- [ ] Typography scale is harmonious
   338вҶ’- [ ] Interactive elements have feedback states
   339вҶ’- [ ] Cognitive load is minimized (7Вұ2 rule)
   340вҶ’
   341вҶ’**Principles:**
   342вҶ’- **Pre-attentive processing:** Use color/size for instant recognition
   343вҶ’- **Gestalt:** Group related elements, separate unrelated
   344вҶ’- **Hick's Law:** Minimize choices at each step
   345вҶ’- **Fitts's Law:** Important targets should be large and close
   346вҶ’
   347вҶ’---
   348вҶ’
   349вҶ’### 4.7 COPYWRITER Agent
   350вҶ’
   351вҶ’**Role:** The wordsmith. Ensures professional, clear communication.
   352вҶ’
   353вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   354вҶ’- Review all user-facing text
   355вҶ’- Ensure consistent terminology
   356вҶ’- Maintain professional tone
   357вҶ’- Check for typos and grammar
   358вҶ’- Verify i18n-readiness
   359вҶ’
   360вҶ’**Checks:**
   361вҶ’- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors
   362вҶ’- [ ] Terminology is consistent throughout
   363вҶ’- [ ] Tone is professional (not casual, not stuffy)
   364вҶ’- [ ] Labels are clear and concise
   365вҶ’- [ ] Error messages are helpful
   366вҶ’- [ ] No placeholder text ("Lorem ipsum", "TODO")
   367вҶ’- [ ] No jargon without explanation
   368вҶ’
   369вҶ’**Style Guide:**
   370вҶ’```
   371вҶ’вң“ "Save changes" (clear action)
   372вҶ’вң— "Submit" (ambiguous)
   373вҶ’
   374вҶ’вң“ "Unable to load schedule. Please try again."
   375вҶ’вң— "Error 500"
   376вҶ’
   377вҶ’вң“ "Maintenance" (consistent term)
   378вҶ’вң— "Maintenance" / "Service" / "Repair" (inconsistent)
   379вҶ’```

   396вҶ’## Opening Hook (30 seconds)

   420вҶ’### 4.9 PERFORMANCE Agent
   421вҶ’
   422вҶ’**Role:** The speed demon. Ensures Google-scale performance.
   423вҶ’
   424вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   425вҶ’- Benchmark all interactions
   426вҶ’- Profile render performance
   427вҶ’- Detect memory leaks
   428вҶ’- Optimize bundle size
   429вҶ’- Monitor Web Vitals
   430вҶ’
   431вҶ’**Targets:**
   432вҶ’| Metric | Target | Tool |
   433вҶ’|--------|--------|------|
   434вҶ’| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) | Chrome DevTools |
   435вҶ’| Initial load | <3s | Lighthouse |
   436вҶ’| LCP | <2.5s | Web Vitals |
   437вҶ’| INP | <200ms | Web Vitals |

   442вҶ’**Checks:**
   443вҶ’- [ ] No frame drops during animation
   444вҶ’- [ ] No main thread blocking >50ms
   445вҶ’- [ ] Memory stable over time (no leaks)
   446вҶ’- [ ] Bundle is code-split appropriately
   447вҶ’- [ ] Assets are optimized (WebP, compression)
   448вҶ’
   449вҶ’---
   450вҶ’
   451вҶ’### 4.10 VISION Agent
   452вҶ’
   453вҶ’**Role:** The eyes. Sees the application like a human.
   454вҶ’
   455вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   456вҶ’- Capture screenshots of application state
   457вҶ’- Analyze visual output semantically
   458вҶ’- Verify expected elements are visible
   459вҶ’- Detect visual regressions
   460вҶ’- Provide visual evidence for tickets
   461вҶ’
   462вҶ’**Tools:**
   463вҶ’- **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser control
   464вҶ’- **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis
   465вҶ’- **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level comparison
   466вҶ’- **Applitools** вҖ” AI visual testing (optional)
   467вҶ’
   468вҶ’**Verification Flow:**
   469вҶ’```
   470вҶ’1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
   471вҶ’2. Navigate to target URL
   472вҶ’3. Set viewport/zoom as specified
   473вҶ’4. Capture screenshot
   474вҶ’5. Analyze with Vision LLM:
   475вҶ’   - "Describe what you see"
   476вҶ’   - "Are there colored bars visible?"
   477вҶ’   - "Is the canvas blank or populated?"
   478вҶ’6. Compare against visual criteria
   479вҶ’7. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence
   480вҶ’```
   481вҶ’
   482вҶ’---
   483вҶ’
   484вҶ’### 4.11 QA Agent
   485вҶ’
   486вҶ’**Role:** The gatekeeper. Final validation before approval.
   487вҶ’
   488вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   489вҶ’- Run full test suite
   490вҶ’- Verify acceptance criteria
   491вҶ’- Cross-check all agent approvals
   492вҶ’- Make final APPROVED/REJECTED decision
   493вҶ’- Document evidence
   494вҶ’
   495вҶ’**Final Checklist:**
   496вҶ’- [ ] All unit tests pass
   497вҶ’- [ ] All E2E tests pass
   498вҶ’- [ ] ARCHITECT approved
   499вҶ’- [ ] ENGINEER approved
   500вҶ’- [ ] DESIGNER approved
   501вҶ’- [ ] COPYWRITER approved
   502вҶ’- [ ] MARKETING approved
   503вҶ’- [ ] PERFORMANCE approved
   504вҶ’- [ ] VISION approved
   505вҶ’
   506вҶ’---
   507вҶ’
   508вҶ’## 4B. Coordination Agents
   509вҶ’
   510вҶ’### 4B.1 PROJECT_MANAGER Agent
   511вҶ’
   512вҶ’**Role:** The planner. Manages timeline, priorities, and stakeholder communication.
   513вҶ’
   514вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   515вҶ’- Define and track project milestones
   516вҶ’- Prioritize tasks and manage backlog
   517вҶ’- Identify dependencies between tasks
   518вҶ’- Communicate status to stakeholders
   519вҶ’- Manage risks and blockers
   520вҶ’- Ensure deadlines are realistic and met
   521вҶ’
   522вҶ’**Artifacts Managed:**
   523вҶ’- `pharos/insights/roadmap.md` вҖ” Project timeline and milestones
   524вҶ’- `pharos/insights/backlog.md` вҖ” Prioritized task list
   525вҶ’- `pharos/insights/risks.md` вҖ” Risk register
   526вҶ’
   527вҶ’**Key Decisions:**
   528вҶ’- What to work on next
   529вҶ’- When to cut scope vs extend timeline
   530вҶ’- How to communicate delays
   531вҶ’
   532вҶ’**Interaction Pattern:**
   533вҶ’```
   534вҶ’1. Review current state from context.md
   535вҶ’2. Check ticket status across all queues
   536вҶ’3. Update roadmap and priorities
   537вҶ’4. Assign next tasks to ORCHESTRATOR
   538вҶ’5. Communicate blockers to TECH_LEAD
   539вҶ’```
   540вҶ’
   541вҶ’---
   542вҶ’
   543вҶ’### 4B.2 TECH_LEAD Agent
   544вҶ’
   545вҶ’**Role:** The technical authority. Makes architectural decisions and maintains code quality standards.
   546вҶ’
   547вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   548вҶ’- Make final technical decisions when agents disagree
   549вҶ’- Review critical code changes
   550вҶ’- Maintain coding standards document
   551вҶ’- Guide ARCHITECT and ENGINEER on complex issues
   552вҶ’- Balance technical debt vs feature delivery
   553вҶ’- Approve major architectural changes
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’**Artifacts Managed:**
   556вҶ’- `pharos/insights/decisions.md` вҖ” Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
   557вҶ’- `pharos/insights/standards.md` вҖ” Coding standards and conventions
   558вҶ’- `pharos/insights/tech-debt.md` вҖ” Technical debt tracker
   559вҶ’
   560вҶ’**Key Decisions:**
   561вҶ’- Which technology/library to use
   562вҶ’- How to structure complex features
   563вҶ’- When to refactor vs ship
   564вҶ’- How to resolve technical disagreements
   565вҶ’
   566вҶ’**Interaction Pattern:**
   567вҶ’```
   568вҶ’1. Review ARCHITECT proposals
   569вҶ’2. Validate against PROJECT.md constraints
   570вҶ’3. Make ADR if significant decision
   571вҶ’4. Communicate decision to ENGINEER
   572вҶ’5. Update standards if needed
   573вҶ’```
   574вҶ’
   575вҶ’---
   576вҶ’
   577вҶ’### 4B.3 PRODUCT_OWNER Agent
   578вҶ’
   579вҶ’**Role:** The client advocate. Ensures product meets client needs and RFQ requirements.
   580вҶ’
   581вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   582вҶ’- Translate RFQ requirements into acceptance criteria
   583вҶ’- Validate features against client expectations
   584вҶ’- Prioritize features by client value
   585вҶ’- Define "done" from client perspective
   586вҶ’- Review demo narrative and messaging
   587вҶ’- Ensure UX matches client workflow
   588вҶ’
   589вҶ’**Artifacts Managed:**
   590вҶ’- `pharos/insights/requirements.md` вҖ” Interpreted requirements with acceptance criteria
   591вҶ’- `pharos/insights/client-feedback.md` вҖ” Client feedback and concerns
   592вҶ’- `pharos/insights/demo-script.md` вҖ” Demo narrative and talking points
   593вҶ’
   594вҶ’**Key Decisions:**
   595вҶ’- Does this feature meet client needs?
   596вҶ’- What is the priority from client perspective?
   597вҶ’- Is the demo compelling?
   598вҶ’- Does the UX match railway operations workflow?
   599вҶ’
   600вҶ’**Interaction Pattern:**
   601вҶ’```
   602вҶ’1. Review RFQ.md for original requirements
   603вҶ’2. Check UX_FOR_DEMO.md for alignment
   604вҶ’3. Validate ticket acceptance criteria
   605вҶ’4. Review MARKETING messaging for accuracy
   606вҶ’5. Approve/reject from client perspective
   607вҶ’```
   608вҶ’
   609вҶ’**RFQ Alignment Checklist:**
   610вҶ’- [ ] Short-term planning (weekly, monthly) supported
   611вҶ’- [ ] Long-term planning (1-10 years) supported
   612вҶ’- [ ] What-If simulation available
   613вҶ’- [ ] Mileage warnings visible
   614вҶ’- [ ] Resource utilization clear
   615вҶ’- [ ] Single interface (no page navigation)
   616вҶ’
   617вҶ’---
   618вҶ’
   619вҶ’### 4B.4 ORCHESTRATOR Agent (Enhanced)
   620вҶ’
   621вҶ’**Role:** The facilitator. Coordinates agent workflow and maintains context.
   622вҶ’
   623вҶ’**Responsibilities:**
   624вҶ’- Maintain `pharos/insights/context.md` (always current)
   625вҶ’- Route tasks to appropriate agents
   626вҶ’- Facilitate agent communication
   627вҶ’- Resolve process blockers (not technical)
   628вҶ’- Ensure session continuity
   629вҶ’- Log all sessions in session-log.md
   630вҶ’
   631вҶ’**Artifacts Managed:**
   632вҶ’- `pharos/insights/context.md` вҖ” Current project state (READ FIRST)
   633вҶ’- `pharos/insights/session-log.md` вҖ” Chronological session history
   634вҶ’- `pharos/tickets/` вҖ” Ticket routing and status
   635вҶ’
   636вҶ’**Workflow Coordination:**
   637вҶ’
   638вҶ’```
   639вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   640вҶ’в”Ӯ                    ORCHESTRATOR WORKFLOW                            в”Ӯ
   641вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
   642вҶ’в”Ӯ  1. PROJECT_MANAGER defines priorities                              в”Ӯ
   643вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   644вҶ’в”Ӯ  2. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns to RESEARCHER              в”Ӯ
   645вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   646вҶ’в”Ӯ  3. RESEARCHER investigates, reports to TECH_LEAD                  в”Ӯ
   647вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   648вҶ’в”Ӯ  4. TECH_LEAD approves approach, ORCHESTRATOR assigns ENGINEER     в”Ӯ
   649вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   650вҶ’в”Ӯ  5. ENGINEER implements, creates ticket                             в”Ӯ
   651вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   652вҶ’в”Ӯ  6. ORCHESTRATOR routes ticket to relevant agents                   в”Ӯ
   653вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   654вҶ’в”Ӯ  7. All agents review in parallel                                   в”Ӯ
   655вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   656вҶ’в”Ӯ  8. QA makes final decision                                         в”Ӯ
   657вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   658вҶ’в”Ӯ  9. PRODUCT_OWNER validates client value                            в”Ӯ
   659вҶ’в”Ӯ           вҶ“                                                         в”Ӯ
   660вҶ’в”Ӯ  10. ORCHESTRATOR updates context, logs session                     в”Ӯ
   661вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   662вҶ’```

   818вҶ’```bash

   880вҶ’    "requiredGates": ["architect", "engineer", "designer", "performance", "vision"],
   881вҶ’    "visualCriteria": {
   882вҶ’      "minVisibleElements": 100,
   883вҶ’      "expectedColors": ["#3ddc84", "#ff6b35", "#4ecdc4"],
   884вҶ’      "canvasNotUniform": true
   885вҶ’    },
   886вҶ’    "performanceCriteria": {
   887вҶ’      "maxFrameTime": 16,
   888вҶ’      "target60fps": true
   889вҶ’    }
   890вҶ’  }
   891вҶ’}
   892вҶ’```
   893вҶ’
   894вҶ’---
   895вҶ’
   896вҶ’## 8. Workflow Examples
   897вҶ’
   898вҶ’### 8.1 Implementing a New Feature
   899вҶ’
   900вҶ’```
   901вҶ’1. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns task to RESEARCHER
   902вҶ’2. RESEARCHER investigates best approach, updates knowledge base
   903вҶ’3. ORCHESTRATOR assigns implementation to ENGINEER
   904вҶ’4. ENGINEER implements feature, writes tests
   905вҶ’5. ENGINEER creates ticket in pending/
   906вҶ’6. All agents review in parallel:
   907вҶ’   - ARCHITECT checks structure
   908вҶ’   - DESIGNER checks visuals
   909вҶ’   - COPYWRITER checks text (if any)
   910вҶ’   - PERFORMANCE benchmarks
   911вҶ’   - VISION captures and analyzes screenshots
   912вҶ’7. QA makes final decision
   913вҶ’8. If APPROVED вҶ’ merge
   914вҶ’9. If REJECTED вҶ’ ENGINEER fixes, resubmits
   915вҶ’```
   916вҶ’
   917вҶ’### 8.2 Handling a Bug Report
   918вҶ’
   919вҶ’```
   920вҶ’1. ORCHESTRATOR creates investigation ticket
   921вҶ’2. VISION captures current state (screenshot)
   922вҶ’3. RESEARCHER investigates root cause
   923вҶ’4. ARCHITECT determines if structural issue
   924вҶ’5. ENGINEER implements fix
   925вҶ’6. VISION verifies fix visually
   926вҶ’7. QA validates all tests pass
   927вҶ’8. APPROVED вҶ’ merge
   928вҶ’```
   929вҶ’
   930вҶ’---
   931вҶ’
   932вҶ’## 9. Success Metrics
   933вҶ’
   934вҶ’PHAROS is successful when:
   935вҶ’
   936вҶ’| Metric | Target | Measurement |
   937вҶ’|--------|--------|-------------|
   938вҶ’| False positives | 0 | Features marked "done" that don't work |
   939вҶ’| Visual bugs | 0 | Verified by VISION agent |
   940вҶ’| Context retention | 100% | No repeated mistakes across sessions |
   941вҶ’| Quality consistency | 100% | All output meets standards |
   942вҶ’| Iteration speed | <2 cycles | Tickets approved within 2 review cycles |
   943вҶ’
   944вҶ’---
   945вҶ’
   946вҶ’## 10. Sources & References
   947вҶ’
   948вҶ’### Multi-Agent Frameworks
   949вҶ’- [Top 7 Frameworks for Building AI Agents in 2025](https://www.analyticsvidhya.com/blog/2024/07/ai-agent-frameworks/)
   950вҶ’- [LangGraph vs AutoGen vs CrewAI Comparison](https://latenode.com/blog/platform-comparisons-alternatives/automation-platform-comparisons/langgraph-vs-autogen-vs-crewai-complete-ai-agent-framework-comparison-architecture-analysis-2025)
   951вҶ’- [Comparing Open-Source AI Agent Frameworks - Langfuse](https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-03-19-ai-agent-comparison)
   952вҶ’
   953вҶ’### Knowledge & RAG
   954вҶ’- [Microsoft GraphRAG GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)
   955вҶ’- [GraphRAG: Unlocking LLM Discovery](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/graphrag-unlocking-llm-discovery-on-narrative-private-data/)
   956вҶ’- [LlamaIndex - Agentic Document Workflows](https://www.llamaindex.ai/blog/introducing-agentic-document-workflows)
   957вҶ’
   958вҶ’### Vector Databases
   959вҶ’- [Vector Database Comparison 2025](https://liquidmetal.ai/casesAndBlogs/vector-comparison/)
   960вҶ’- [Best Vector Database For RAG 2025](https://digitaloneagency.com.au/best-vector-database-for-rag-in-2025-pinecone-vs-weaviate-vs-qdrant-vs-milvus-vs-chroma/)
   961вҶ’
   962вҶ’### Visual Testing
   963вҶ’- [AI-Powered Visual Testing in Playwright](https://testrig.medium.com/ai-powered-visual-testing-in-playwright-from-pixels-to-perception-dd3ee49911d5)
   964вҶ’- [Playwright MCP Integration](https://kailash-pathak.medium.com/modern-test-automation-with-ai-llm-and-playwright-mcp-model-context-protocol-0c311292c7fb)
   965вҶ’- [Using Vision LLMs For UI Testing](https://courses.cs.washington.edu/courses/cse503/25wi/final-reports/Using%20Vision%20LLMs%20For%20UI%20Testing.pdf)
   966вҶ’
   967вҶ’### Performance Monitoring
   968вҶ’- [Best Frontend Performance Monitoring Tools 2025](https://cssauthor.com/best-frontend-performance-monitoring-tools/)
   969вҶ’- [Best Real User Monitoring Tools 2025](https://www.debugbear.com/software/best-real-user-monitoring-tools)
   970вҶ’
   971вҶ’### MCP & Claude
   972вҶ’- [Model Context Protocol Introduction](https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol)
   973вҶ’- [Advanced Tool Use on Claude](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/advanced-tool-use)
   974вҶ’- [Claude Code MCP Documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/mcp)
   975вҶ’
   976вҶ’---
   977вҶ’
   978вҶ’## 11. Getting Started
   979вҶ’
   980вҶ’### 11.1 First-Time Setup
   981вҶ’
   982вҶ’```bash
   983вҶ’# 1. Ensure you're on the framework branch
   984вҶ’git checkout framework/quality-system
   985вҶ’
   986вҶ’# 2. Read the context
   987вҶ’cat pharos/insights/context.md
   988вҶ’
   989вҶ’# 3. Check project status
   990вҶ’./init.sh
   991вҶ’
   992вҶ’# 4. Review any pending tickets
   993вҶ’ls pharos/tickets/pending/
   994вҶ’```
   995вҶ’
   996вҶ’### 11.2 As an Agent
   997вҶ’
   998вҶ’1. **Identify your role** from Section 4
   999вҶ’2. **Read the context** from `pharos/insights/context.md`
  1000вҶ’3. **Check for tickets** in your domain
  1001вҶ’4. **Perform your checks** per your agent specification
  1002вҶ’5. **Update the ticket** with your decision and evidence
  1003вҶ’6. **Log your session** in `pharos/insights/session-log.md`
  1004вҶ’
  1005вҶ’### 11.3 Agent Invocation Prompt
  1006вҶ’
  1007вҶ’```markdown
  1008вҶ’You are the PHAROS [AGENT_NAME] Agent.
  1009вҶ’
  1010вҶ’Read the current context from: pharos/insights/context.md
  1011вҶ’Check for tickets requiring your review in: pharos/tickets/pending/
  1012вҶ’
  1013вҶ’Your responsibilities:
  1014вҶ’[List from Section 4]
  1015вҶ’
  1016вҶ’Your checks:
  1017вҶ’[Checklist from Section 4]
  1018вҶ’
  1019вҶ’Update any reviewed tickets with:
  1020вҶ’- status: APPROVED or REJECTED
  1021вҶ’- feedback: Specific, actionable feedback
  1022вҶ’- evidence: Screenshots, logs, or other proof
  1023вҶ’
  1024вҶ’Log your session in: pharos/insights/session-log.md
  1025вҶ’```
  1026вҶ’
  1027вҶ’---
  1028вҶ’
  1029вҶ’*PHAROS: Guiding development toward excellence, one agent at a time.*
  1030вҶ’

# PHAROS Framework

> **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**
>
> *The Lighthouse of Excellence* вҖ” A complete multi-agent development and quality assurance framework that operates like a world-class engineering organization (Google + Palantir + Pentagram standards).

---

## 1. Executive Summary

### 1.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is not just a QA system. It is a **complete multi-agent framework** that simulates how a world-class engineering organization operates:

**Leadership & Coordination:**
- **Project Manager** вҖ” Timeline, priorities, dependencies, stakeholder communication
- **Tech Lead** вҖ” Technical decisions, code standards, architectural guidance
- **Product Owner** вҖ” Requirements alignment, client needs, acceptance criteria

**Development Team:**
- **Researcher** вҖ” Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions
- **Architect** вҖ” System design, dependencies, patterns, structural integrity
- **Engineer** вҖ” Implementation, tests, production-grade code
- **Designer** вҖ” UX/UI, visual hierarchy, accessibility, cognitive psychology
- **Copywriter** вҖ” Wording, terminology, tone, clarity
- **Marketing** вҖ” Positioning, messaging, demo narrative, value propositions

**Quality Assurance:**
- **Performance Engineer** вҖ” Speed, memory, optimization, benchmarks
- **Vision Agent** вҖ” Visual verification, screenshot analysis
- **QA Agent** вҖ” Final validation, acceptance testing

**Orchestration:**
- **Orchestrator** вҖ” Process facilitation, context maintenance, agent coordination

### 1.2 The Problem We Solve

Current AI development has critical gaps:

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| AI marks features "done" without verification | Tests pass, screen is blue |
| No systematic quality gates | Inconsistent output quality |
| Context lost between sessions | Repeated mistakes |
| No specialized expertise | Jack of all trades, master of none |
| No research capability | Outdated solutions |
| No visual verification | Blind to actual output |

### 1.3 The PHAROS Solution

A multi-agent system where **specialized agents handle their domain**, communicate via **persistent tickets**, and **nothing ships without unanimous approval**.

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                           PHAROS AGENT NETWORK                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ӮRESEARCHERв”Ӯ в”ӮARCHITECT в”Ӯ в”Ӯ ENGINEER в”Ӯ в”Ӯ DESIGNER в”Ӯ в”ӮCOPYWRITERв”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в”Ӯ
