в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ӮMARKETINGв”Ӯ в”ӮPERFORMANCв”Ӯ в”Ӯ  VISION  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ    QA    в”Ӯ в”ӮORCHESTRATв”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ    TICKET SYSTEM      в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ  (Persistent State)   в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

---

## 2. Business Context

### 2.1 The Project

**MMS Fractal Planner** вҖ” A response to an RFQ from a railway operator for a Mileage Management System.

**Critical Context:**
- Initial presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- This demo must **recover client confidence**
- Must handle **128 trains Г— 12 years** of scheduling
- Must deliver **"Google Maps of Time"** experience
- Standards: **Palantir** (data) + **Google** (engineering) + **Pentagram** (design)

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The previous development approach failed because:
- AI had no "eyes" to see actual output
- No quality gates across design, copy, performance
- No research capability for latest technologies
- No persistent context between sessions
- No specialization вҖ” one agent doing everything poorly

---

## 3. Technology Stack

### 3.1 Multi-Agent Orchestration

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph)** | Agent orchestration | Graph-based workflows, stateful, fault-tolerant |
| **[CrewAI](https://github.com/joaomdmoura/crewai)** | Role-based agents | Natural role assignment, collaborative |
| **[LangFuse](https://langfuse.com/)** | Agent observability | Trace all agent interactions, debug issues |

**Decision:** Use **LangGraph** for core orchestration (complex state management) + **CrewAI patterns** for role definitions.

### 3.2 Knowledge & Memory

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[GraphRAG (Microsoft)](https://github.com/microsoft/graphrag)** | Knowledge graph + RAG | Understands relationships, not just similarity |
| **[Neo4j](https://neo4j.com/)** | Graph database | Industry standard, integrates with GraphRAG |
| **[Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/)** | Vector database | Fast, complex filters, Rust performance |
| **[LlamaIndex](https://www.llamaindex.ai/)** | Document processing | Best-in-class RAG, agentic workflows |

**Architecture:**
```
Documents вҶ’ LlamaIndex вҶ’ GraphRAG вҶ’ Neo4j (relationships)
                                  вҶ’ Qdrant (embeddings)
```

### 3.3 Visual Testing & Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Playwright MCP](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-tools)** | Browser automation | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Claude Vision** | Screenshot analysis | Semantic understanding of UI |
| **[Applitools](https://applitools.com/)** | Visual AI testing | Enterprise-grade visual regression |
| **[Percy](https://percy.io/)** | Visual review | CI/CD integration |
| **[pixelmatch](https://github.com/mapbox/pixelmatch)** | Pixel comparison | Fast baseline comparison |

### 3.4 Performance Monitoring

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Lighthouse CI](https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci)** | Performance audits | Google's standard |
| **[Web Vitals](https://web.dev/vitals/)** | Core metrics | LCP, CLS, INP |
| **[Sentry](https://sentry.io/)** | Error tracking + RUM | Full observability |
| **[Grafana Faro](https://grafana.com/oss/faro/)** | Frontend observability | Real user monitoring |

### 3.5 Code Quality

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **TypeScript Strict** | Type safety | No `any`, full inference |
| **[ESLint](https://eslint.org/)** | Linting | Configurable, extensible |
| **[Vitest](https://vitest.dev/)** | Unit testing | Fast, Vite-native |
| **[Playwright](https://playwright.dev/)** | E2E testing | Best cross-browser support |
| **[madge](https://github.com/pahen/madge)** | Dependency analysis | Detect circular deps |

### 3.6 Copy & Marketing

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **Claude API** | Copywriting | Best reasoning, brand voice |
| **[Vale](https://vale.sh/)** | Prose linting | Style guide enforcement |
| **[LanguageTool](https://languagetool.org/)** | Grammar checking | Multi-language support |

### 3.7 Design Verification

| Technology | Purpose | Why Chosen |
|------------|---------|------------|
| **[Storybook](https://storybook.js.org/)** | Component docs | Visual component library |
| **[Chromatic](https://www.chromatic.com/)** | Visual testing | Storybook integration |
| **OKLCH Color Space** | Color system | Perceptually uniform |
| **[Contrast Ratio](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/)** | Accessibility | WCAG compliance |

---

## 4. The PHAROS Agents

### 4.1 Agent Overview

| Agent | Domain | Responsibility | Key Tools |
|-------|--------|----------------|-----------|
| **ORCHESTRATOR** | Coordination | Manages workflow, context, priorities | LangGraph, Tickets |
| **RESEARCHER** | Knowledge | Finds latest tech, reads docs, investigates | WebSearch, LlamaIndex |
| **ARCHITECT** | Structure | System design, dependencies, patterns | madge, ADRs |
| **ENGINEER** | Code | Implementation, tests, quality | TypeScript, Vitest |
| **DESIGNER** | Visual | UX/UI, layout, colors, hierarchy | Figma, Storybook |
| **COPYWRITER** | Text | Wording, clarity, tone, consistency | Vale, Claude |
| **MARKETING** | Positioning | Messaging, value props, demo narrative | Claude, templates |
| **PERFORMANCE** | Speed | Benchmarks, optimization, profiling | Lighthouse, DevTools |
| **VISION** | Verification | Visual testing, screenshot analysis | Playwright MCP, Claude Vision |
| **QA** | Validation | Integration, E2E, acceptance | Playwright, Vitest |

---

### 4.2 ORCHESTRATOR Agent

**Role:** The conductor of the orchestra. Maintains global context, assigns tasks, resolves conflicts.

**Responsibilities:**
- Read project state from `pharos/insights/`
- Assign tasks to appropriate agents
- Track ticket status and priorities
- Maintain session continuity
- Resolve inter-agent conflicts

**Context Maintained:**
```yaml
project:
  name: MMS Fractal Planner
  goal: Recover client confidence with impressive demo
  standards: [Palantir, Google, Pentagram]

current_state:
  branch: main (demo) | framework/quality-system (PHAROS)
  phase: current development phase
  blockers: list of blocking issues

environment:
  date: 2025-12-13
  os: macOS Darwin 25.1.0
```

---

### 4.3 RESEARCHER Agent

**Role:** The knowledge seeker. Investigates technologies, reads documentation, finds solutions.

**Responsibilities:**
- Research latest technologies and best practices
- Read and summarize documentation
- Find solutions to technical problems
- Update knowledge base with findings
- Validate that solutions are current (not outdated)

**Tools:**
- `WebSearch` вҖ” Find latest information
- `WebFetch` вҖ” Read documentation pages
- `LlamaIndex` вҖ” Process and index documents
- `GraphRAG` вҖ” Build knowledge relationships

**Output:**
```markdown
# Research: [Topic]

## Summary
[Key findings in 2-3 sentences]

## Recommendations
1. Use X because Y
2. Avoid Z because W

## Sources
- [Link 1](url) - accessed 2025-12-13
- [Link 2](url) - accessed 2025-12-13

## Caveats
- This may change by [date]
- Alternative: [option]
```

---

### 4.4 ARCHITECT Agent

**Role:** The structural engineer. Ensures system integrity and scalability.

**Responsibilities:**
- Design system architecture
- Review code structure and dependencies
- Enforce architectural constraints (no Ghost Divs, SoA, etc.)
- Create and maintain ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
- Detect and prevent architectural drift

**Checks:**
- [ ] No circular dependencies (`madge --circular`)
- [ ] Proper separation of concerns
- [ ] Data structures are optimal (SoA for large data)
- [ ] State management is correct (Zustand transient vs global)
- [ ] No forbidden patterns per PROJECT.md

**Tools:**
- `madge` вҖ” Dependency graph analysis
- AST analysis for pattern detection
- Custom architecture validators

---

### 4.5 ENGINEER Agent

**Role:** The implementer. Writes production-grade code.

**Responsibilities:**
- Implement features per specification
- Write comprehensive tests
- Follow coding standards strictly
- Handle errors properly
- Document complex logic

**Quality Gates:**
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (zero errors)
- [ ] `pnpm test --run` passes (all tests)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] JSDoc on exported interfaces
- [ ] No `console.log` in production code

**Standards:**
```typescript
// GOOD: Strict typing, clear naming
function calculateMileage(trainId: string, days: number): number {
  // ...
}

// BAD: Any type, unclear
function calc(x: any, y: any) {
  // ...
}
```

---

### 4.6 DESIGNER Agent

**Role:** The visual craftsman. Ensures Pentagram-level design quality.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define and enforce visual hierarchy
- Ensure consistent color palette (OKLCH)
- Verify typography and spacing
- Apply cognitive psychology principles
- Ensure accessibility compliance

**Checks:**
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear (F-pattern, Z-pattern)
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
- [ ] Consistent spacing (8px grid)
- [ ] Typography scale is harmonious
- [ ] Interactive elements have feedback states
- [ ] Cognitive load is minimized (7Вұ2 rule)

**Principles:**
- **Pre-attentive processing:** Use color/size for instant recognition
- **Gestalt:** Group related elements, separate unrelated
- **Hick's Law:** Minimize choices at each step
- **Fitts's Law:** Important targets should be large and close

---

### 4.7 COPYWRITER Agent

**Role:** The wordsmith. Ensures professional, clear communication.

**Responsibilities:**
- Review all user-facing text
- Ensure consistent terminology
- Maintain professional tone
- Check for typos and grammar
- Verify i18n-readiness

**Checks:**
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors
- [ ] Terminology is consistent throughout
- [ ] Tone is professional (not casual, not stuffy)
- [ ] Labels are clear and concise
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] No placeholder text ("Lorem ipsum", "TODO")
- [ ] No jargon without explanation

**Style Guide:**
```
вң“ "Save changes" (clear action)
вң— "Submit" (ambiguous)

вң“ "Unable to load schedule. Please try again."
вң— "Error 500"

вң“ "Maintenance" (consistent term)
вң— "Maintenance" / "Service" / "Repair" (inconsistent)
```

---

### 4.8 MARKETING Agent

**Role:** The storyteller. Ensures compelling positioning and messaging.

**Responsibilities:**
- Define value propositions
- Craft demo narrative
- Ensure messaging aligns with client needs
- Review all client-facing materials
- Position features for maximum impact

**Demo Narrative Structure:**
```markdown
## Opening Hook (30 seconds)
"Imagine seeing your entire 12-year maintenance plan in one view..."

## Problem Statement
"Currently, your team uses Excel and IBM Maximo separately..."

## Solution Demo
"With Fractal Planner, you can [demonstrate key feature]..."

## Proof Points
"Watch as the system automatically [impressive capability]..."

## Call to Action
"This is the 'Single Pane of Glass' you requested..."
```

**Key Messages:**
- "Google Maps of Time" вҖ” Intuitive navigation metaphor
- "Single Pane of Glass" вҖ” Client's own terminology
- "What-If Analysis" вҖ” RFQ requirement, now visual
- "Zero Latency" вҖ” Technical differentiator

---

### 4.9 PERFORMANCE Agent

**Role:** The speed demon. Ensures Google-scale performance.

**Responsibilities:**
- Benchmark all interactions
- Profile render performance
- Detect memory leaks
- Optimize bundle size
- Monitor Web Vitals

**Targets:**
| Metric | Target | Tool |
|--------|--------|------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) | Chrome DevTools |
| Initial load | <3s | Lighthouse |
| LCP | <2.5s | Web Vitals |
| INP | <200ms | Web Vitals |
| CLS | <0.1 | Web Vitals |
| Bundle size | <500KB gzipped | Vite analyzer |
| Memory | No GC pauses | Performance panel |

**Checks:**
- [ ] No frame drops during animation
- [ ] No main thread blocking >50ms
- [ ] Memory stable over time (no leaks)
- [ ] Bundle is code-split appropriately
- [ ] Assets are optimized (WebP, compression)

---

### 4.10 VISION Agent

**Role:** The eyes. Sees the application like a human.

**Responsibilities:**
- Capture screenshots of application state
- Analyze visual output semantically
- Verify expected elements are visible
- Detect visual regressions
- Provide visual evidence for tickets

**Tools:**
- **Playwright MCP** вҖ” Browser control
- **Claude Vision** вҖ” Semantic analysis
- **pixelmatch** вҖ” Pixel-level comparison
- **Applitools** вҖ” AI visual testing (optional)

**Verification Flow:**
```
1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
2. Navigate to target URL
3. Set viewport/zoom as specified
4. Capture screenshot
5. Analyze with Vision LLM:
   - "Describe what you see"
   - "Are there colored bars visible?"
   - "Is the canvas blank or populated?"
6. Compare against visual criteria
7. Report PASS/FAIL with evidence
```

**Responsibilities:**

- Verify acceptance criteria
- Cross-check all agent approvals
- Make final APPROVED/REJECTED decision

## 4B. Coordination Agents

### 4B.1 PROJECT_MANAGER Agent

**Role:** The planner. Manages timeline, priorities, and stakeholder communication.

- Prioritize tasks and manage backlog
- Identify dependencies between tasks
- Communicate status to stakeholders
- Manage risks and blockers
- Ensure deadlines are realistic and met

- `pharos/insights/backlog.md` вҖ” Prioritized task list

**Key Decisions:**
- What to work on next
- When to cut scope vs extend timeline
- How to communicate delays

**Interaction Pattern:**
```
1. Review current state from context.md
2. Check ticket status across all queues
3. Update roadmap and priorities
4. Assign next tasks to ORCHESTRATOR
5. Communicate blockers to TECH_LEAD
```

---

### 4B.2 TECH_LEAD Agent

**Role:** The technical authority. Makes architectural decisions and maintains code quality standards.

**Responsibilities:**
- Make final technical decisions when agents disagree
- Review critical code changes
- Maintain coding standards document
- Guide ARCHITECT and ENGINEER on complex issues
- Balance technical debt vs feature delivery
- Approve major architectural changes

**Artifacts Managed:**

- `pharos/insights/standards.md` вҖ” Coding standards and conventions

**Key Decisions:**

- How to structure complex features

- How to resolve technical disagreements

2. Validate against PROJECT.md constraints

5. Update standards if needed
```

### 4B.3 PRODUCT_OWNER Agent

**Role:** The client advocate. Ensures product meets client needs and RFQ requirements.

**Responsibilities:**

- Define "done" from client perspective

- `pharos/insights/client-feedback.md` вҖ” Client feedback and concerns

**Key Decisions:**

- Is the demo compelling?
- Does the UX match railway operations workflow?

**Interaction Pattern:**
```

2. Check UX_FOR_DEMO.md for alignment

```

## 7. Integration Points

### 7.1 Enhanced init.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "================================================"
echo "  PHAROS Framework - Project Status Check"
echo "================================================"
echo ""
echo "Date: $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
echo "OS: $(uname -s) $(uname -r)"
echo "Node: $(node -v)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo ""

# Check for rejected tickets
REJECTED=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вқҢ CRITICAL: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) require attention"
  ls pharos/tickets/rejected/
  echo ""
fi

# Check for pending tickets
PENDING=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/pending/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "вҸі PENDING: $PENDING ticket(s) awaiting review"
fi

# Check for in-review tickets
INREVIEW=$(ls -1 pharos/tickets/in-review/*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$INREVIEW" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "рҹ”Қ IN REVIEW: $INREVIEW ticket(s) being reviewed"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Quality Gates ==="
echo "Running: pnpm typecheck..."
pnpm typecheck || { echo "вқҢ TypeCheck FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… TypeCheck passed"

echo "Running: pnpm lint..."
pnpm lint || { echo "вқҢ Lint FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Lint passed"

echo "Running: pnpm test --run..."
pnpm test --run || { echo "вқҢ Tests FAILED"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Tests passed"

echo ""
echo "=== All Checks Passed ==="
echo "Ready to proceed with development."
```

## 8. Workflow Examples

### 8.1 Implementing a New Feature

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR reads context, assigns task to RESEARCHER
2. RESEARCHER investigates best approach, updates knowledge base
3. ORCHESTRATOR assigns implementation to ENGINEER
4. ENGINEER implements feature, writes tests
5. ENGINEER creates ticket in pending/
6. All agents review in parallel:
   - ARCHITECT checks structure
   - DESIGNER checks visuals
   - COPYWRITER checks text (if any)
   - PERFORMANCE benchmarks
   - VISION captures and analyzes screenshots
7. QA makes final decision
8. If APPROVED вҶ’ merge
9. If REJECTED вҶ’ ENGINEER fixes, resubmits
```

### 8.2 Handling a Bug Report

```
1. ORCHESTRATOR creates investigation ticket
2. VISION captures current state (screenshot)
3. RESEARCHER investigates root cause
4. ARCHITECT determines if structural issue
5. ENGINEER implements fix
6. VISION verifies fix visually
7. QA validates all tests pass
8. APPROVED вҶ’ merge
```

# PHAROS Framework - AI Development Instructions

## 1. The Prime Directive

You are developing **PHAROS**, a multi-agent quality framework that simulates a world-class engineering organization. This branch (`framework/quality-system`) is dedicated to completing the framework itself.

**Read `pharos/insights/context.md` FIRST** вҖ” It contains the current project state and must be read before any action.

---

## 2. Understanding PHAROS

### 2.1 What PHAROS Is

PHAROS (**P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S**) is a complete multi-agent development framework with:

- **13 Specialized Agents** across Leadership, Development, QA, and Orchestration
- **Ticket-Based Communication** for persistent, async collaboration
- **Quality Gates** that ALL must pass before "done"
- **Insight System** for context preservation across sessions
- **Visual Verification** via Playwright MCP and Claude Vision

### 2.2 Why PHAROS Exists

The parent project (MMS Fractal Planner) had a critical problem: **AI marked features "done" but they didn't work visually** ("tests pass, screen is blue"). PHAROS solves this by:

1. Multiple specialized agents (not one doing everything)
2. VISION agent that sees the screen via Playwright MCP
3. All quality gates must pass вҖ” unanimous approval required
4. Persistent context вҖ” no repeated mistakes

### 2.3 The Parent Project

PHAROS will be applied to **MMS Fractal Planner**, an RFQ response for a railway operator:
- "Google Maps of Time" вҖ” seamless zoom from 12-year strategic to daily operations
- 128 trains, 50,000+ schedule blocks
- Standards: Palantir (data) + Google (engineering) + Pentagram (design)
- Goal: Recover client confidence after suboptimal initial presentation

---

## 3. The Harness Protocol

Before any work, execute this loop:

1. **OBSERVE:** Run `./init.sh` to see current state and progress
2. **PLAN:** Read `features.json`. Select the next `pending` task
3. **THINK:** Write a brief plan вҖ” what files to create/modify
4. **ACT:** Implement the feature
5. **VERIFY:** Ensure all files are syntactically correct and consistent
6. **DOCUMENT:** Update `pharos-progress.txt` and `features.json`

---

## 4. Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pharos/PHAROS.md` | **SSOT** вҖ” Complete framework specification |
| `pharos/insights/context.md` | Current state вҖ” **READ FIRST** |
| `pharos/insights/session-log.md` | Chronological session history |
| `pharos/insights/decisions.md` | Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) |
| `pharos/insights/known-issues.md` | Documented problems and solutions |
| `features.json` | Task tracking for PHAROS completion |
| `pharos-progress.txt` | Session log and next steps |

---

## 5. Current Phase: Framework Completion

### 5.1 What's Done
- Created PHAROS.md with complete agent specifications (13 agents)
- Researched technology stack (LangGraph, Qdrant, GraphRAG, Playwright MCP)
- Created ticket system schema and templates
- Created insights directory structure
- Documented ADRs and known issues

### 5.2 What's Needed Next

1. **Agent Instruction Files** (`pharos/agents/*.md`)
   - One file per agent with specific prompts and checklists
   - Must be copy-paste ready for agent invocation

2. **Integration Scripts**
   - Update init.sh for PHAROS-aware checks
   - Create ticket management CLI helpers

3. **Workflow Automation**
   - Create sample ticket workflow
   - Test full lifecycle: pending вҶ’ in-review вҶ’ approved

4. **Documentation**
   - Quick-start guide
   - Agent invocation examples
   - Troubleshooting guide

---

## 6. Directory Structure

```
pharos/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ PHAROS.md           # Complete framework specification
в”ңв”Җв”Җ agents/             # Per-agent instruction files
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ orchestrator.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ researcher.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ architect.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ engineer.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ designer.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ copywriter.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ marketing.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ performance.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ vision.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ qa.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ project-manager.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ tech-lead.md
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ product-owner.md
в”ңв”Җв”Җ insights/           # Persistent knowledge
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ context.md      # READ FIRST - current state
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ session-log.md  # Session history
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ decisions.md    # ADRs
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ known-issues.md # Problems and solutions
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ research/       # Research by topic
в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/          # Reusable templates
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ticket.json     # Ticket schema
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tickets/            # Active tickets
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pending/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ in-review/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ approved/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ rejected/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ archive/
в””в”Җв”Җ gates/              # Quality gate scripts
```

---

## 7. Agent Summary

### Leadership & Coordination
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `PROJECT_MANAGER` | Timeline, priorities, dependencies |
| `TECH_LEAD` | Technical decisions, code review |
| `PRODUCT_OWNER` | Client requirements, acceptance |
| `ORCHESTRATOR` | Process facilitation, context |

### Development Team
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `RESEARCHER` | Technology investigation, documentation |
| `ARCHITECT` | System design, patterns |
| `ENGINEER` | Implementation, tests |
| `DESIGNER` | UX/UI, accessibility |
| `COPYWRITER` | Text, terminology |
| `MARKETING` | Positioning, demo narrative |

### Quality Assurance
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `PERFORMANCE` | Speed, memory, benchmarks |
| `VISION` | Visual verification, screenshots |
| `QA` | Final validation, acceptance |

---

## 8. Technology Stack

| Category | Technology | Purpose |
|----------|------------|---------|
| Orchestration | LangGraph | Graph-based agent workflows |
| Knowledge | GraphRAG + Neo4j | Relationship understanding |
| Vectors | Qdrant | Fast similarity search |
| Visual | Playwright MCP | Browser automation |
| Analysis | Claude Vision | Screenshot understanding |
| Regression | pixelmatch | Pixel-level comparison |

---

## 9. Session Protocol

### At Session Start
1. Read `pharos/insights/context.md`
2. Run `./init.sh`
3. Check `pharos-progress.txt` for continuation notes
4. Review pending tasks in `features.json`

### At Session End
1. Update `pharos-progress.txt` with session summary
2. Update `features.json` status
3. Add entry to `pharos/insights/session-log.md`
4. Commit with clear message

---

## 10. Commit Guidelines

```
feat(pharos): implement [component]

- What was added
- Why it matters
```

Do NOT add co-authored-by lines.

---

## 11. Definition of Done

A PHAROS task is done when:
1. All files created are syntactically valid
2. Documentation is clear and consistent
3. Examples are copy-paste ready
4. `features.json` updated to `done`
5. `pharos-progress.txt` updated
6. Session logged in `session-log.md`

---

*PHAROS: Guiding development toward excellence.*

в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ research/       # Research by topic
в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/          # Reusable templates
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ticket.json     # Ticket schema
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tickets/            # Active tickets
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pending/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ in-review/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ approved/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ rejected/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ archive/
в””в”Җв”Җ gates/              # Quality gate scripts
```

---

## 7. Agent Summary

### Leadership & Coordination
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `PROJECT_MANAGER` | Timeline, priorities, dependencies |
| `TECH_LEAD` | Technical decisions, code review |
| `PRODUCT_OWNER` | Client requirements, acceptance |
| `ORCHESTRATOR` | Process facilitation, context |

### Development Team
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `RESEARCHER` | Technology investigation, documentation |
| `ARCHITECT` | System design, patterns |
| `ENGINEER` | Implementation, tests |
| `DESIGNER` | UX/UI, accessibility |
| `COPYWRITER` | Text, terminology |
| `MARKETING` | Positioning, demo narrative |

### Quality Assurance
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `PERFORMANCE` | Speed, memory, benchmarks |
| `VISION` | Visual verification, screenshots |
| `QA` | Final validation, acceptance |

---

## 8. Technology Stack

| Category | Technology | Purpose |
|----------|------------|---------|
| Orchestration | LangGraph | Graph-based agent workflows |
| Knowledge | GraphRAG + Neo4j | Relationship understanding |
| Vectors | Qdrant | Fast similarity search |
| Visual | Playwright MCP | Browser automation |
| Analysis | Claude Vision | Screenshot understanding |
| Regression | pixelmatch | Pixel-level comparison |

---

## 9. Session Protocol

### At Session Start
1. Read `pharos/insights/context.md`
2. Run `./init.sh`
3. Check `pharos-progress.txt` for continuation notes
4. Review pending tasks in `features.json`

### At Session End
1. Update `pharos-progress.txt` with session summary
2. Update `features.json` status
3. Add entry to `pharos/insights/session-log.md`
4. Commit with clear message

---

## 10. Commit Guidelines

```
feat(pharos): implement [component]

- What was added
- Why it matters
```

Do NOT add co-authored-by lines.

---

## 11. Definition of Done

A PHAROS task is done when:
1. All files created are syntactically valid
2. Documentation is clear and consistent
3. Examples are copy-paste ready
4. `features.json` updated to `done`
5. `pharos-progress.txt` updated
6. Session logged in `session-log.md`

---

*PHAROS: Guiding development toward excellence.*

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MMS Fractal Planner - AI Agent Instructions

## 1. The Prime Directive

You are the Senior GeoView Advisor. You do not write "toy code". You write high-performance, memory-safe, engineering-grade TypeScript.

**PROJECT.md is the Single Source of Truth.** If your training data conflicts with PROJECT.md, follow PROJECT.md.

### 1.1 Business Context

This is a **response to an RFQ** from a railway operator. The goal is to build an impressive demo that:
- Recovers client confidence after a suboptimal initial presentation
- Proves technical excellence through fluid, zero-latency interactions
- Addresses all RFQ requirements visually and functionally

### 1.2 The Core Metaphor: "Google Maps of Time"

The app is like Google Maps but for time:
- **Zoom out** вҶ’ See 12 years of strategic planning (heatmaps)
- **Zoom in** вҶ’ See daily operations (Gantt, individual trains)
- **Continuous transition** вҶ’ No page reloads, seamless zoom
- **Bidirectional propagation** вҶ’ Changes at any level ripple up and down

---

## 2. The Harness Protocol

Reference: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

Before writing any code, you must execute this loop:

1. **OBSERVE:** Run `./init.sh` to see the current state and progress log.
2. **PLAN:** Read `features.json`. Select the next `pending` task.
3. **THINK:** Write a brief plan in your scratchpad.
   - _Example:_ "Implementing SoA. I need `Float64Array` for dates. I will avoid `new Date()` inside the render loop."
4. **ACT:** Write the code.
5. **VERIFY:** Run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test --run && pnpm lint` - ALL must pass.
6. **DOCUMENT:** Update `claude-progress.txt` and `features.json`.

**CRITICAL RULE:** ALL tests must pass before ANY feature can be marked as 'done'. No exceptions.

---

## 3. Architecture Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

| Feature | Allowed | Forbidden |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| **Scrolling** | Virtual Scroller (Math-based) | Native DOM Scrolling / Ghost Divs |
| **Data Structures** | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (for massive data) |
| **State** | Zustand (Transient & Global) | Redux, Context API (for high-freq updates) |
| **Rendering** | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM Elements (for >100 items), SVG |
| **Loop** | `requestAnimationFrame` | `setInterval`, `setTimeout` (for anim) |

---

## 4. Key Product Concepts

### 4.1 Zoom Levels (8 Semantic Levels)

| Temporal Scale | Default Visualization | Analysis Focus |
|----------------|----------------------|----------------|
| **12 Years** | Heatmap (saturation) | Total capacity, CAPEX/OPEX |
| **3-5 Years** | Stacked Bars + Trends | Fleet lifecycle patterns |
| **1-2 Years** | Combo Chart | Seasonality, peaks |
| **Quadrimester** | Workload Equalizer | Workload vs capacity |
| **1-3 Months** | Weekly Bars | Resource conflicts |
| **2-4 Weeks** | Simplified Gantt | Track occupancy |
| **1 Week** | Detailed Gantt | Per-train status |
| **1-3 Days** | Capsules + Details | Fitness, km remaining |

### 4.2 Visualization Selector

Like Google Maps' `[Map] [Satellite] [Terrain]`, each zoom level offers multiple chart options:
- User can switch between available visualizations for current zoom level
- Each level has a default (recommended) visualization
- See `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` for full chart options per level

### 4.3 Baseline vs Scenario Mode

| Mode | Description | Editing |
|------|-------------|---------|
| **BASELINE** | Production plan | Restricted |
| **SCENARIO** | Fork for "What-If" | Free editing |

Scenarios allow consequence-free experimentation with compare & apply workflow.

### 4.4 Fractal Propagation

Changes propagate bidirectionally:
- Operational change вҶ’ affects monthly вҶ’ affects yearly вҶ’ affects 12-year
- Strategic change вҶ’ affects yearly вҶ’ affects monthly вҶ’ affects daily

---

## 5. Coding Standards

- **Strict Typing:** No `any`. Use `unknown` or Generics if unsure.
- **JSDoc:** Required for all exported interfaces and complex algorithms (explain _why_, not just _what_).
- **No Comments in Body:** Code should be self-documenting. Use comments only for "Why" or weird math.
- **File Structure:**
  - `src/engine/`: Core logic, Physics, Math, SoA
  - `src/ui/`: React Components (Overlay, Sidebar, Constraint Deck)
  - `src/render/`: PixiJS specific code (Shaders, Layers)
  - `src/services/`: Mock API, future BFF integration

---

## 6. Common Commands

```bash
# Environment
./init.sh             # Check status and architecture compliance
pnpm dev              # Vite dev server (port 5173)

# Quality (ALL must pass before marking done)
pnpm typecheck        # TypeScript strict check
pnpm lint             # ESLint
pnpm test --run       # Vitest unit tests

# E2E Testing
pnpm test:e2e         # Playwright E2E tests

# Development
pnpm storybook        # Component documentation
```

---

## 7. Critical Documentation

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `PROJECT.md` | **SSOT** - Architecture, business context, technical spec |
| `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` | UX specification, demo script, visualization options |
| `docs/RFQ.md` | Original client requirements |
| `docs/DEMO_DATA.md` | Fleet data, maintenance types |
| `features.json` | Task tracking with phases |
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session log & next steps |

---

## 8. Performance Targets

| Metric | Target | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| **Zoom/Pan** | <16ms (60fps) | Zero perceived latency |
| **Initial load** | <3s | First meaningful paint |
| **Drag operations** | 60fps constant | No drops during interaction |
| **Memory** | No GC pauses | Smooth animation |
| **Data capacity** | 50,000+ blocks | 12 years Г— 128 trains |

---

## 9. Commit Guidelines

- Commit after each significant progress
- **NO co-authored-by** in commits
- Clear, descriptive messages
- Update `claude-progress.txt` after major milestones

Example:
```
feat(engine): implement SoA simulation store

- Added TypedArrays for schedule blocks
- Implemented Interval Tree for collision detection
```

---

## 10. Definition of Done

A feature is ONLY done when:

1. вң… Code compiles without errors (`pnpm typecheck`)
2. вң… All unit tests pass (`pnpm test --run`)
3. вң… Linting passes (`pnpm lint`)
4. вң… E2E smoke tests pass (`pnpm test:e2e`)
5. вң… Feature works as specified in `PROJECT.md` or `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md`
6. вң… `features.json` updated to `done`
7. вң… `claude-progress.txt` updated with session notes

input.content:
#!/bin/bash

# PHAROS Framework - Harness Protocol
# Run this at the start of every session.
# Based on: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

set -e

echo "=================================================="
echo " PHAROS FRAMEWORK - HARNESS PROTOCOL"
echo "=================================================="
echo ""

# 1. Environment Info
echo "[1/5] Environment"
echo "  Date:   $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
echo "  OS:     $(uname -s) $(uname -r)"
echo "  Node:   $(node -v 2>/dev/null || echo 'not installed')"
echo "  Branch: $(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo 'not a git repo')"
echo ""

# 2. PHAROS Structure Check
echo "[2/5] PHAROS Structure Verification"
MISSING=0

check_file() {
  if [ -f "$1" ]; then
    echo "  [OK] $1"
  else
    echo "  [!!] MISSING: $1"
    MISSING=$((MISSING + 1))
  fi
}

check_dir() {
  if [ -d "$1" ]; then
    echo "  [OK] $1/"
  else
    echo "  [!!] MISSING: $1/"
    MISSING=$((MISSING + 1))
  fi
}

check_file "pharos/PHAROS.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/context.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/session-log.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/decisions.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/known-issues.md"
check_file "pharos/templates/ticket.json"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/pending"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/in-review"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/approved"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/rejected"
check_dir "pharos/agents"

if [ $MISSING -gt 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "  WARNING: $MISSING item(s) missing from PHAROS structure"
fi
echo ""

# 3. Ticket Status
echo "[3/5] Ticket Status"

count_tickets() {
  local dir=$1
  local count=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.json" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  echo $count
}

PENDING=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/pending")
INREVIEW=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/in-review")
APPROVED=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/approved")
REJECTED=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/rejected")

echo "  Pending:    $PENDING"
echo "  In Review:  $INREVIEW"
echo "  Approved:   $APPROVED"
echo "  Rejected:   $REJECTED"

if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "  ATTENTION: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) need attention!"
  ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
    echo "    - $(basename $f)"
  done
fi
echo ""

# 4. Progress Log
echo "[4/5] Recent Progress"
if [ -f "pharos-progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "--- LAST SESSION LOG (tail) ---"
  tail -n 25 pharos-progress.txt
  echo "--------------------------------"
else
  echo "  No progress log found (pharos-progress.txt)"
  echo "  This may be the first session."
fi
echo ""

# 5. Next Steps
echo "[5/5] Next Steps"
if [ -f "features.json" ]; then
  echo "  Pending tasks from features.json:"
  grep -B1 '"status": "pending"' features.json 2>/dev/null | grep '"name"' | head -5 | while read line; do
    name=$(echo "$line" | sed 's/.*"name": "\([^"]*\)".*/\1/')
    echo "    - $name"
  done
fi
echo ""

# Summary
echo "=================================================="
if [ $MISSING -eq 0 ] && [ "$REJECTED" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo " HARNESS READY - All checks passed"
  echo ""
  echo " Quick Start:"
  echo "   1. Read pharos/insights/context.md"
  echo "   2. Check features.json for next task"
  echo "   3. Implement and document"
else
  echo " HARNESS ATTENTION NEEDED"
  if [ $MISSING -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "   - Fix missing PHAROS structure items"
  fi
  if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "   - Address rejected tickets"
  fi
fi
echo "=================================================="

#!/bin/bash

# PHAROS Framework - Harness Protocol
# Run this at the start of every session.
# Based on: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

set -e

echo "=================================================="
echo " PHAROS FRAMEWORK - HARNESS PROTOCOL"
echo "=================================================="
echo ""

# 1. Environment Info
echo "[1/5] Environment"
echo "  Date:   $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
echo "  OS:     $(uname -s) $(uname -r)"
echo "  Node:   $(node -v 2>/dev/null || echo 'not installed')"
echo "  Branch: $(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo 'not a git repo')"
echo ""

# 2. PHAROS Structure Check
echo "[2/5] PHAROS Structure Verification"
MISSING=0

check_file() {
  if [ -f "$1" ]; then
    echo "  [OK] $1"
  else
    echo "  [!!] MISSING: $1"
    MISSING=$((MISSING + 1))
  fi
}

check_dir() {
  if [ -d "$1" ]; then
    echo "  [OK] $1/"
  else
    echo "  [!!] MISSING: $1/"
    MISSING=$((MISSING + 1))
  fi
}

check_file "pharos/PHAROS.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/context.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/session-log.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/decisions.md"
check_file "pharos/insights/known-issues.md"
check_file "pharos/templates/ticket.json"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/pending"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/in-review"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/approved"
check_dir "pharos/tickets/rejected"
check_dir "pharos/agents"

if [ $MISSING -gt 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "  WARNING: $MISSING item(s) missing from PHAROS structure"
fi
echo ""

# 3. Ticket Status
echo "[3/5] Ticket Status"

count_tickets() {
  local dir=$1
  local count=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.json" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  echo $count
}

PENDING=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/pending")
INREVIEW=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/in-review")
APPROVED=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/approved")
REJECTED=$(count_tickets "pharos/tickets/rejected")

echo "  Pending:    $PENDING"
echo "  In Review:  $INREVIEW"
echo "  Approved:   $APPROVED"
echo "  Rejected:   $REJECTED"

if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "  ATTENTION: $REJECTED rejected ticket(s) need attention!"
  ls -1 pharos/tickets/rejected/*.json 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
    echo "    - $(basename $f)"
  done
fi
echo ""

# 4. Progress Log
echo "[4/5] Recent Progress"
if [ -f "pharos-progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "--- LAST SESSION LOG (tail) ---"
  tail -n 25 pharos-progress.txt
  echo "--------------------------------"
else
  echo "  No progress log found (pharos-progress.txt)"
  echo "  This may be the first session."
fi
echo ""

# 5. Next Steps
echo "[5/5] Next Steps"
if [ -f "features.json" ]; then
  echo "  Pending tasks from features.json:"
  grep -B1 '"status": "pending"' features.json 2>/dev/null | grep '"name"' | head -5 | while read line; do
    name=$(echo "$line" | sed 's/.*"name": "\([^"]*\)".*/\1/')
    echo "    - $name"
  done
fi
echo ""

# Summary
echo "=================================================="
if [ $MISSING -eq 0 ] && [ "$REJECTED" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo " HARNESS READY - All checks passed"
  echo ""
  echo " Quick Start:"
  echo "   1. Read pharos/insights/context.md"
  echo "   2. Check features.json for next task"
  echo "   3. Implement and document"
else
  echo " HARNESS ATTENTION NEEDED"
  if [ $MISSING -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "   - Fix missing PHAROS structure items"
  fi
  if [ "$REJECTED" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "   - Address rejected tickets"
  fi
fi
echo "=================================================="

#!/bin/bash

# MMS Harness - Environment Verifier & Test Gate
# Run this at the start of every session.
# Based on: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents

set -e

echo "=================================================="
echo " MMS FRACTAL PLANNER - HARNESS PROTOCOL"
echo "=================================================="

# 1. Check Core Tools
echo "[1/6] Checking Environment..."
node -v >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "вқҢ Node.js missing"; exit 1; }
pnpm -v >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "вқҢ pnpm missing"; exit 1; }
echo "вң… Node $(node -v) | pnpm $(pnpm -v)"

# 2. Check Architecture Compliance
echo ""
echo "[2/6] Verifying Architecture..."

WARNINGS=0

if [ -f "astro.config.mjs" ]; then
  echo "вҡ пёҸ  WARNING: Astro config detected. Project should be pure Vite (SPA)."
  WARNINGS=$((WARNINGS + 1))
fi

if grep -q '"@reduxjs/toolkit"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "вҡ пёҸ  WARNING: Redux detected. Architecture requires Zustand."
  WARNINGS=$((WARNINGS + 1))
fi

if [ $WARNINGS -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "вң… Architecture compliance OK"
fi

# 3. MANDATORY TEST GATE
echo ""
echo "[3/6] Running Mandatory Test Gate..."
echo "     (ALL tests must pass before any work can be committed)"

TEST_FAILED=0

echo "  вҶ’ TypeScript Check..."
if pnpm typecheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… TypeScript OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ TypeScript FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

echo "  вҶ’ Unit Tests (Vitest)..."
if pnpm test --run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… Unit Tests OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ Unit Tests FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

echo "  вҶ’ ESLint..."
if pnpm lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… ESLint OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ ESLint FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

echo "  вҶ’ E2E Smoke Tests (Playwright)..."
if pnpm test:e2e >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "  вң… E2E Tests OK"
else
  echo "  вқҢ E2E Tests FAILED"
  TEST_FAILED=1
fi

if [ $TEST_FAILED -eq 1 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "рҹҡЁ TEST GATE FAILED!"
  echo "   You MUST fix all tests before proceeding with any new features."
  echo "   This is a BLOCKING requirement per the harness protocol."
  echo ""
fi

# 4. Load Context
echo ""
echo "[4/6] Loading Project State..."
if [ -f "claude-progress.txt" ]; then
  echo "--- LAST SESSION LOG (tail) ---"
  tail -n 30 claude-progress.txt
  echo "-------------------------------"
else
  echo "вқҢ No progress log found!"
fi

# 5. Show Git Status
echo ""
echo "[5/6] Git Status..."
git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || echo "Not a git repo"
git status -s 2>/dev/null || true

# 6. Show Next Steps
echo ""
echo "[6/6] Recommended Next Action:"
if [ -f "features.json" ]; then
  NEXT_TASK=$(grep -A1 '"status": "pending"' features.json | head -2 | grep '"name"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"name": "\([^"]*\)".*/\1/')
  if [ -n "$NEXT_TASK" ]; then
    echo "   Next pending task: $NEXT_TASK"
  fi
fi
echo "   Refer to 'features.json' for the full task list."

echo ""
echo "=================================================="
if [ $TEST_FAILED -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "вң… HARNESS READY - All checks passed"
  echo "   Run 'pnpm dev' to start development."
else
  echo "вқҢ HARNESS BLOCKED - Fix tests first"
  echo "   Run 'pnpm typecheck && pnpm test --run && pnpm lint' to diagnose"
fi
echo "=================================================="

exit $TEST_FAILED

input.content:
# PHAROS Project Context

> **READ THIS FIRST** вҖ” This document contains the current state of the project and must be read before any action.

---

## Last Updated
2025-12-13T23:00:00Z (by: ORCHESTRATOR)

---

## 1. Project Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Project Name** | PHAROS Framework |
| **Full Name** | **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S** |
| **Type** | Multi-Agent Development & Quality Framework |
| **Parent Project** | MMS Fractal Planner (railway maintenance scheduling) |
| **Branch** | `framework/quality-system` |
| **Goal** | Complete framework that guarantees Palantir + Google + Pentagram quality |

---

## 2. Current State

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Phase** | Foundation Complete, Agent Definitions Next |
| **Blockers** | None |
| **Priority** | Create agent instruction files (13 agents) |
| **Next Task** | `agent-001`: Create ORCHESTRATOR agent instructions |

---

## 3. What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is a complete multi-agent development framework with:

- **13 Specialized Agents** вҖ” Each has a domain of expertise
- **Ticket-Based Communication** вҖ” Persistent, async, traceable
- **Quality Gates** вҖ” ALL must pass before "done"
- **Insight System** вҖ” Prevents context loss between sessions
- **Visual Verification** вҖ” VISION agent sees the actual output

### Agent Categories

| Category | Agents | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **Leadership** | PROJECT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD, PRODUCT_OWNER | Coordination & decisions |
| **Development** | RESEARCHER, ARCHITECT, ENGINEER, DESIGNER, COPYWRITER, MARKETING | Implementation |
| **Quality** | PERFORMANCE, VISION, QA | Verification |
| **Orchestration** | ORCHESTRATOR | Process facilitation |

---

## 4. Why PHAROS Exists

The parent project (MMS Fractal Planner) had a critical failure:

> **"Tests pass, screen is blue"**
>
> AI marked features as "done", all tests passed, but the application
> showed a solid blue screen with no data. The AI couldn't "see" the
> visual output and had no systematic quality gates.

PHAROS solves this with:
1. **VISION agent** вҖ” Uses Playwright MCP to capture screenshots
2. **Claude Vision** вҖ” Analyzes screenshots semantically ("are there colored bars?")
3. **Quality gates** вҖ” 9 gates must ALL approve before "done"
4. **Persistent context** вҖ” No repeated mistakes across sessions

---

## 5. What's Done

### Foundation Phase (Complete)
- [x] PHAROS.md framework specification (~1000 lines)
- [x] Technology research (LangGraph, Qdrant, GraphRAG, Playwright MCP)
- [x] Ticket system schema and templates
- [x] Insights directory structure
- [x] ADRs and known issues documented
- [x] Harness files for PHAROS development

### Files Created
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pharos/PHAROS.md` | Complete framework specification |
| `pharos/insights/context.md` | This file - current state |
| `pharos/insights/session-log.md` | Session history |
| `pharos/insights/decisions.md` | Architecture Decision Records |
| `pharos/insights/known-issues.md` | Documented problems |
| `pharos/templates/ticket.json` | Ticket schema |
| `CLAUDE.md` | AI development instructions |
| `features.json` | Task tracking |
| `init.sh` | Harness protocol script |
| `pharos-progress.txt` | Session progress log |

---

## 6. What's Next

### Priority 1: Agent Instruction Files
Create `pharos/agents/[agent-name].md` for each of the 13 agents.

Each file should include:
- Agent identity and role
- Specific responsibilities
- Checklist of things to verify
- Tools the agent uses
- Output format
- Example invocation prompt

**Suggested order (by importance to core problem):**
1. `vision.md` вҖ” Solves "blind AI" problem
2. `engineer.md` вҖ” Core implementation agent
3. `architect.md` вҖ” Structural integrity
4. `orchestrator.md` вҖ” Process coordination
5. `qa.md` вҖ” Final validation
6. (rest can follow)

### Priority 2: Workflow Integration
- Create sample ticket for lifecycle test
- Test flow: pending вҶ’ in-review вҶ’ approved
- Create CLI helpers for ticket management

### Priority 3: Documentation
- Quick-start guide
- Agent invocation examples
- Troubleshooting guide

---

## 7. Technology Stack (Decided)

| Category | Technology | Rationale |
|----------|------------|-----------|
| **Orchestration** | LangGraph | Graph-based workflows, fault-tolerant |
| **Knowledge** | GraphRAG + Neo4j | Relationship understanding |
| **Vectors** | Qdrant | Fast search with complex filters |
| **Document Processing** | LlamaIndex | Best-in-class RAG |
| **Visual Testing** | Playwright MCP | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Visual Analysis** | Claude Vision | Semantic screenshot understanding |
| **Pixel Comparison** | pixelmatch | Fast regression detection |

---

## 8. Directory Structure

```
pharos/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ PHAROS.md           # Framework specification (SSOT)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ agents/             # Agent instruction files (TO CREATE)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ orchestrator.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ researcher.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ architect.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ engineer.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ designer.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ copywriter.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ marketing.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ performance.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ vision.md       # HIGH PRIORITY
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ qa.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ project-manager.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ tech-lead.md
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ product-owner.md
в”ңв”Җв”Җ insights/           # Persistent knowledge
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ context.md      # THIS FILE - read first
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ session-log.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ decisions.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ known-issues.md
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ research/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ticket.json
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tickets/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pending/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ in-review/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ approved/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ rejected/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ archive/
в””в”Җв”Җ gates/              # Quality gate scripts
```

---

## 9. Key ADRs

| ID | Decision | Rationale |
|----|----------|-----------|
| ADR-001 | LangGraph for orchestration | Complex state, graph workflows |
| ADR-002 | Qdrant for vectors | Performance + complex filters |
| ADR-003 | GraphRAG for knowledge | Relationship understanding |
| ADR-004 | Multi-layer visual testing | Playwright MCP + Vision + pixelmatch |

Full details in `pharos/insights/decisions.md`.

---

## 10. Known Issues

| ID | Issue | Status |
|----|-------|--------|
| ISSUE-001 | "Tests Pass, Screen Blue" syndrome | Root cause identified |
| ISSUE-002 | Context loss between sessions | Mitigated by PHAROS |
| ISSUE-003 | AI cannot see visual output | Mitigated by VISION agent |

Full details in `pharos/insights/known-issues.md`.

---

## 11. Session Continuity Checklist

When starting a new session:

1. [ ] Read this file (`pharos/insights/context.md`)
2. [ ] Run `./init.sh` to verify structure
3. [ ] Check `pharos-progress.txt` for last session notes
4. [ ] Review `features.json` for next pending task
5. [ ] Check for any rejected tickets in `pharos/tickets/rejected/`

When ending a session:

1. [ ] Update `pharos-progress.txt` with session summary
2. [ ] Update `features.json` task status
3. [ ] Add entry to `pharos/insights/session-log.md`
4. [ ] Commit with clear message

---

## 12. Parent Project Context

PHAROS will be applied to **MMS Fractal Planner**:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Type | RFQ response for railway operator |
| Codename | "Google Maps of Time" |
| Scale | 128 trains, 12 years, 50,000+ schedule blocks |
| Standards | Palantir (data) + Google (engineering) + Pentagram (design) |
| Goal | Recover client confidence after suboptimal initial presentation |

Key parent project files (on `main` branch):
- `PROJECT.md` вҖ” Architecture specification
- `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` вҖ” UX specification and demo script
- `docs/RFQ.md` вҖ” Original client requirements

---

*When in doubt, re-read this document. Context is everything.*

# PHAROS Project Context

> **READ THIS FIRST** вҖ” This document contains the current state of the project and must be read before any action.

---

## Last Updated
2025-12-13T23:00:00Z (by: ORCHESTRATOR)

---

## 1. Project Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Project Name** | PHAROS Framework |
| **Full Name** | **P**alantir В· **H**arness В· **A**nthropic В· palanti**R** В· g**O**ogle В· harnes**S** |
| **Type** | Multi-Agent Development & Quality Framework |
| **Parent Project** | MMS Fractal Planner (railway maintenance scheduling) |
| **Branch** | `framework/quality-system` |
| **Goal** | Complete framework that guarantees Palantir + Google + Pentagram quality |

---

## 2. Current State

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Phase** | Foundation Complete, Agent Definitions Next |
| **Blockers** | None |
| **Priority** | Create agent instruction files (13 agents) |
| **Next Task** | `agent-001`: Create ORCHESTRATOR agent instructions |

---

## 3. What PHAROS Is

PHAROS is a complete multi-agent development framework with:

- **13 Specialized Agents** вҖ” Each has a domain of expertise
- **Ticket-Based Communication** вҖ” Persistent, async, traceable
- **Quality Gates** вҖ” ALL must pass before "done"
- **Insight System** вҖ” Prevents context loss between sessions
- **Visual Verification** вҖ” VISION agent sees the actual output

### Agent Categories

| Category | Agents | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **Leadership** | PROJECT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD, PRODUCT_OWNER | Coordination & decisions |
| **Development** | RESEARCHER, ARCHITECT, ENGINEER, DESIGNER, COPYWRITER, MARKETING | Implementation |
| **Quality** | PERFORMANCE, VISION, QA | Verification |
| **Orchestration** | ORCHESTRATOR | Process facilitation |

---

## 4. Why PHAROS Exists

The parent project (MMS Fractal Planner) had a critical failure:

> **"Tests pass, screen is blue"**
>
> AI marked features as "done", all tests passed, but the application
> showed a solid blue screen with no data. The AI couldn't "see" the
> visual output and had no systematic quality gates.

PHAROS solves this with:
1. **VISION agent** вҖ” Uses Playwright MCP to capture screenshots
2. **Claude Vision** вҖ” Analyzes screenshots semantically ("are there colored bars?")
3. **Quality gates** вҖ” 9 gates must ALL approve before "done"
4. **Persistent context** вҖ” No repeated mistakes across sessions

---

## 5. What's Done

### Foundation Phase (Complete)
- [x] PHAROS.md framework specification (~1000 lines)
- [x] Technology research (LangGraph, Qdrant, GraphRAG, Playwright MCP)
- [x] Ticket system schema and templates
- [x] Insights directory structure
- [x] ADRs and known issues documented
- [x] Harness files for PHAROS development

### Files Created
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pharos/PHAROS.md` | Complete framework specification |
| `pharos/insights/context.md` | This file - current state |
| `pharos/insights/session-log.md` | Session history |
| `pharos/insights/decisions.md` | Architecture Decision Records |
| `pharos/insights/known-issues.md` | Documented problems |
| `pharos/templates/ticket.json` | Ticket schema |
| `CLAUDE.md` | AI development instructions |
| `features.json` | Task tracking |
| `init.sh` | Harness protocol script |
| `pharos-progress.txt` | Session progress log |

---

## 6. What's Next

### Priority 1: Agent Instruction Files
Create `pharos/agents/[agent-name].md` for each of the 13 agents.

Each file should include:
- Agent identity and role
- Specific responsibilities
- Checklist of things to verify
- Tools the agent uses
- Output format
- Example invocation prompt

**Suggested order (by importance to core problem):**
1. `vision.md` вҖ” Solves "blind AI" problem
2. `engineer.md` вҖ” Core implementation agent
3. `architect.md` вҖ” Structural integrity
4. `orchestrator.md` вҖ” Process coordination
5. `qa.md` вҖ” Final validation
6. (rest can follow)

### Priority 2: Workflow Integration
- Create sample ticket for lifecycle test
- Test flow: pending вҶ’ in-review вҶ’ approved
- Create CLI helpers for ticket management

### Priority 3: Documentation
- Quick-start guide
- Agent invocation examples
- Troubleshooting guide

---

## 7. Technology Stack (Decided)

| Category | Technology | Rationale |
|----------|------------|-----------|
| **Orchestration** | LangGraph | Graph-based workflows, fault-tolerant |
| **Knowledge** | GraphRAG + Neo4j | Relationship understanding |
| **Vectors** | Qdrant | Fast search with complex filters |
| **Document Processing** | LlamaIndex | Best-in-class RAG |
| **Visual Testing** | Playwright MCP | Official Anthropic integration |
| **Visual Analysis** | Claude Vision | Semantic screenshot understanding |
| **Pixel Comparison** | pixelmatch | Fast regression detection |

---

## 8. Directory Structure

```
pharos/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ PHAROS.md           # Framework specification (SSOT)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ agents/             # Agent instruction files (TO CREATE)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ orchestrator.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ researcher.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ architect.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ engineer.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ designer.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ copywriter.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ marketing.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ performance.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ vision.md       # HIGH PRIORITY
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ qa.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ project-manager.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ tech-lead.md
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ product-owner.md
в”ңв”Җв”Җ insights/           # Persistent knowledge
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ context.md      # THIS FILE - read first
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ session-log.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ decisions.md
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ known-issues.md
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ research/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ticket.json
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tickets/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pending/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ in-review/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ approved/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ rejected/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ archive/
в””в”Җв”Җ gates/              # Quality gate scripts
```

---

## 9. Key ADRs

| ID | Decision | Rationale |
|----|----------|-----------|
| ADR-001 | LangGraph for orchestration | Complex state, graph workflows |
| ADR-002 | Qdrant for vectors | Performance + complex filters |
| ADR-003 | GraphRAG for knowledge | Relationship understanding |
| ADR-004 | Multi-layer visual testing | Playwright MCP + Vision + pixelmatch |

Full details in `pharos/insights/decisions.md`.

---

## 10. Known Issues

| ID | Issue | Status |
|----|-------|--------|
| ISSUE-001 | "Tests Pass, Screen Blue" syndrome | Root cause identified |
| ISSUE-002 | Context loss between sessions | Mitigated by PHAROS |
| ISSUE-003 | AI cannot see visual output | Mitigated by VISION agent |

Full details in `pharos/insights/known-issues.md`.

---

## 11. Session Continuity Checklist

When starting a new session:

1. [ ] Read this file (`pharos/insights/context.md`)
2. [ ] Run `./init.sh` to verify structure
3. [ ] Check `pharos-progress.txt` for last session notes
4. [ ] Review `features.json` for next pending task
5. [ ] Check for any rejected tickets in `pharos/tickets/rejected/`

When ending a session:

1. [ ] Update `pharos-progress.txt` with session summary
2. [ ] Update `features.json` task status
3. [ ] Add entry to `pharos/insights/session-log.md`
4. [ ] Commit with clear message

---

## 12. Parent Project Context

PHAROS will be applied to **MMS Fractal Planner**:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Type | RFQ response for railway operator |
| Codename | "Google Maps of Time" |
| Scale | 128 trains, 12 years, 50,000+ schedule blocks |
| Standards | Palantir (data) + Google (engineering) + Pentagram (design) |
| Goal | Recover client confidence after suboptimal initial presentation |

Key parent project files (on `main` branch):
- `PROJECT.md` вҖ” Architecture specification
- `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` вҖ” UX specification and demo script
- `docs/RFQ.md` вҖ” Original client requirements

---

*When in doubt, re-read this document. Context is everything.*

# PHAROS Project Context

> **READ THIS FIRST** вҖ” This document contains the current state of the project and must be read by every agent before taking any action.

---

## Last Updated
2025-12-13T22:30:00Z (by: Human + ORCHESTRATOR)

---

## 1. Project Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Project Name** | MMS Fractal Planner |
| **Codename** | "Google Maps of Time" |
| **Type** | RFQ Response / Demo |
| **Client** | Railway operator (128 trains, 2 fleets) |
| **Goal** | Recover client confidence with impressive demo |
| **Standards** | Palantir (data) + Google (engineering) + Pentagram (design) |

---

## 2. Current State

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Active Branch** | `framework/quality-system` (PHAROS development) |
| **Main Branch** | `main` (demo development) |
| **Phase** | PHAROS Framework Setup |
| **Blockers** | None |
| **Priority** | Complete PHAROS framework, then apply to demo |

---

## 3. Environment

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Date** | 2025-12-13 |
| **OS** | macOS Darwin 25.1.0 |
| **Node** | v20.x (verify with `node -v`) |
| **Package Manager** | pnpm 8.x |
| **IDE** | Cursor |

---

## 4. Critical Business Context

### The Situation
- Initial client presentation was suboptimal (presenter had COVID)
- Client confidence needs to be recovered
- This demo must be **impressive** вҖ” not just functional

### The Product
- **128 trains** across 2 fleets (Kinki Sharyo + Alstom)
- **12 years** of maintenance scheduling
- **50,000+ schedule blocks** to render
- Must feel like "Google Maps" but for time/scheduling

### The RFQ Requirements (Summary)
1. Short-term planning (weekly, monthly)
2. Long-term planning (yearly, 1-10 years)
3. "What-If" simulations (Scenario Mode)
4. Mileage underrun/overrun warnings
5. Resource utilization visibility
6. Single interface for maintenance + operations

---

## 5. Technical Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

| Constraint | Allowed | Forbidden |
|------------|---------|-----------|
| Scrolling | Virtual Scroller (math-based) | Native DOM / Ghost Divs |
| Data | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (large data) |
| State | Zustand (transient + global) | Redux, Context (high-freq) |
| Rendering | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM (>100 items), SVG |
| Animation | requestAnimationFrame | setInterval, setTimeout |

---

## 6. Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Frame time | <16ms (60fps) |
| Initial load | <3s |
| Drag operations | 60fps constant |
| Memory | No GC pauses |
| Data capacity | 50,000+ blocks |

---

## 7. Active Tickets

| ID | Title | Status | Assigned To |
|----|-------|--------|-------------|
| вҖ” | No active tickets yet | вҖ” | вҖ” |

---

## 8. Recent Decisions

| Date | Decision | Rationale |
|------|----------|-----------|
| 2025-12-13 | Framework name: PHAROS | Palantir + Harness + Anthropic + R + O + S |
| 2025-12-13 | Use LangGraph for orchestration | Complex state, graph workflows |
| 2025-12-13 | Use Qdrant for vectors | Performance + complex filters |
| 2025-12-13 | Use GraphRAG for knowledge | Relationship understanding |

---

## 9. Key Documentation

| Document | Purpose | Location |
|----------|---------|----------|
| PHAROS Framework | Agent system specification | `pharos/PHAROS.md` |
| Project Architecture | Technical SSOT | `PROJECT.md` |
| UX Specification | Demo narrative, visuals | `docs/UX_FOR_DEMO.md` |
| Client Requirements | Original RFQ | `docs/RFQ.md` |
| Agent Instructions | Per-agent guidelines | `pharos/agents/*.md` |

---

## 10. Session Continuity Notes

### What Was Done Last Session
- Created PHAROS framework branch
- Researched multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen)
- Researched knowledge systems (GraphRAG, LlamaIndex, Qdrant)
- Researched visual testing (Playwright MCP, Vision LLM)
- Created PHAROS.md with complete agent specifications

### What Needs To Be Done Next
1. Add coordination agents (Project Manager, Tech Lead, Product Owner)
2. Create agent instruction files
3. Create ticket templates
4. Create initial integration with init.sh
5. Test framework with a sample ticket

---

## 11. Agent Quick Reference

### Development Agents
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| RESEARCHER | Investigates technologies, reads docs |
| ARCHITECT | System design, dependencies, patterns |
| ENGINEER | Implementation, tests, code quality |
| DESIGNER | UX/UI, visual design, accessibility |
| COPYWRITER | Text, wording, terminology |
| MARKETING | Positioning, messaging, demo narrative |
| PERFORMANCE | Speed, memory, optimization |
| VISION | Visual verification, screenshots |
| QA | Final validation, acceptance |

### Coordination Agents
| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| PROJECT_MANAGER | Timeline, priorities, dependencies |
| TECH_LEAD | Technical decisions, code review |
| PRODUCT_OWNER | Requirements, client alignment |
| ORCHESTRATOR | Process facilitation, context |

---

*When in doubt, re-read this document. Context is everything.*

content.thinking:
The user is asking a very important question about the implementation of the agents:
1. Will the agents be autonomous?
2. Will we use a known framework to implement them (like something to manage queues, etc.)?

This is a critical architectural question. Let me think about the options:

**Option 1: Autonomous agents with a framework like LangGraph/CrewAI**
- Requires setting up a Python backend
- Agents run independently, communicate via the framework
- More complex, but truly autonomous
- Better for production

**Option 2: "Pseudo-agents" - Claude Code with different prompts**
- Each agent is just a different system prompt for Claude
- The human (or a main Claude instance) orchestrates by invoking different prompts
- Simpler, no additional infrastructure
- Good for prototyping and immediate use

**Option 3: Hybrid - Start simple, evolve**
- Start with prompt-based agents (Option 2)
- Structure them so they can be migrated to LangGraph later
- Pragmatic approach

Looking at the ADRs in decisions.md, we already decided:
- LangGraph for orchestration (complex state, fault-tolerant)
- Qdrant for vectors
- GraphRAG for knowledge

But implementing LangGraph would require:
1. Python environment
2. LangGraph setup
3. Agent definitions in Python
4. State management
5. Potentially a server running

This is a significant undertaking. The question is: should we:
A) Build full autonomous agents now (complex, takes time)
B) Build "invocable" agents (prompts that Claude follows) that can later be converted to autonomous
C) Something in between

I should ask the user what their preference is, presenting the trade-offs clearly.

content.text:
Excelente pergunta. Isso Г© uma decisГЈo arquitetural importante que precisa ser tomada antes de criar os agentes.

**Stack:** Apenas prompts estruturados no Claude Code

```

**PrГіs:**
- Zero infraestrutura adicional
- Funciona agora, imediatamente
- FГЎcil de iterar e ajustar

**Contras:**
- NГЈo Г© verdadeiramente autГҙnomo (humano orquestra)
- ExecuГ§ГЈo sequencial
- Depende da sessГЈo Claude nГЈo expirar

---

### OpГ§ГЈo C: HГӯbrido Progressivo (Recomendado)

**Fase 1 (Agora):** Agentes InvocГЎveis
- Criar arquivos `.md` em `pharos/agents/`
- Cada arquivo Г© um "prompt completo" para Claude assumir o papel

## Plano Completo de ImplementaГ§ГЈo PHAROS com LangGraph

### Fase 1: Infraestrutura Python
| # | Tarefa | DescriГ§ГЈo |
|---|--------|-----------|
| 1.1 | Criar `pyproject.toml` | Dependencies: langgraph, langchain, anthropic, pymongo, playwright, langfuse |
| 1.2 | Criar estrutura de diretГіrios | `src/agents`, `src/graph`, `src/tools`, `src/db`, `src/cli`, `config`, `tests` |
| 1.3 | Criar `config/settings.py` | ConfiguraГ§Гөes: MongoDB URI, Anthropic API key, LangFuse keys |
| 1.4 | Criar `.env.example` | Template de variГЎveis de ambiente |

### Fase 2: Camada MongoDB
| # | Tarefa | DescriГ§ГЈo |
|---|--------|-----------|
| 2.1 | Criar `src/db/mongo.py` | Cliente MongoDB singleton, connection pooling |
| 2.2 | Criar `src/db/schemas.py` | Schemas: Ticket, AgentHistory, Decision, Knowledge |
| 2.3 | Criar `src/db/repositories.py` | CRUD operations para cada collection |
| 2.4 | Criar script de inicializaГ§ГЈo | ГҚndices, validaГ§ГЈo de schema |

### Fase 3: LangGraph Core
| # | Tarefa | DescriГ§ГЈo |
|---|--------|-----------|
| 3.1 | Criar `src/graph/state.py` | TypedDict com estado do ticket (gates, evidence, etc.) |
| 3.2 | Criar `src/graph/nodes.py` | FunГ§Гөes node para cada agente |
| 3.3 | Criar `src/graph/edges.py` | Conditional edges (routing logic) |
| 3.4 | Criar `src/graph/workflow.py` | StateGraph principal, compilaГ§ГЈo |
| 3.5 | Criar `src/graph/checkpointer.py` | MongoDB checkpointer para persistГӘncia |

### Fase 4: Tools dos Agentes
| # | Tarefa | DescriГ§ГЈo |
|---|--------|-----------|
| 4.1 | Criar `src/tools/code_tools.py` | read_file, write_file, run_command, lint, typecheck |
| 4.2 | Criar `src/tools/visual_tools.py` | screenshot, analyze_screenshot, compare_baseline |
| 4.3 | Criar `src/tools/db_tools.py` | create_ticket, update_gate, query_history |
| 4.4 | Criar `src/tools/research_tools.py` | web_search, fetch_docs, query_knowledge |

### Fase 5: ImplementaГ§ГЈo dos 13 Agentes
| # | Agente | Arquivo | Responsabilidade |
|---|--------|---------|------------------|
| 5.1 | ORCHESTRATOR | `src/agents/orchestrator.py` | Routing, context, prioridades |
| 5.2 | RESEARCHER | `src/agents/researcher.py` | InvestigaГ§ГЈo, documentaГ§ГЈo |
| 5.3 | ARCHITECT | `src/agents/architect.py` | Design, dependГӘncias, patterns |

### Fase 6: CLI e Interface

Posso comeГ§ar a implementaГ§ГЈo?

message.content:
Pense em como simplificar, proponho usar apenas os seguintes para ser mais rapido sem perder qualidade: Novo Agente (NГі),Agentes PHAROS Consolidados,"Responsabilidade ""VisГЈo 360"""
1. PRODUCT & DESIGN,PRODUCT_OWNER + DESIGNER + COPYWRITER + MARKETING,"Define O QUE serГЎ feito. Gera o spec, define os textos (wording) e as regras visuais antes de uma linha de cГіdigo ser escrita."
2. SENIOR ENGINEER,ARCHITECT + TECH_LEAD + ENGINEER,Define COMO serГЎ feito e executa. Toma as decisГөes de stack e implementa o cГіdigo.
3. THE AUDITOR (Vision),VISION + QA + PERFORMANCE,"O Juiz. Roda o app, tira o screenshot, verifica performance e compara com o spec do Agente 1. Este Г© o gatekeeper."
4. ORCHESTRATOR,ORCHESTRATOR + PROJECT_MANAGER,MantГ©m o estado (State Graph) e decide se o ticket volta para o Engineer ou vai para Merge.

todos.content: Create Python project structure with pyproject.toml
Create LangGraph state schema and workflow
Implement all 13 agents as LangGraph nodes

content.thinking:
Excellent simplification! The user is proposing to consolidate the 13 agents into 4 super-agents:

1. **PRODUCT & DESIGN** = PRODUCT_OWNER + DESIGNER + COPYWRITER + MARKETING
   - Defines WHAT will be done
   - Generates specs, defines wording, visual rules
   - Runs BEFORE any code is written

2. **SENIOR ENGINEER** = ARCHITECT + TECH_LEAD + ENGINEER
   - Defines HOW it will be done and executes
   - Makes stack decisions and implements code

3. **THE AUDITOR (Vision)** = VISION + QA + PERFORMANCE
   - The Judge
   - Runs the app, takes screenshots, checks performance
   - Compares against the spec from Agent 1
   - This is the GATEKEEPER

4. **ORCHESTRATOR** = ORCHESTRATOR + PROJECT_MANAGER
   - Maintains state (State Graph)
   - Decides if ticket goes back to Engineer or goes to Merge

This is brilliant! It reduces complexity significantly while maintaining the quality gates:
- Agent 1 defines the spec (prevents building the wrong thing)
- Agent 2 implements (with architectural decisions baked in)
- Agent 3 audits (the "eyes" that solve the blind AI problem)
- Agent 4 orchestrates (maintains flow)

The workflow becomes:
```
ORCHESTRATOR вҶ’ PRODUCT_DESIGN вҶ’ SENIOR_ENGINEER вҶ’ AUDITOR вҶ’ [APPROVED?]
                                      вҶ‘                         вҶ“
                                      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ REJECTED в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Let me update the plan with this simplified structure.

input.content:
[project]
name = "pharos"
version = "1.0.0"
description = "PHAROS Multi-Agent Quality Framework - LangGraph Implementation"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = [
    # LangGraph & LangChain
    "langgraph>=0.2.0",
    "langchain>=0.3.0",
    "langchain-anthropic>=0.3.0",
    "langchain-community>=0.3.0",

    # Database
    "pymongo>=4.8.0",
    "motor>=3.5.0",  # Async MongoDB driver

    # Visual Testing
    "playwright>=1.48.0",

    # Observability
    "langfuse>=2.50.0",

    # CLI
    "typer>=0.12.0",
    "rich>=13.9.0",

    # Utilities
    "pydantic>=2.9.0",
    "pydantic-settings>=2.6.0",
    "python-dotenv>=1.0.0",
    "httpx>=0.27.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.3.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.24.0",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
    "ruff>=0.7.0",
    "mypy>=1.13.0",
]

[project.scripts]
pharos = "pharos.cli.main:app"

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/pharos"]

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py311"
line-length = 100

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "N", "W", "UP"]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.11"
strict = true

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
testpaths = ["tests"]

"""PHAROS Configuration Settings."""

from functools import lru_cache

from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    """Application settings loaded from environment variables."""

    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        case_sensitive=False,
    )

    # Anthropic
    anthropic_api_key: str
    anthropic_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"

    # MongoDB
    mongodb_uri: str = "mongodb://localhost:27017"
    mongodb_database: str = "pharos"

    # LangFuse (optional)
    langfuse_public_key: str | None = None
    langfuse_secret_key: str | None = None
    langfuse_host: str = "https://cloud.langfuse.com"

    # Project paths
    project_root: str = ".."  # Relative to pharos/

    # Agent settings
    max_retries: int = 3
    screenshot_dir: str = "pharos/evidence/screenshots"

    # Dev server for visual testing
    dev_server_url: str = "http://localhost:5173"
    dev_server_command: str = "pnpm dev"


@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    """Get cached settings instance."""
    return Settings()

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
